title: Le français en route vers le DELF

days.title: Passé composé ou imparfait : action ou décor ?
days.objective: Learn to choose between passé composé for completed events and imparfait for background description.
days.paretoFocus: The single question 'Is it a finished event or an ongoing setting?' decides most tense choices in A2 narration.
drills: Describe your morning today using three passé composé actions inside an imparfait setting.
Take five sentences and split each into a 'décor' (imparfait) and an 'événement' (passé composé) clause.
Retell what happened when the phone rang while you were cooking, using 'quand' + both tenses.

items: Recall: which tense marks a sudden, completed event in the past?
Say aloud three imparfait forms of 'être' and 'avoir' (j'étais, il avait, nous avions).
Translate: 'It was raining when I arrived.'

activities.title: Événement vs description
activities.intro: Passé composé pushes the story forward (what happened); imparfait paints the background (what was going on).

items.hr: Il pleuvait quand je suis sorti.
items.en: It was raining when I went out.
items.note: imparfait = décor, passé composé = événement

items.hr: Nous dînions quand le téléphone a sonné.
items.en: We were having dinner when the phone rang.

items.hr: Elle était fatiguée, alors elle est rentrée tôt.
items.en: She was tired, so she went home early.
items.note: state = imparfait, action = passé composé

items.hr: Le soleil brillait et les enfants jouaient.
items.en: The sun was shining and the children were playing.
items.note: two parallel descriptions

items.hr: Soudain, un chien a traversé la rue.
items.en: Suddenly, a dog crossed the street.
items.note: 'soudain' signals a passé composé event

activities.title: Choisir le bon temps
activities.intro: Decide whether each gap is a completed event or a background description.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Quand je ___ petit, j'habitais à Lyon.'
questions.explanation: A description of a past state (being little) uses the imparfait: étais.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Hier, il ___ un accident sur l'autoroute.'
questions.explanation: A single completed event uses the passé composé: il y a eu -> 'il ___ un accident' becomes 'y a eu'.

questions.prompt: Type the imparfait of 'faire' for 'il': 'Il ___ froid ce matin-là.'
questions.explanation: Weather description in the past = imparfait: il faisait froid.

questions.prompt: Type the passé composé of 'finir' for 'nous': 'Nous ___ le film à minuit.'
questions.explanation: A completed action with a clear end point uses the passé composé: nous avons fini.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I was reading when you arrived.'
questions.explanation: Background action (lisais, imparfait) interrupted by an event (es arrivé, passé composé).

activities.title: Qu'est-ce qui s'est passé ?
activities.intro: A friend asks about your evening. Play 'Me' and mix the two tenses.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Alors, ta soirée d'hier, c'était bien ?
lines.en: So, your evening yesterday, was it good?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui ! Il pleuvait, mais on a quand même fait une promenade.
lines.en: Yes! It was raining, but we still went for a walk.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ah bon ? Et qu'est-ce qui s'est passé ?
lines.en: Oh really? And what happened?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: On marchait tranquillement quand un ami nous a appelés.
lines.en: We were walking calmly when a friend called us.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et vous êtes allés au restaurant ?
lines.en: And did you go to the restaurant?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, on avait faim, donc on a mangé une pizza ensemble.
lines.en: Yes, we were hungry, so we ate a pizza together.

days.title: L'imparfait : habitudes et descriptions
days.objective: Master the imparfait to describe past habits, states, and ongoing situations.
days.paretoFocus: Learn the endings -ais/-ais/-ait/-ions/-iez/-aient once and you can conjugate almost any verb in the imparfait.
drills: List five things you always did as a child, each in the imparfait.
Describe your old bedroom using 'il y avait' and three imparfait descriptions.
Turn five present-tense habits into imparfait to say what you 'used to' do.

items: Recall the six imparfait endings out loud.
Give the imparfait stem rule: take the 'nous' present form and drop -ons.
Say the imparfait of 'faire', 'aller' and 'être' for 'je'.

activities.title: Former l'imparfait
activities.intro: Take the present 'nous' form, drop -ons, and add the imparfait endings. Only 'être' is irregular (ét-).

items.hr: Quand j'étais jeune, je jouais au foot.
items.en: When I was young, I used to play football.
items.note: habit in the past

items.hr: Nous allions à la mer chaque été.
items.en: We used to go to the sea every summer.
items.note: aller -> nous allons -> all-

items.hr: Il y avait un grand jardin derrière la maison.
items.en: There was a big garden behind the house.
items.note: 'il y avait' = there was/were

items.hr: Elle finissait toujours ses devoirs avant le dîner.
items.en: She always finished her homework before dinner.
items.note: finir -> nous finissons -> finiss-

items.hr: Vous étiez souvent en retard.
items.en: You were often late.
items.note: être is the only irregular stem: ét-

activities.title: Conjuguer à l'imparfait
activities.intro: Practise the imparfait forms and their meaning of past habit or description.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct imparfait: 'Chaque matin, nous ___ du café.'
questions.explanation: A repeated past habit uses the imparfait; boire -> nous buvons -> buvions.

questions.prompt: Which sentence expresses a past habit?
options: Je regardais la télé tous les soirs.
J'ai regardé la télé hier soir.
questions.answer: Je regardais la télé tous les soirs.
questions.explanation: The imparfait 'regardais' plus 'tous les soirs' expresses a repeated past habit.

questions.prompt: Type the imparfait of 'avoir' for 'je': 'Avant, j'___ un chat.'
questions.explanation: avoir -> nous avons -> av- + ais = j'avais.

questions.prompt: Type the imparfait of 'être' for 'ils': 'Ils ___ contents.'
questions.explanation: être uses the irregular stem ét-: ils étaient.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'We lived in a small town.'
questions.explanation: habiter -> nous habitons -> habit- + ions = habitions, an ongoing past situation.

activities.title: Quand tu étais petit
activities.intro: Your partner is curious about your childhood. Play 'Me' and answer in the imparfait.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Où est-ce que tu habitais quand tu étais petit ?
lines.en: Where did you live when you were little?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: J'habitais dans un petit village à la campagne.
lines.en: I lived in a small village in the countryside.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et qu'est-ce que tu faisais le week-end ?
lines.en: And what did you do on weekends?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je jouais dehors avec mes cousins toute la journée.
lines.en: I played outside with my cousins all day long.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Il y avait beaucoup d'enfants dans ton quartier ?
lines.en: Were there many children in your neighbourhood?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, nous étions une vraie petite bande d'amis.
lines.en: Yes, we were a real little gang of friends.

days.title: Le passé composé avec être
days.objective: Use être as the auxiliary for movement verbs and make the past participle agree with the subject.
days.paretoFocus: Memorising the small family of 'être' verbs (the DR & MRS VANDERTRAMP set) covers nearly all agreement cases.
drills: Recite the main être verbs (aller, venir, arriver, partir, entrer, sortir...) with their participles.
Write four sentences about a woman's trip, making every participle agree (arrivée, partie...).
Contrast 'j'ai monté la valise' vs 'je suis monté' to feel the transitive exception.

items: Recall: with 'être', the participle agrees in gender and number with the ___?
Say the passé composé of 'aller' for 'elle' and 'elles'.
Translate: 'They (f.) came home late.'

activities.title: Les verbes avec être
activities.intro: About fifteen verbs of movement/state use être. The participle then agrees with the subject like an adjective.

items.hr: Elle est arrivée à huit heures.
items.en: She arrived at eight o'clock.
items.note: arrivée = feminine agreement

items.hr: Ils sont partis en vacances.
items.en: They left on holiday.
items.note: partis = masculine plural

items.hr: Nous sommes allés au cinéma.
items.en: We went to the cinema.
items.note: allés agrees with 'nous'

items.hr: Marie est née en 2001.
items.en: Marie was born in 2001.
items.note: naître uses être

items.hr: Les filles sont rentrées ensemble.
items.en: The girls came home together.
items.note: rentrées = feminine plural, add -es

activities.title: Accord avec être
activities.intro: Choose the correct auxiliary and make the participle agree.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Elle ___ tôt ce matin.'
questions.explanation: partir uses être and the participle agrees with 'elle': est partie.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is correct?
options: Mes soeurs sont venues me voir.
Mes soeurs ont venu me voir.
Mes soeurs sont venu me voir.
Mes soeurs est venues me voir.
questions.answer: Mes soeurs sont venues me voir.
questions.explanation: venir uses être; the participle agrees feminine plural: sont venues.

questions.prompt: Complete with être + agreement, 'aller' for 'nous' (mixed group): 'Nous ___ au marché.'
questions.explanation: aller takes être; a mixed or masculine 'nous' gives sommes allés.

questions.prompt: Complete, 'descendre' for 'elle': 'Elle ___ par l'escalier.'
questions.explanation: descendre (intransitive movement) uses être: est descendue, feminine agreement.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'They stayed at home.'
questions.explanation: rester uses être; restés agrees with the masculine plural subject 'ils'.

activities.title: Un week-end à la mer
activities.intro: Your partner asks about your weekend trip. Play 'Me' and use être verbs.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu es parti où ce week-end ?
lines.en: Where did you go this weekend?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je suis allé à la mer avec ma soeur.
lines.en: I went to the sea with my sister.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vous êtes arrivés à quelle heure ?
lines.en: What time did you arrive?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nous sommes arrivés vers midi, sous le soleil.
lines.en: We arrived around noon, in the sunshine.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et ta soeur, elle est rentrée avec toi ?
lines.en: And your sister, did she come back with you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, elle est restée un jour de plus chez une amie.
lines.en: No, she stayed one more day at a friend's place.

days.title: Raconter une histoire au passé
days.objective: Combine passé composé, imparfait, and connectors to tell a coherent past story.
days.paretoFocus: A few connectors (d'abord, ensuite, puis, soudain, enfin) turn isolated sentences into a real narrative.
drills: Tell a 5-sentence story of a day that went wrong, using d'abord/ensuite/enfin.
Add one imparfait 'setting' sentence to open a story before the first event.
Retell a film's plot in six sentences, alternating description and events.

items: Recall four sequencing connectors for a story.
Which tense sets the scene at the start of a story?
Translate: 'First we ate, then we went out.'

activities.title: Les connecteurs du récit
activities.intro: Use imparfait to open the scene, passé composé for the chain of events, and connectors to order them.

items.hr: C'était un samedi matin ordinaire.
items.en: It was an ordinary Saturday morning.
items.note: imparfait sets the scene

items.hr: D'abord, nous avons pris le petit déjeuner.
items.en: First, we had breakfast.
items.note: d'abord = first

items.hr: Ensuite, nous sommes sortis faire les courses.
items.en: Then, we went out to do the shopping.
items.note: ensuite = then

items.hr: Soudain, il a commencé à pleuvoir.
items.en: Suddenly, it started to rain.
items.note: soudain introduces a surprise event

items.hr: Enfin, nous sommes rentrés, trempés mais heureux.
items.en: Finally, we came home, soaked but happy.
items.note: enfin = finally

activities.title: Construire un récit
activities.intro: Order the story and pick the tense that fits each moment.

questions.prompt: Which connector best begins a sequence of actions?
questions.explanation: 'D'abord' (first) opens a sequence; the others are not sequencing connectors.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Il faisait beau, alors nous ___ à la plage.'
questions.explanation: The setting is imparfait (faisait), the event is passé composé: sommes allés.

questions.prompt: Type a connector meaning 'then/next': 'On a mangé, ___ on a fait la vaisselle.'
questions.explanation: 'ensuite' links the next action in the sequence.

questions.prompt: Type the passé composé of 'voir' for 'nous': 'Puis nous ___ un beau coucher de soleil.'
questions.explanation: voir has the irregular participle 'vu': nous avons vu.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Suddenly the train left without us.'
questions.explanation: 'Soudain' opens a surprise event in the passé composé; partir uses être.

activities.title: Raconte-moi ta journée
activities.intro: Your partner wants the full story of your day. Play 'Me' and use connectors.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Raconte, comment s'est passée ta journée ?
lines.en: Tell me, how did your day go?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: D'abord, je me suis réveillé très tard.
lines.en: First, I woke up very late.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Oh non ! Et ensuite ?
lines.en: Oh no! And then?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ensuite, j'ai couru pour prendre le bus, mais il était déjà parti.
lines.en: Then, I ran to catch the bus, but it had already left.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quelle catastrophe ! Comment ça s'est terminé ?
lines.en: What a disaster! How did it end?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Enfin, un collègue m'a emmené en voiture et tout s'est bien passé.
lines.en: Finally, a colleague drove me and everything went well.

days.title: Les pronoms COD : le, la, les
days.objective: Replace direct objects with le, la, l' and les to avoid repetition.
days.paretoFocus: Ask 'quoi/qui?' after the verb: if there is a direct object with no preposition, replace it with le/la/les before the verb.
drills: Answer five 'Tu aimes...?' questions using le/la/les instead of the noun.
Rewrite five sentences replacing the direct object noun with a COD pronoun.
Practise placement in the negative: 'Je ne le connais pas.'

items: Recall: where does the COD pronoun go relative to the conjugated verb?
When does 'le' or 'la' become 'l''?
Translate: 'I know them well.'

activities.title: Remplacer le complément direct
activities.intro: A direct object (no preposition) is replaced by le (m.), la (f.), l' (before a vowel), or les (plural), placed before the verb.

items.hr: Tu vois le film ? Oui, je le vois.
items.en: Do you see the film? Yes, I see it.
items.note: le = the film

items.hr: Elle achète la robe ? Oui, elle l'achète.
items.en: Is she buying the dress? Yes, she is buying it.
items.note: la -> l' before a vowel

items.hr: Vous connaissez mes amis ? Oui, je les connais.
items.en: Do you know my friends? Yes, I know them.
items.note: les = plural

items.hr: Je ne le regarde pas.
items.en: I don't watch it.
items.note: pronoun stays before the verb in the negative

items.hr: Tu as pris les clés ? Oui, je les ai prises.
items.en: Did you take the keys? Yes, I took them.
items.note: COD before avoir triggers participle agreement: prises

activities.title: Utiliser le, la, les
activities.intro: Replace the direct object and place the pronoun correctly.

questions.prompt: Replace the object: 'Je regarde la télévision.'
questions.explanation: 'la télévision' is feminine singular, so it becomes 'la' before the verb: Je la regarde.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct answer: 'Tu invites Paul et Marie ?'
questions.explanation: Two people as a direct object become 'les': je les invite.

questions.prompt: Answer with a COD pronoun: 'Tu aimes ce livre ?' -> 'Oui, je ___ aime.'
questions.explanation: 'ce livre' is masculine, but 'aime' starts with a vowel, so le -> l': je l'aime.

questions.prompt: Rewrite with a pronoun: 'Je ne connais pas cette femme.' -> 'Je ne ___ connais pas.'
questions.explanation: 'cette femme' is feminine singular: la, placed before the verb even in the negative.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I've already seen them.'
questions.explanation: The COD 'les' sits before 'ai'; the participle agrees: vus (masculine plural).

activities.title: Tu le fais ?
activities.intro: Your partner asks about your habits. Play 'Me' and answer with COD pronouns.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu regardes souvent les séries françaises ?
lines.en: Do you often watch French series?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, je les regarde presque tous les soirs.
lines.en: Yes, I watch them almost every evening.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et le journal, tu le lis le matin ?
lines.en: And the newspaper, do you read it in the morning?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, je ne le lis pas, je préfère la radio.
lines.en: No, I don't read it, I prefer the radio.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu connais la nouvelle voisine ?
lines.en: Do you know the new neighbour?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, je la connais un peu, elle est très sympa.
lines.en: Yes, I know her a little, she is very nice.

days.title: Les pronoms COI : lui, leur
days.objective: Replace indirect objects introduced by 'à' with lui and leur, and combine them with COD pronouns.
days.paretoFocus: If the verb is followed by 'à + a person', use lui (singular) or leur (plural), regardless of gender.
drills: Answer five 'Tu parles à...?' questions with lui or leur.
Rewrite five sentences replacing 'à + person' with a COI pronoun.
Combine COD + COI in order: 'Je le lui donne.'

items: Recall: which pronoun replaces 'à mes parents'?
Does 'lui' change for gender? (no)
Translate: 'I am giving it to him.'

activities.title: Remplacer le complément indirect
activities.intro: Verbs like parler à, téléphoner à, donner à take an indirect object. Replace 'à + person' with lui (one person) or leur (several).

items.hr: Tu parles à ton frère ? Oui, je lui parle.
items.en: Do you talk to your brother? Yes, I talk to him.
items.note: lui = to him/to her

items.hr: Elle téléphone à ses parents ? Oui, elle leur téléphone.
items.en: Does she call her parents? Yes, she calls them.
items.note: leur = to them

items.hr: Je lui offre un cadeau.
items.en: I am giving him/her a gift.
items.note: lui works for both genders

items.hr: Ne leur dis pas la vérité.
items.en: Don't tell them the truth.
items.note: leur stays before the verb

items.hr: Je le lui donne.
items.en: I give it to him.
items.note: COD (le) before COI (lui) in this pair

activities.title: Utiliser lui et leur
activities.intro: Replace the indirect object and, where asked, combine pronouns in the right order.

questions.prompt: Replace the indirect object: 'Je téléphone à ma mère.'
questions.explanation: 'téléphoner à' + one person = lui (gender-neutral): Je lui téléphone.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct combination: 'Tu donnes le livre à Paul ?'
questions.explanation: COD 'le' (the book) comes before COI 'lui' (to Paul): je le lui donne.

questions.prompt: Answer with a COI pronoun: 'Tu écris à tes amis ?' -> 'Oui, je ___ écris.'
questions.explanation: 'à tes amis' is plural people, so the COI pronoun is leur.

questions.prompt: Rewrite: 'Elle offre des fleurs à sa grand-mère.' -> 'Elle ___ offre des fleurs.'
questions.explanation: 'à sa grand-mère' is one person, so lui replaces it (no feminine form).

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I've already spoken to them.'
questions.explanation: The COI 'leur' goes before the auxiliary 'ai'; no agreement with an indirect object.

activities.title: Tu leur as dit ?
activities.intro: Your partner asks who you spoke to. Play 'Me' and answer with lui/leur.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as parlé à tes parents de ton voyage ?
lines.en: Did you talk to your parents about your trip?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, je leur ai tout expliqué hier soir.
lines.en: Yes, I explained everything to them last night.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et à ta soeur, tu lui as envoyé les photos ?
lines.en: And to your sister, did you send her the photos?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, je les lui ai envoyées ce matin.
lines.en: Yes, I sent them to her this morning.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu vas offrir un souvenir à ton collègue ?
lines.en: Are you going to give a souvenir to your colleague?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bien sûr, je vais lui offrir un petit cadeau.
lines.en: Of course, I'm going to give him a little gift.

days.title: Les pronoms y et en
days.objective: Use y to replace places and 'à + thing', and en to replace 'de + noun' or quantities.
days.paretoFocus: Remember two triggers: 'y' for places/à + thing, 'en' for de + noun and any quantity.
drills: Answer five 'Tu vas à...?' questions using y.
Answer five 'Tu as combien de...?' questions using en.
Replace 'de la musique / au parc' in sentences with en / y.

items: Recall: which pronoun replaces a place like 'à Paris' or 'au marché'?
Which pronoun replaces 'du pain' or 'de la chance'?
Translate: 'I have three of them.'

activities.title: Quand utiliser y et en
activities.intro: 'y' replaces a place or 'à + thing'; 'en' replaces 'de + noun' or a quantity. Both go before the verb.

items.hr: Tu vas au marché ? Oui, j'y vais.
items.en: Are you going to the market? Yes, I'm going there.
items.note: y = place

items.hr: Elle pense à ses vacances ? Oui, elle y pense.
items.en: Is she thinking about her holidays? Yes, she thinks about it.
items.note: penser à + thing = y

items.hr: Tu veux du café ? Oui, j'en veux.
items.en: Do you want some coffee? Yes, I want some.
items.note: en = de + noun

items.hr: Combien de livres as-tu ? J'en ai trois.
items.en: How many books do you have? I have three (of them).
items.note: en with a quantity

items.hr: Nous n'y allons jamais.
items.en: We never go there.
items.note: y stays before the verb in the negative

activities.title: Choisir y ou en
activities.intro: Decide whether the sentence needs y (place / à) or en (de / quantity).

questions.prompt: Replace the phrase: 'Je vais à la piscine.'
questions.explanation: 'à la piscine' is a place, so it is replaced by 'y': J'y vais.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct answer: 'Tu bois de l'eau ?'
questions.explanation: 'de l'eau' (de + noun) is replaced by 'en': j'en bois.

questions.prompt: Answer with the right pronoun: 'Tu penses à ton examen ?' -> 'Oui, j'___ pense.'
questions.explanation: 'penser à + thing' is replaced by 'y': j'y pense.

questions.prompt: Answer with a quantity: 'Tu as des frères ?' -> 'Oui, j'___ ai deux.'
questions.explanation: With a quantity, 'des frères' is replaced by 'en': j'en ai deux.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'We need some.'
questions.explanation: 'avoir besoin de + noun' takes 'en'; the pronoun goes before the verb.

activities.title: Tu y vas ? Tu en veux ?
activities.intro: Your partner asks about plans and preferences. Play 'Me' and use y and en.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu vas souvent à la salle de sport ?
lines.en: Do you often go to the gym?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, j'y vais trois fois par semaine.
lines.en: Yes, I go there three times a week.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu bois des protéines après le sport ?
lines.en: Do you drink protein after working out?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, j'en bois toujours un peu.
lines.en: Yes, I always drink a bit of it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu penses parfois à arrêter ?
lines.en: Do you sometimes think about stopping?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, je n'y pense jamais, j'adore ça !
lines.en: No, I never think about it, I love it!

days.title: Révision des pronoms
days.objective: Consolidate COD, COI, y, and en, choosing the right pronoun and placing it correctly.
days.paretoFocus: One decision tree: direct object -> le/la/les; à + person -> lui/leur; place/à + thing -> y; de + noun/quantity -> en.
drills: Answer ten mixed questions, each requiring a different pronoun type.
Rewrite a short paragraph replacing every repeated noun with the correct pronoun.
Practise combined pronouns: 'Je le lui donne', 'Il m'en parle'.

items: Recall the four pronoun triggers (direct object, à + person, place, de/quantity).
Give the pronoun order when COD and COI are both present.
Translate: 'She gives them to us.'

activities.title: Le bon pronom au bon endroit
activities.intro: Review all four pronoun types together and notice how each is triggered by the verb's construction.

items.hr: Tu vois Marie ? Oui, je la vois. (COD)
items.en: Do you see Marie? Yes, I see her.
items.note: direct object -> la

items.hr: Tu parles à Marie ? Oui, je lui parle. (COI)
items.en: Do you talk to Marie? Yes, I talk to her.
items.note: à + person -> lui

items.hr: Tu vas chez Marie ? Oui, j'y vais. (lieu)
items.en: Are you going to Marie's? Yes, I'm going there.
items.note: place -> y

items.hr: Tu parles de Marie ? Oui, j'en parle. (de + nom)
items.en: Do you talk about Marie? Yes, I talk about her/it.
items.note: de + noun -> en

items.hr: Il me le donne, puis il m'en parle.
items.en: He gives it to me, then he talks to me about it.
items.note: combined pronouns before the verb

activities.title: Tous les pronoms
activities.intro: Pick the correct pronoun type for each construction and place it correctly.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct pronoun: 'Je réponds à mes collègues.'
questions.explanation: 'répondre à + people' is a COI with several people: leur.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct answer: 'Tu as besoin de ce stylo ?'
questions.explanation: 'avoir besoin de + noun' takes en: j'en ai besoin.

questions.prompt: Replace the place: 'Je travaille à Zagreb.' -> 'J'___ travaille.'
questions.explanation: 'à Zagreb' is a place, replaced by y: j'y travaille.

questions.prompt: Combine COD + COI: 'Il donne le cadeau à sa mère.' -> 'Il ___ ___ donne.' (two words)
questions.explanation: COD 'le' precedes COI 'lui': il le lui donne.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'She sent them to us.'
questions.explanation: The indirect 'nous' comes before the direct 'les'; the participle agrees: envoyés.

activities.title: Un petit test amical
activities.intro: Your partner quizzes you with rapid questions. Play 'Me' and use the right pronoun each time.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as vu le nouveau film ?
lines.en: Have you seen the new film?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, je l'ai vu la semaine dernière.
lines.en: Yes, I saw it last week.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as parlé à Julie de la soirée ?
lines.en: Did you talk to Julie about the party?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, je lui en ai parlé hier.
lines.en: Yes, I talked to her about it yesterday.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu vas au cours de français demain ?
lines.en: Are you going to the French class tomorrow?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, j'y vais, et j'apporte des gâteaux : j'en ai fait plein !
lines.en: Yes, I'm going there, and I'm bringing cakes: I made lots of them!

days.title: Le futur simple : formation et radicaux irréguliers
days.objective: Learn to form the simple future tense with regular and irregular stems.
days.paretoFocus: Master the future endings (-ai, -as, -a, -ons, -ez, -ont) and the most common irregular stems (être, avoir, aller, faire).
drills: Conjugate five regular -er verbs in the future for all persons.
Write the irregular future stems for être, avoir, aller, faire, venir.
Say three sentences about tomorrow using the future simple.

items: Recall the six future endings out loud.
Conjugate 'aller' and 'faire' in the future for je, tu, nous.
Translate: 'I will speak', 'They will be', 'We will have'.

activities.title: Le futur simple
activities.intro: The simple future is formed from an infinitive-based stem plus fixed endings. Many common verbs have irregular stems you must memorize.

items.hr: je parlerai
items.en: I will speak
items.note: Regular: infinitive + ai

items.hr: tu finiras
items.en: you will finish
items.note: -ir verbs keep the full infinitive

items.hr: il sera
items.en: he will be
items.note: Irregular stem: ser-

items.hr: nous aurons
items.en: we will have
items.note: Irregular stem: aur-

items.hr: vous irez
items.en: you will go
items.note: Irregular stem: ir- (aller)

items.hr: elles feront
items.en: they will do/make
items.note: Irregular stem: fer- (faire)

activities.title: Pratiquer le futur simple
activities.intro: Choose or type the correct future form. Watch out for irregular stems.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct future form: 'Demain, je ___ à Paris.' (aller)
questions.explanation: The verb 'aller' has the irregular future stem 'ir-', so je + ir- + ai = irai.

questions.prompt: Type the future form of 'être' for 'nous': Nous ___ prêts.
questions.explanation: 'Être' uses the stem 'ser-'; nous + ser- + ons = serons.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct future form: 'Vous ___ vos devoirs ce soir.' (faire)
questions.explanation: 'Faire' has the irregular stem 'fer-'; vous + fer- + ez = ferez.

questions.prompt: Type the future form of 'parler' for 'ils': Ils ___ français.
questions.explanation: Regular -er verb: infinitive 'parler' + ont = parleront.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'We will have plenty of time.'
questions.explanation: 'Avoir' has the future stem 'aur-'; the sentence means 'We will have a lot of time.'

activities.title: Nos projets pour demain
activities.intro: You play 'Me'. Talk with a friend about what you will do tomorrow, using the simple future.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Qu'est-ce que tu feras demain ?
lines.en: What will you do tomorrow?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Demain, je travaillerai le matin.
lines.en: Tomorrow, I will work in the morning.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et l'après-midi, tu seras libre ?
lines.en: And in the afternoon, will you be free?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, j'irai au cinéma avec ma sœur.
lines.en: Yes, I will go to the cinema with my sister.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vous aurez le temps de dîner après ?
lines.en: Will you have time to have dinner afterwards?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bien sûr, nous mangerons ensemble au restaurant.
lines.en: Of course, we will eat together at the restaurant.

days.title: Faire des projets : plans et prédictions
days.objective: Use the future and useful expressions to talk about plans and make predictions.
days.paretoFocus: Combine the future simple with time markers and project verbs (avoir l'intention de, penser, projeter) to express intentions.
drills: Write three sentences about your plans for next year using the future.
Make two predictions about the weather using 'il fera' and 'il y aura'.
Use 'avoir l'intention de + infinitive' in two sentences.

items: Recall three time markers for the future (demain, la semaine prochaine, dans deux ans).
Say what you will do next weekend.
Translate: 'It will rain tomorrow', 'I intend to travel'.

activities.title: Parler de ses projets
activities.intro: To describe plans and predictions, combine the future tense with time expressions and intention verbs.

items.hr: la semaine prochaine
items.en: next week
items.note: Common future time marker

items.hr: avoir l'intention de
items.en: to intend to
items.note: Followed by an infinitive

items.hr: Je pense voyager en Italie.
items.en: I'm thinking of travelling to Italy.
items.note: penser + infinitive

items.hr: Il fera beau demain.
items.en: It will be nice tomorrow.
items.note: Weather prediction with faire

items.hr: Dans deux ans, je déménagerai.
items.en: In two years, I will move house.

activities.title: Projets et prédictions
activities.intro: Practise expressing plans and predictions.

questions.prompt: Which expression means 'to intend to'?
questions.explanation: 'Avoir l'intention de' means 'to intend to' and is followed by an infinitive.

questions.prompt: Type the future of 'faire' for weather: Demain, il ___ beau.
questions.explanation: Weather predictions use 'faire': il + fer- + a = fera.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct time marker: '___, je commencerai un nouveau travail.'
questions.explanation: 'La semaine prochaine' (next week) points to the future, matching the future verb.

questions.prompt: Complete with 'y avoir' in the future: Il ___ beaucoup de monde à la fête.
questions.explanation: 'Il y a' becomes 'il y aura' in the future; 'avoir' uses the stem 'aur-'.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I intend to go on holiday.'
questions.explanation: 'Avoir l'intention de + infinitive' expresses an intention: 'I intend to go on holiday.'

activities.title: Les vacances de l'été prochain
activities.intro: You play 'Me'. Discuss summer holiday plans and predictions with a colleague.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quels sont tes projets pour l'été prochain ?
lines.en: What are your plans for next summer?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: J'ai l'intention de voyager en Espagne.
lines.en: I intend to travel to Spain.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Il fera très chaud là-bas en août.
lines.en: It will be very hot there in August.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est vrai, mais je pense visiter la côte.
lines.en: That's true, but I'm thinking of visiting the coast.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Est-ce qu'il y aura beaucoup de touristes ?
lines.en: Will there be a lot of tourists?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sûrement, donc je réserverai mon hôtel tôt.
lines.en: Surely, so I will book my hotel early.

days.title: Le comparatif : plus, moins, aussi... que
days.objective: Compare people and things using the comparative of adjectives and adverbs.
days.paretoFocus: Use 'plus/moins/aussi + adjective + que' correctly, including the irregular 'meilleur' for 'bon'.
drills: Compare two cities using plus, moins and aussi.
Write one sentence with 'meilleur que' and one with 'mieux que'.
Compare two foods using an adjective of your choice.

items: Recall the three comparative words (plus, moins, aussi).
Translate: 'Paris is bigger than Lyon.'
Give the comparative of 'bon' and 'bien'.

activities.title: Comparer avec des adjectifs
activities.intro: The comparative expresses more, less, or equal degrees. The pattern is plus/moins/aussi + adjective + que.

items.hr: plus grand que
items.en: bigger than
items.note: superiority

items.hr: moins cher que
items.en: less expensive than
items.note: inferiority

items.hr: aussi rapide que
items.en: as fast as
items.note: equality

items.hr: meilleur que
items.en: better than
items.note: Irregular comparative of 'bon'

items.hr: Elle est plus grande que moi.
items.en: She is taller than me.
items.note: Adjective agrees with the subject

activities.title: Faire des comparaisons
activities.intro: Practise comparatives of adjectives and adverbs.

questions.prompt: Which is the correct comparative of 'bon'?
questions.explanation: 'Bon' has the irregular comparative 'meilleur'; you never say 'plus bon'.

questions.prompt: Complete with the comparative of equality: Marie est ___ intelligente ___ Paul.
questions.explanation: Equality uses 'aussi + adjective + que': 'aussi intelligente que'.

questions.prompt: Choose the sentence meaning 'This car is less expensive than that one.'
options: Cette voiture est moins chère que celle-là.
Cette voiture est plus chère que celle-là.
Cette voiture est aussi chère que celle-là.
questions.answer: Cette voiture est moins chère que celle-là.
questions.explanation: 'Moins... que' expresses inferiority ('less expensive than').

questions.prompt: Type the missing word: Le train est ___ rapide que le bus. (superiority)
questions.explanation: Superiority is expressed with 'plus + adjective + que'.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'This book is more interesting than the film.'
questions.explanation: The comparative structure is 'plus + adjective + que': 'This book is more interesting than the film.'

activities.title: Comparer deux villes
activities.intro: You play 'Me'. Compare two cities you know with a friend.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu préfères Paris ou Lyon ?
lines.en: Do you prefer Paris or Lyon?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Paris est plus grand que Lyon.
lines.en: Paris is bigger than Lyon.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mais Paris est aussi plus cher, non ?
lines.en: But Paris is also more expensive, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, Lyon est moins cher que Paris.
lines.en: Yes, Lyon is less expensive than Paris.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et la nourriture ?
lines.en: And the food?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pour moi, la cuisine de Lyon est meilleure que celle de Paris.
lines.en: For me, the food in Lyon is better than the food in Paris.

days.title: Le superlatif : le plus, le moins
days.objective: Express the highest or lowest degree using the superlative.
days.paretoFocus: Form 'le/la/les plus/moins + adjective' with correct article agreement and the irregular 'le meilleur'.
drills: Name the best and worst thing in your city using superlatives.
Write two sentences using 'le/la plus + adjective'.
Use 'le meilleur' in one sentence.

items: Recall how the superlative differs from the comparative.
Translate: 'It is the biggest city in France.'
Give the superlative of 'bon'.

activities.title: Le superlatif
activities.intro: The superlative expresses the extreme degree. Use the definite article with plus or moins, agreeing in gender and number.

items.hr: le plus grand
items.en: the biggest (masc.)
items.note: article agrees with the noun

items.hr: la plus belle
items.en: the most beautiful (fem.)

items.hr: les moins chers
items.en: the least expensive (plural)

items.hr: le meilleur
items.en: the best
items.note: Irregular superlative of 'bon'

items.hr: C'est la plus grande ville de France.
items.en: It is the biggest city in France.
items.note: 'de' introduces the group compared

activities.title: Utiliser le superlatif
activities.intro: Practise forming and using superlatives.

questions.prompt: Choose the superlative: 'C'est ___ restaurant de la ville.' (the best)
questions.explanation: The superlative of 'bon' is 'le meilleur'; 'le plus bon' is incorrect.

questions.prompt: Complete the feminine superlative: C'est ___ plus belle plage du pays.
questions.explanation: The article agrees with 'plage' (feminine): 'la plus belle'.

questions.prompt: Which sentence means 'These are the least expensive shoes'?
options: Ce sont les chaussures les moins chères.
Ce sont les chaussures les plus chères.
Ce sont des chaussures moins chères.
Ce sont les chaussures aussi chères.
questions.answer: Ce sont les chaussures les moins chères.
questions.explanation: 'Les moins chères' is the superlative of inferiority, agreeing in the feminine plural.

questions.prompt: Type the missing word for the superlative: C'est le film ___ intéressant de l'année. (most)
questions.explanation: For a noun placed adjective, repeat the article: 'le film le plus intéressant'.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'It's the biggest city in the country.'
questions.explanation: Superlative 'la plus grande' agrees with 'ville'; 'du pays' shows the group compared.

activities.title: Le meilleur de la ville
activities.intro: You play 'Me'. Recommend the best places in your city to a visitor.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quel est le meilleur café de la ville ?
lines.en: What is the best café in the city?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Le café de la place est le meilleur.
lines.en: The café on the square is the best.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et le musée le plus intéressant ?
lines.en: And the most interesting museum?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est le musée d'art moderne, sans doute.
lines.en: It's the modern art museum, no doubt.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Où sont les restaurants les moins chers ?
lines.en: Where are the least expensive restaurants?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Les restaurants les moins chers sont près de la gare.
lines.en: The least expensive restaurants are near the station.

days.title: Les pronoms relatifs : qui et que
days.objective: Connect clauses using the relative pronouns qui (subject) and que (object).
days.paretoFocus: Choose 'qui' when the pronoun is the subject of the verb and 'que' when it is the direct object.
drills: Write two sentences using 'qui' as the subject.
Write two sentences using 'que' as the object.
Join two short sentences into one using qui or que.

items: Recall the difference between qui and que.
Translate: 'The man who speaks is my teacher.'
Translate: 'The book that I read is good.'

activities.title: Qui ou que ?
activities.intro: Relative pronouns link two clauses. 'Qui' replaces the subject; 'que' replaces the direct object.

items.hr: L'homme qui parle est mon voisin.
items.en: The man who is speaking is my neighbour.
items.note: 'qui' = subject of 'parle'

items.hr: Le livre que je lis est intéressant.
items.en: The book that I am reading is interesting.
items.note: 'que' = object of 'lis'

items.hr: la femme qui travaille ici
items.en: the woman who works here

items.hr: le film que nous regardons
items.en: the film that we are watching

items.hr: qu'il
items.en: that he
items.note: 'que' becomes 'qu'' before a vowel

activities.title: Choisir qui ou que
activities.intro: Decide whether the relative pronoun is a subject (qui) or an object (que).

questions.prompt: Choose: 'C'est la personne ___ habite à côté.'
questions.explanation: The pronoun is the subject of 'habite', so use 'qui'.

questions.prompt: Type the correct relative pronoun: Le gâteau ___ tu as fait est délicieux.
questions.explanation: The pronoun is the direct object of 'as fait', so use 'que'.

questions.prompt: Choose: 'Voici le train ___ va à Marseille.'
questions.explanation: 'Le train' is the subject of 'va', so 'qui' is correct.

questions.prompt: Type qui or que: Les amis ___ j'invite sont sympas.
questions.explanation: 'Les amis' is the object of 'invite' (I invite them), so use 'que'.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'The car I'm buying is red.'
questions.explanation: 'Que' is the direct object of 'achète': 'The car that I am buying is red.'

activities.title: Parler de nos amis
activities.intro: You play 'Me'. Describe people and things using qui and que.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Qui est la fille qui parle avec Marc ?
lines.en: Who is the girl who is talking with Marc?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est une amie que je connais depuis l'école.
lines.en: She's a friend that I have known since school.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et le garçon qui porte un chapeau ?
lines.en: And the boy who is wearing a hat?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est le cousin qui habite à Nice.
lines.en: He's the cousin who lives in Nice.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: C'est la personne que tu as invitée hier ?
lines.en: Is he the person that you invited yesterday?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, c'est le voisin que j'aime beaucoup.
lines.en: Yes, he's the neighbour that I like a lot.

days.title: Décrire avec qui et que : relier ses phrases
days.objective: Build richer descriptions by linking sentences with relative pronouns.
days.paretoFocus: Combine two simple sentences into one longer descriptive sentence using qui or que.
drills: Describe an object in your room using a relative clause.
Describe a person you admire using qui and que.
Rewrite three pairs of sentences as single sentences.

items: Recall when to use qui versus que.
Join: 'J'ai un ami. Il parle cinq langues.'
Join: 'Voici la maison. Nous l'avons achetée.'

activities.title: Relier ses phrases
activities.intro: To describe in detail, merge short sentences with qui (subject) or que (object) so your speech flows.

items.hr: J'ai un ami qui parle cinq langues.
items.en: I have a friend who speaks five languages.
items.note: Combines two ideas

items.hr: Voici la maison que nous avons achetée.
items.en: Here is the house that we bought.
items.note: Past participle agrees with 'que'

items.hr: un objet qui est très utile
items.en: an object that is very useful

items.hr: le plat que je préfère
items.en: the dish that I prefer

items.hr: une ville qui a beaucoup de charme
items.en: a city that has a lot of charm

activities.title: Combiner des phrases
activities.intro: Merge or complete sentences with the right relative pronoun.

questions.prompt: Combine: 'J'ai un chien. Il est très gentil.' Which is correct?
options: J'ai un chien qui est très gentil.
J'ai un chien que est très gentil.
J'ai un chien qu'il est très gentil.
J'ai un chien où est très gentil.
questions.answer: J'ai un chien qui est très gentil.
questions.explanation: 'Le chien' is the subject of 'est', so use 'qui'.

questions.prompt: Type qui or que: C'est un sujet ___ m'intéresse beaucoup.
questions.explanation: 'Un sujet' is the subject of 'intéresse', so use 'qui'.

questions.prompt: Combine: 'Voici le gâteau. Ma mère l'a préparé.' Which is correct?
options: Voici le gâteau que ma mère a préparé.
Voici le gâteau qui ma mère a préparé.
Voici le gâteau qu'il ma mère a préparé.
Voici le gâteau où ma mère a préparé.
questions.answer: Voici le gâteau que ma mère a préparé.
questions.explanation: 'Le gâteau' is the object of 'a préparé', so use 'que'.

questions.prompt: Type the relative pronoun: La chanson ___ tu écoutes est belle.
questions.explanation: 'La chanson' is the object of 'écoutes' (you listen to it), so use 'que'.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I like people who are honest.'
questions.explanation: 'Qui' is the subject of 'sont': 'I like people who are honest.'

activities.title: Décrire un objet préféré
activities.intro: You play 'Me'. Describe your favourite objects and people using relative clauses.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quel est l'objet que tu préfères chez toi ?
lines.en: What is the object that you prefer at home?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est une montre qui appartenait à mon grand-père.
lines.en: It's a watch that belonged to my grandfather.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: C'est un cadeau que tu as reçu ?
lines.en: Is it a gift that you received?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, c'est un souvenir qui compte beaucoup pour moi.
lines.en: Yes, it's a memory that matters a lot to me.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et la personne que tu admires le plus ?
lines.en: And the person that you admire the most?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est ma mère, qui est très courageuse.
lines.en: It's my mother, who is very brave.

days.title: Venir de et être en train de
days.objective: Express the recent past and an action in progress.
days.paretoFocus: Use 'venir de + infinitive' for something just done and 'être en train de + infinitive' for something happening now.
drills: Say three things you have just done using 'venir de'.
Describe two actions in progress using 'être en train de'.
Contrast one recent past and one ongoing action in the same sentence.

items: Recall the meaning of 'venir de' and 'être en train de'.
Translate: 'I have just eaten.'
Translate: 'She is (in the middle of) working.'

activities.title: Passé récent et action en cours
activities.intro: 'Venir de + infinitive' means to have just done something. 'Être en train de + infinitive' means to be in the middle of doing something.

items.hr: Je viens de manger.
items.en: I have just eaten.
items.note: recent past

items.hr: Il vient d'arriver.
items.en: He has just arrived.
items.note: 'de' becomes 'd'' before a vowel

items.hr: Nous sommes en train de travailler.
items.en: We are (busy) working.
items.note: action in progress

items.hr: Elle est en train de lire.
items.en: She is (in the middle of) reading.

items.hr: Tu viens de partir ?
items.en: Have you just left?

activities.title: Récent ou en cours ?
activities.intro: Choose between the recent past and the action in progress.

questions.prompt: Choose: 'Je ___ finir mes devoirs, ils sont terminés.'
questions.explanation: 'Viens de finir' means 'have just finished', the action is completed.

questions.prompt: Complete with 'être en train de': Ils ___ préparer le dîner en ce moment.
questions.explanation: For an action in progress, use 'être en train de': ils sont en train de préparer.

questions.prompt: Which means 'She has just called'?
questions.explanation: 'Venir de + infinitive' expresses the recent past: 'has just called'.

questions.prompt: Type 'venir de' for 'nous': Nous ___ arriver à la maison.
questions.explanation: 'Venir' conjugated: nous venons; before the vowel 'arriver', 'de' becomes 'd''.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I'm in the middle of reading a book.'
questions.explanation: 'Être en train de + infinitive' shows an ongoing action: 'I am reading a book.'

activities.title: Qu'est-ce que tu fais ?
activities.intro: You play 'Me'. A friend calls to ask what you are doing and what you just did.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Allô ! Qu'est-ce que tu fais maintenant ?
lines.en: Hello! What are you doing now?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je suis en train de préparer le déjeuner.
lines.en: I am (busy) preparing lunch.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ah, et Paul, il est là ?
lines.en: Ah, and Paul, is he there?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, il vient de sortir.
lines.en: No, he has just gone out.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vous venez de manger ?
lines.en: Have you just eaten?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, nous sommes en train de cuisiner ensemble.
lines.en: No, we are cooking together.

days.title: Les connecteurs : parce que, donc, d'abord, ensuite, enfin
days.objective: Organize and connect ideas with cause, consequence, and sequence connectors.
days.paretoFocus: Use 'parce que' for cause, 'donc' for consequence, and 'd'abord/ensuite/enfin' to sequence a series of actions.
drills: Explain a choice using 'parce que'.
State a consequence using 'donc'.
Describe your morning routine using d'abord, ensuite, enfin.

items: Recall a cause connector and a consequence connector.
Order the sequence words: d'abord, ensuite, enfin.
Translate: 'I am tired, so I am going to bed.'

activities.title: Relier ses idées
activities.intro: Connectors organize discourse. Use them to explain causes, consequences, and the order of events.

items.hr: parce que
items.en: because
items.note: introduces a cause

items.hr: donc
items.en: so / therefore
items.note: introduces a consequence

items.hr: d'abord
items.en: first
items.note: start of a sequence

items.hr: ensuite
items.en: then / next
items.note: middle of a sequence

items.hr: enfin
items.en: finally
items.note: end of a sequence

items.hr: Je suis fatigué, donc je vais dormir.
items.en: I am tired, so I am going to sleep.

activities.title: Employer les connecteurs
activities.intro: Choose the connector that fits the logic of each sentence.

questions.prompt: Choose: 'Je reste à la maison ___ il pleut.'
questions.explanation: A cause is introduced by 'parce que', becoming 'parce qu'' before the vowel 'il'.

questions.prompt: Type the consequence connector: Il fait froid, ___ je mets un manteau.

days.title: Les pronoms toniques : moi, toi, lui, elle
days.objective: Use stressed pronouns to emphasise who you mean, answer short questions and follow prepositions naturally.
days.paretoFocus: Stressed pronouns appear in almost every real conversation: after prepositions, in comparisons and in short answers like 'Moi aussi'.
drills: Say five sentences about your friends using 'avec moi', 'chez lui', 'sans elle', 'pour nous', 'comme eux'.

items: List the eight stressed pronouns from memory in one minute.
Which pronoun follows 'chez' for a group of men? Say a full sentence with it.
Rebuild three comparisons ending in 'que moi', 'que toi', 'que lui'.

activities.title: Moi, toi, lui, elle
activities.intro: French has a second set of personal pronouns used when the pronoun stands alone or carries stress: moi, toi, lui, elle, nous, vous, eux, elles. You need them after a preposition, after 'que' in a comparison, after 'c'est', and when you put the person first for emphasis. They never replace the subject pronoun, they are added to it.

items.hr: Moi, je préfère le train.
items.en: Me, I prefer the train.
items.note: The stressed pronoun is added before the normal subject 'je'.

items.hr: Tu viens au cinéma avec nous ?
items.en: Are you coming to the cinema with us?
items.note: After a preposition, always the stressed form.

items.hr: C'est lui qui a réservé la table.
items.en: He is the one who booked the table.
items.note: c'est + stressed pronoun to point someone out.

items.hr: Elle court plus vite que moi.
items.en: She runs faster than me.
items.note: After 'que' in a comparison, never 'je'.

items.hr: Eux, ils habitent à Lyon depuis longtemps.
items.en: Them, they have lived in Lyon for a long time.
items.note: eux = they (masculine or mixed), elles = they (all feminine).

activities.title: Insister sur la personne
activities.intro: Practise choosing the right stressed pronoun after prepositions, comparisons and 'c'est'.

questions.prompt: Which pronoun replaces 'Paul' in 'Je travaille avec Paul' ?
questions.explanation: After a preposition such as 'avec' French uses a stressed pronoun, and the masculine singular stressed form is 'lui'.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'C'est ___ qui ont réservé la table.' (they, a group of men)
questions.explanation: After 'c'est' you need a stressed pronoun, and the masculine plural stressed form is 'eux'. 'ils' can only be a subject in front of a verb.

questions.prompt: Complete the comparison: 'Ma sœur est plus grande que ___.' (than me)
questions.explanation: 'que' introduces the second part of a comparison and is always followed by a stressed pronoun, so 'je' is impossible here.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Vous partez déjà sans ___ ?' (without him)
questions.explanation: 'sans' is a preposition, so the masculine singular stressed pronoun 'lui' is required.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Me, I am staying here with them.'
questions.explanation: The stressed pronoun opens the sentence for emphasis, the normal subject 'je' still comes before the verb, and 'avec' takes the stressed form 'eux'.

activities.title: Qui part avec qui ?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are organising a car journey and you keep saying who travels with whom, using stressed pronouns.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Alors, qui part avec moi samedi ?
lines.en: So, who is leaving with me on Saturday?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Moi, je pars avec toi. Marie vient aussi ?
lines.en: Me, I am going with you. Is Marie coming too?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Non, elle préfère voyager avec eux, ils partent plus tôt.
lines.en: No, she prefers to travel with them, they are leaving earlier.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: D'accord. Et Thomas ? Il rentre chez lui après ?
lines.en: All right. And Thomas? Is he going back home afterwards?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Oui, mais il conduit moins bien que toi.
lines.en: Yes, but he drives less well than you.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Alors c'est moi qui conduis. Vous êtes d'accord, vous ?
lines.en: Then I am the one who drives. Do you agree?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Nous, on est d'accord. Rendez-vous chez nous à huit heures.
lines.en: We agree. See you at our place at eight.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Parfait, je serai chez vous avant eux.
lines.en: Perfect, I will be at your place before them.

days.title: Les adverbes en -ment : dire comment on fait les choses
days.objective: Form adverbs from adjectives with -ment and use them to say how an action is done.
days.paretoFocus: One suffix turns hundreds of adjectives you already know into adverbs, which instantly makes your descriptions more precise.
drills: Turn ten adjectives you know into adverbs and say a short sentence with each.
Describe how three people you know speak, work and drive, using one adverb each time.
Say the same sentence three times, changing only the adverb: lentement, rapidement, calmement.

items: State the rule for adjectives ending in a vowel and give one example.
What happens to adjectives ending in -ant and in -ent? Give one adverb for each.
Produce five adverbs from memory and use them in five sentences about your week.

activities.title: Comment ? Avec -ment
activities.intro: Most French adverbs of manner are built from the feminine form of the adjective plus -ment: lent becomes lente, then lentement. If the adjective already ends in a vowel, you add -ment directly to the masculine form. Adjectives in -ant and -ent give -amment and -emment, both pronounced the same way.

items.hr: Elle parle lentement quand elle explique.
items.en: She speaks slowly when she explains.
items.note: lent, lente, lentement

items.hr: Mon frère conduit très prudemment.
items.en: My brother drives very carefully.
items.note: prudent gives prudemment, not prudentment

items.hr: Répondez poliment au client.
items.en: Answer the customer politely.
items.note: poli ends in a vowel, so just add -ment

items.hr: Ils changent constamment d'avis.
items.en: They constantly change their minds.
items.note: constant gives constamment

items.hr: C'est vraiment gentil de ta part.
items.en: That is really kind of you.
items.note: vrai, vraiment: no feminine -e before the suffix

activities.title: Former les adverbes
activities.intro: Practise building adverbs from adjectives and placing them correctly in the sentence.

questions.prompt: What is the adverb built from the adjective 'heureux'?
questions.explanation: You start from the feminine form 'heureuse' and add -ment, which gives heureusement.

questions.prompt: What is the adverb built from the adjective 'fréquent'?
questions.explanation: Adjectives ending in -ent replace that ending with -emment, so fréquent gives fréquemment.

questions.prompt: Build the adverb from 'doux' and complete: 'Parle plus ___, le bébé dort.'
questions.explanation: The feminine of doux is douce, and adding -ment gives doucement.

questions.prompt: Build the adverb from 'facile' and complete: 'Il a trouvé la solution très ___.'
questions.explanation: Facile already ends in -e, so you simply add -ment to get facilement.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'She always answers very politely.'
questions.explanation: Short adverbs like 'toujours' come right after the verb, and the adverb of manner closes the sentence.

activities.title: Un nouveau collègue
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are describing how a new colleague works, using adverbs in -ment in every answer.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Alors, comment travaille le nouveau ?
lines.en: So, how does the new guy work?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il travaille sérieusement, mais un peu lentement.
lines.en: He works conscientiously, but a bit slowly.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et avec les clients, il se débrouille comment ?
lines.en: And how does he manage with the customers?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il leur parle toujours calmement et très poliment.
lines.en: He always speaks to them calmly and very politely.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Il apprend vite, tu crois ?
lines.en: Do you think he learns quickly?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, il comprend rapidement et il note tout soigneusement.
lines.en: Yes, he understands quickly and he writes everything down carefully.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bon, alors on le garde.
lines.en: Right, we are keeping him then.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Franchement, c'est vraiment une bonne idée.
lines.en: Honestly, that is really a good idea.

days.title: La négation complète : ne... plus, ne... jamais, ne... rien, ne... personne
days.objective: Use the full range of French negations and place them correctly in the present and the passé composé.
days.paretoFocus: Saying 'not any more', 'never', 'nothing' and 'nobody' covers most of what you need to refuse, correct and explain a change in your life.
drills: Say four true sentences about yourself, one with each negation.
Turn five affirmative past sentences into the passé composé with 'rien' and with 'personne'.
Describe an empty office using 'il n'y a personne' and 'il n'y a rien'.

items: Where does 'rien' go in the passé composé, and where does 'personne' go?
Give the negation that means a habit has stopped, in a full sentence.
Say two sentences where 'personne' and 'rien' are the subject.

activities.title: Ne... plus, ne... jamais, ne... rien, ne... personne
activities.intro: All these negations use 'ne' before the verb and a second word after it. In the passé composé, 'plus', 'jamais' and 'rien' sit between the auxiliary and the participle, but 'personne' comes after the participle. 'Rien' and 'personne' can also be the subject, and then 'ne' still stays in front of the verb.

items.hr: Je ne fume plus depuis janvier.
items.en: I do not smoke any more since January.
items.note: ne... plus marks the end of a habit

items.hr: Elle ne mange jamais de viande.
items.en: She never eats meat.
items.note: after a negation, du, de la and des become de

items.hr: Je n'ai rien compris à la réunion.
items.en: I did not understand anything in the meeting.
items.note: rien sits before the past participle

items.hr: Je n'ai vu personne dans le couloir.
items.en: I saw nobody in the corridor.
items.note: personne comes after the past participle

items.hr: Personne ne m'a répondu.
items.en: Nobody answered me.
items.note: personne as subject, but 'ne' stays before the verb

activities.title: Tout refuser
activities.intro: Practise choosing the right negation and putting it in the right place.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is correct for 'I saw nobody at the party'?
options: Je n'ai personne vu à la fête.
Je n'ai vu personne à la fête.
Je ne personne ai vu à la fête.
Je n'ai vu rien à la fête.
questions.answer: Je n'ai vu personne à la fête.
questions.explanation: Unlike 'rien', the word 'personne' is placed after the past participle, so the correct order is auxiliary, participle, personne.

questions.prompt: Which negation says that something used to be true but has stopped?
questions.explanation: 'ne... plus' means 'not any more' and marks the end of a situation or a habit.

questions.prompt: Complete so the sentence means he said nothing: 'Il n'a ___ dit pendant toute la réunion.'
questions.explanation: 'rien' goes between the auxiliary 'a' and the past participle 'dit'.

questions.prompt: Complete so the sentence means she never goes: 'Elle ne va ___ au cinéma, elle déteste ça.'
questions.explanation: 'ne... jamais' expresses a total absence of frequency, and the second element follows the conjugated verb.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'There is nobody in the kitchen.'
questions.explanation: 'ne' becomes 'n'' before the 'y' of 'il y a', and 'personne' follows the verb.

activities.title: Un bureau vide
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You arrive at the office late in the evening and answer everything in the negative, using a different negation each time.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as trouvé quelqu'un au bureau ?
lines.en: Did you find anybody at the office?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, je n'ai vu personne, tout était fermé.
lines.en: No, I saw nobody, everything was closed.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et le gardien ? Il t'a expliqué quelque chose ?
lines.en: And the caretaker? Did he explain anything to you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il ne m'a rien dit, il ne parle jamais beaucoup.
lines.en: He told me nothing, he never talks much.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu passes encore là-bas le samedi ?
lines.en: Do you still go there on Saturdays?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, je n'y vais plus, c'est trop loin de chez moi.
lines.en: No, I do not go there any more, it is too far from my place.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Personne ne t'a prévenu du changement, alors ?
lines.en: So nobody warned you about the change?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Personne. Je n'ai reçu aucun message.
lines.en: Nobody. I did not get a single message.

days.title: Ne... que : exprimer la restriction
days.objective: Express restriction with ne... que and tell it apart from a real negation.
days.paretoFocus: 'Only' is one of the most used words in daily life, and French expresses it with a structure that looks like a negation but means the opposite.
drills: Say five true sentences about your budget, your time and your diet using this structure.
Take three negative sentences you know and change them so they restrict instead of deny.
Move the restricting word around one sentence and say aloud what changes in the meaning.

items: Explain why 'Je ne bois que du café' keeps 'du' and not 'de'.
Give two sentences where the restriction falls on a time expression.
Say the same idea twice: once with the restriction, once with 'seulement'.

activities.title: La restriction avec ne... que
activities.intro: 'ne... que' means 'only'. It is built like a negation, but it does not deny anything: it limits. The word 'que' is placed immediately before the element you are restricting, so moving it changes the meaning. Because it is not a true negation, partitive articles such as du and de la stay unchanged.

items.hr: Je n'ai que dix euros sur moi.
items.en: I only have ten euros on me.
items.note: que comes right before the restricted element

items.hr: Il ne travaille que le matin.
items.en: He only works in the morning.
items.note: here the restriction falls on the time

items.hr: Elle n'a mangé qu'une salade.
items.en: She only ate a salad.
items.note: que becomes qu' before a vowel

items.hr: Nous ne restons que deux jours à Marseille.
items.en: We are only staying two days in Marseille.
items.note: in the present, que follows the conjugated verb

items.hr: Je ne bois que de l'eau le soir.
items.en: I only drink water in the evening.
items.note: no change to de l', unlike after a real negation

activities.title: Seulement, pas moins
activities.intro: Practise recognising and building sentences of restriction.

questions.prompt: What does 'Je ne mange que des légumes' mean?
options: I do not eat vegetables any more.
questions.explanation: This structure restricts instead of denying, so the speaker does eat, but nothing except vegetables.

questions.prompt: Which sentence correctly says 'He only speaks to his sister'?
options: Il ne qu'à parle sa sœur.
Il ne parle qu'à sa sœur.
Il n'parle que à sa sœur.
Il ne parle à que sa sœur.
questions.answer: Il ne parle qu'à sa sœur.
questions.explanation: 'ne' stays before the verb and the second element goes directly before the restricted group, becoming 'qu'' in front of the vowel of 'à'.

questions.prompt: Complete so the sentence means there are only three seats left: 'Il ne reste ___ trois places dans le train.'
questions.explanation: The restricting word is placed immediately before 'trois places', the element being limited.

questions.prompt: Complete with the elided form used before a vowel: 'Je n'ai ___ un petit problème.'
questions.explanation: Before a word starting with a vowel the final -e drops and an apostrophe is written.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I only drink coffee in the morning.'
questions.explanation: The restriction sits before 'du café', and the partitive article 'du' is kept because this is not a true negation.

activities.title: Un budget serré
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Money and time are short this month, so every answer you give limits something.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: On sort ce week-end ? Il y a un concert samedi.
lines.en: Shall we go out this weekend? There is a concert on Saturday.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je veux bien, mais je n'ai que vingt euros jusqu'à vendredi.
lines.en: I would like to, but I only have twenty euros until Friday.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ce n'est pas grave, l'entrée est gratuite avant vingt heures.
lines.en: It does not matter, entry is free before eight.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Alors d'accord, mais je ne reste qu'une heure.
lines.en: All right then, but I am only staying one hour.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Une heure seulement ? Tu travailles dimanche ?
lines.en: Only one hour? Are you working on Sunday?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, et je ne dors que cinq heures par nuit en ce moment.
lines.en: Yes, and I am only sleeping five hours a night at the moment.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu bois un verre quand même ?
lines.en: Will you still have a drink?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je ne bois que de l'eau ce soir, merci.
lines.en: I am only drinking water tonight, thanks.

days.title: Depuis, pendant, il y a : situer une action dans le temps
days.objective: Choose correctly between depuis, pendant and il y a to situate an action in time.
days.paretoFocus: These three words answer the everyday questions 'since when', 'for how long' and 'when', and mixing them up is one of the most noticeable learner mistakes.
drills: Say three sentences about your current life with 'depuis' and the present tense.
Say three sentences about finished periods with 'pendant' and the passé composé.
Say three sentences locating past events with 'il y a' and a duration.

items: Which of the three goes with a verb in the present because the action continues?
Turn 'J'habite ici depuis deux ans' into a sentence about a finished stay.
Answer aloud: 'Depuis quand tu apprends le français ?' and 'Tu as commencé il y a combien de temps ?'

activities.title: Depuis, pendant, il y a
activities.intro: 'Depuis' introduces something that started in the past and is still true, so the verb stays in the present. 'Pendant' gives the length of a finished period, usually with the passé composé. 'Il y a' places a past event at a distance from now, and it translates the English 'ago'.

items.hr: J'habite dans ce quartier depuis trois ans.
items.en: I have been living in this area for three years.
items.note: depuis + present: the situation continues

items.hr: Je travaille dans cette entreprise depuis 2019.
items.en: I have worked in this company since 2019.
items.note: depuis also accepts a starting point

items.hr: J'ai travaillé pendant six mois à Lille.
items.en: I worked for six months in Lille.
items.note: pendant + a completed duration

items.hr: Je suis arrivé il y a deux semaines.
items.en: I arrived two weeks ago.
items.note: il y a + duration = ago, always a finished action

items.hr: Pendant les vacances, je lis beaucoup.
items.en: During the holidays, I read a lot.
items.note: pendant can also mean 'during' with a period noun

activities.title: Quand ? Combien de temps ?
activities.intro: Practise picking the right time marker for a continuing situation, a finished period or a past point.

questions.prompt: Which sentence means 'I have been waiting for twenty minutes and I am still waiting'?
options: J'ai attendu il y a vingt minutes.
J'attends il y a vingt minutes.
questions.explanation: A situation that started earlier and continues now takes 'depuis' with the present tense.

questions.prompt: Which word places a past event at a distance from now, like the English 'ago'?
questions.explanation: 'il y a' followed by a duration counts backwards from the present moment to a finished event.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Elle a vécu au Canada ___ cinq ans, puis elle est rentrée en France.'
questions.explanation: The stay is over and its total length is given, which calls for 'pendant' with the passé composé.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Nous nous connaissons ___ l'école primaire.' (since primary school)
questions.explanation: The friendship started at that point and still lasts, so the marker of a continuing situation is used with the present.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I finished my studies three years ago.'
questions.explanation: 'il y a' plus a duration comes after the verb group and expresses how long ago the finished action happened.

activities.title: Depuis quand tu es ici ?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A neighbour asks about your history in the city and you answer using the three time markers.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vous habitez ici depuis longtemps ?
lines.en: Have you been living here long?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: J'habite ici depuis six mois, mais je connais la ville depuis l'enfance.
lines.en: I have been living here for six months, but I have known the city since childhood.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ah bon ? Vous êtes déjà venu avant ?
lines.en: Really? Have you been here before?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, j'ai étudié ici pendant deux ans, il y a dix ans.
lines.en: Yes, I studied here for two years, ten years ago.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et le quartier a beaucoup changé ?
lines.en: And has the area changed a lot?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Beaucoup. Le marché a fermé il y a trois ans.
lines.en: A lot. The market closed three years ago.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: C'est vrai. Vous travaillez dans le centre ?
lines.en: That is true. Do you work in the centre?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, depuis janvier. Avant, j'ai travaillé pendant un an à la campagne.
lines.en: Yes, since January. Before that, I worked for a year in the countryside.

days.title: Les indéfinis : quelqu'un, quelque chose, chaque, plusieurs
days.objective: Talk about unspecified people, things and quantities using the common French indefinites.
days.paretoFocus: You constantly need to mention a person or a thing you cannot or do not want to name, and these four words cover nearly all of those cases.
drills: Describe your morning using 'quelqu'un' and 'quelque chose' at least twice each.
Say five sentences with 'chaque' and check that the noun after it stays singular.
Compare 'plusieurs' and 'quelques' in three sentences about your week.

items: How do you add an adjective to 'quelque chose'? Give two examples.
Say three sentences with 'chaque' about your daily routine.
Turn 'j'ai une idée' into a sentence with an indefinite pronoun as the subject.

activities.title: Quelqu'un, quelque chose, chaque, plusieurs
activities.intro: 'Quelqu'un' refers to an unidentified person and 'quelque chose' to an unidentified thing. Both take 'de' before an adjective and 'à' before an infinitive. 'Chaque' is a determiner and is always followed by a singular noun, while 'plusieurs' means more than two and is followed by a plural noun with no article.

items.hr: Quelqu'un a laissé un message pour toi.
items.en: Someone left a message for you.
items.note: quelqu'un is always singular

items.hr: J'ai quelque chose à te dire.
items.en: I have something to tell you.
items.note: quelque chose + à + infinitive

items.hr: Chaque étudiant reçoit un badge à l'entrée.
items.en: Each student gets a badge at the entrance.
items.note: chaque + singular noun, never chaques

items.hr: Il y a plusieurs solutions à ce problème.
items.en: There are several solutions to this problem.
items.note: plusieurs + plural noun, no article

items.hr: Tu veux quelque chose de chaud ?
items.en: Do you want something hot?
items.note: quelque chose de + masculine adjective

activities.title: Une personne, une chose
activities.intro: Practise choosing the right indefinite word and the right form of the noun after it.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct word: '___ chambre a une salle de bains privée.'
questions.explanation: 'Chaque' is invariable and goes with a singular noun, which fits 'chambre' followed by the singular verb 'a'.

questions.prompt: Which form correctly says 'something interesting'?
questions.explanation: An adjective after 'quelque chose' needs 'de' and stays in the masculine singular form.

questions.prompt: Complete with a word for an unnamed person: '___ m'a téléphoné pendant la réunion.'
questions.explanation: The subject is a person whose identity is unknown, and the pronoun used for that is singular, so the verb stays in the third person singular.

questions.prompt: Complete with a word meaning 'several': 'J'ai visité ___ appartements avant de choisir.'
questions.explanation: 'plusieurs' expresses an indefinite number greater than two and is followed directly by the plural noun.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Each room has a big window.'
questions.explanation: 'Chaque' keeps the noun and the verb in the singular, and the adjective 'grande' agrees with the feminine noun 'fenêtre'.

activities.title: Il y a quelqu'un ?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are reporting what happened while your colleague was out, using indefinites in each answer.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Il s'est passé quelque chose pendant mon absence ?
lines.en: Did anything happen while I was away?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, quelqu'un est venu te voir vers onze heures.
lines.en: Yes, someone came to see you around eleven.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Il a laissé un nom ?
lines.en: Did he leave a name?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, mais il a laissé quelque chose d'important sur ton bureau.
lines.en: No, but he left something important on your desk.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: D'accord. Et les dossiers, ils sont prêts ?
lines.en: All right. And the files, are they ready?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Plusieurs dossiers sont prêts, mais chaque client doit encore signer.
lines.en: Several files are ready, but each client still has to sign.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as besoin de quelque chose ?
lines.en: Do you need anything?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, quelqu'un pour m'aider demain matin.
lines.en: Yes, someone to help me tomorrow morning.

days.title: Verbe + infinitif : commencer à, finir de, essayer de, oublier de
days.objective: Link two verbs correctly by choosing between à, de and no preposition at all.
days.paretoFocus: French speakers chain verbs constantly, and the preposition is fixed by the first verb, so learning the pattern per verb unlocks long, natural sentences.
drills: Say five sentences that start with 'J'essaie de...' about your habits.
Describe your day with 'je commence à' and 'je finis de' three times each.
Write down five verbs you use often and note whether they take à, de or nothing.

items: Which of these verbs takes 'à' and which take 'de'? Say one sentence for each.
Recall three verbs that take no preposition before the infinitive.
Turn three of your own sentences into the passé composé and keep the preposition.

activities.title: Deux verbes à la suite
activities.intro: When one verb is followed by another, the second is always in the infinitive, but the link word depends on the first verb. Some verbs take 'à', others take 'de', and modal-type verbs such as vouloir, pouvoir and aller take nothing at all. There is no logic to guess from, so each verb is learnt with its preposition.

items.hr: Je commence à comprendre la grammaire.
items.en: I am starting to understand the grammar.
items.note: commencer à, and continuer à follows the same pattern

items.hr: Il a fini de travailler à sept heures.
items.en: He finished working at seven.
items.note: finir de, even in the passé composé

items.hr: J'essaie de parler français tous les jours.
items.en: I try to speak French every day.
items.note: essayer de, and the y stays in j'essaie

items.hr: N'oublie pas d'appeler ta mère.
items.en: Do not forget to call your mother.
items.note: oublier de, and de becomes d' before a vowel

items.hr: Je vais continuer à apprendre cette langue.
items.en: I am going to keep learning this language.
items.note: aller takes no preposition, continuer takes à

activities.title: À, de ou rien ?
activities.intro: Practise choosing the preposition demanded by the first verb.
