questions.prompt: Choose the correct link word: 'Elle a essayé ___ réparer la porte toute seule.'
questions.explanation: The verb essayer always introduces an infinitive with 'de', whatever the tense.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses the structure correctly?
questions.explanation: Commencer takes 'à' before an infinitive, so the other three forms are impossible.

questions.prompt: Complete with the right link word before a vowel: 'J'ai oublié ___ envoyer le message hier soir.'
questions.explanation: Oublier takes 'de', and that word is elided to d' in front of the vowel of envoyer.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Nous finissons ___ manger dans cinq minutes.'
questions.explanation: Finir introduces an infinitive with 'de', so the sentence needs that preposition after the conjugated verb.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I am starting to understand the rule.'
questions.explanation: The conjugated verb keeps its fixed preposition 'à', and the second verb stays in the infinitive.

activities.title: Tu as fini ?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your flatmate checks on your progress and each of your answers chains two verbs together.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as fini de ranger la cuisine ?
lines.en: Have you finished tidying the kitchen?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Presque. J'ai commencé à laver les verres.
lines.en: Almost. I have started washing the glasses.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et le lave-vaisselle ? Il marche de nouveau ?
lines.en: And the dishwasher? Is it working again?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: J'essaie de le réparer, mais ce n'est pas facile.
lines.en: I am trying to repair it, but it is not easy.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: N'oublie pas d'appeler le propriétaire.
lines.en: Do not forget to call the landlord.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je vais l'appeler ce soir, j'ai oublié de le faire hier.
lines.en: I am going to call him tonight, I forgot to do it yesterday.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu continues à travailler tard cette semaine ?
lines.en: Are you still working late this week?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, je finis de travailler à dix-huit heures maintenant.
lines.en: No, I finish work at six now.

days.title: L'obligation et l'interdiction : devoir, il faut, il est interdit de
days.objective: Express personal obligation, general obligation and prohibition in everyday situations.
days.paretoFocus: Rules, instructions and duties come up in every workplace, station and public building, and these three structures cover all of them.
drills: State three things you must do this week with the personal form.
State three general rules of your building or workplace with the impersonal form.
Say three prohibitions you have seen on a sign, using the formal structure.

items: Conjugate the verb of obligation in all six persons out loud.
Say the same rule twice: once personally, once impersonally.
Turn two obligations into prohibitions without changing the verb.

activities.title: Ce qu'on doit faire
activities.intro: 'Devoir' expresses a personal obligation and is conjugated for each person: je dois, tu dois, il doit, nous devons, vous devez, ils doivent. 'Il faut' is impersonal and never changes, so it states a rule that applies to everyone. To forbid something formally, French uses 'il est interdit de' plus an infinitive.

items.hr: Je dois partir avant huit heures.
items.en: I have to leave before eight.
items.note: devoir + infinitive, personal obligation

items.hr: Il faut réserver à l'avance.
items.en: You have to book in advance.
items.note: il faut is impersonal and always singular

items.hr: Il ne faut pas fumer dans le couloir.
items.en: You must not smoke in the corridor.
items.note: the negative of il faut expresses a strong ban

items.hr: Il est interdit de stationner devant l'entrée.
items.en: Parking in front of the entrance is forbidden.
items.note: il est interdit de + infinitive, formal notices

items.hr: Vous devez montrer votre carte d'identité.
items.en: You must show your identity card.
items.note: vous devez, common in official instructions

activities.title: Obligations et interdictions
activities.intro: Practise the forms of devoir and the impersonal structures of obligation and prohibition.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Nous ___ arriver avant midi.'
questions.explanation: The subject is 'nous', and the first person plural of devoir is devons.

questions.prompt: Which sentence forbids something rather than requiring it?
options: Il est interdit de manger dans la salle.
Il faut arriver tôt le matin.
questions.answer: Il est interdit de manger dans la salle.
questions.explanation: 'il est interdit de' bans an action, while the other three state something that has to be done.

questions.prompt: Complete the impersonal obligation: 'Il ___ montrer une pièce d'identité à la réception.'
questions.explanation: The impersonal structure uses the invariable third person form of falloir followed directly by the infinitive.

questions.prompt: Complete with the verb of personal obligation: 'Elle ___ travailler samedi matin.'
questions.explanation: The subject 'elle' takes the third person singular of devoir, which is doit.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'You must not park here.'
questions.explanation: The negation wraps around the impersonal verb, and the infinitive follows straight after 'pas'.

activities.title: Le règlement de l'immeuble
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are a new tenant asking about the building rules and repeating them back.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bienvenue. Il faut lire le règlement avant de vous installer.
lines.en: Welcome. You have to read the rules before you settle in.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: D'accord. Est-ce que je dois signer quelque chose ?
lines.en: All right. Do I have to sign anything?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Oui, vous devez signer l'état des lieux aujourd'hui.
lines.en: Yes, you must sign the inventory today.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Très bien. Et pour les poubelles, il faut faire comment ?
lines.en: Fine. And what do I have to do about the bins?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Il faut les sortir le mardi soir. Il est interdit de les laisser dans le couloir.
lines.en: You have to take them out on Tuesday evening. Leaving them in the corridor is forbidden.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Compris. Il ne faut pas faire de bruit après vingt-deux heures, je suppose ?
lines.en: Understood. You must not make noise after ten in the evening, I suppose?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Exactement. Et il est interdit de fumer dans les parties communes.
lines.en: Exactly. And smoking in the common areas is forbidden.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pas de problème, je ne fume pas. Je dois payer la caution quand ?
lines.en: No problem, I do not smoke. When do I have to pay the deposit?

days.title: L'accord du participe passé avec avoir : le COD placé avant
days.objective: Agree the past participle with a direct object that comes before the verb in the passé composé with avoir.
days.paretoFocus: This agreement appears every time you answer with a pronoun or use a relative clause in the past, which is constant in real conversation and writing.
drills: Answer five questions using 'je l'ai...' and change the participle each time.
Build three sentences with 'que j'ai...' about objects you bought or read.
Say five sentences where the object comes after the verb and check that nothing changes.

items: State the rule in one sentence: when does the participle agree with avoir?
Give the four written forms of one participle and say which sound different.
Correct aloud: 'Les photos que j'ai pris sont belles.'

activities.title: Le participe passé et le COD
activities.intro: With 'avoir', the past participle normally does not change. It only agrees in gender and number when the direct object comes before the verb, which happens with the pronouns le, la, les, nous, vous, with the relative 'que', and in questions with 'quel'. Most of the time the agreement is silent, but it is compulsory in writing and audible with participles like fait or pris.

items.hr: J'ai acheté une robe bleue.
items.en: I bought a blue dress.
items.note: the object follows the verb, so no agreement

items.hr: La robe que j'ai achetée est bleue.
items.en: The dress I bought is blue.
items.note: que refers to a feminine noun placed before, hence achetée

items.hr: Tu as vu mes clés ? Oui, je les ai vues.
items.en: Have you seen my keys? Yes, I have seen them.
items.note: les = feminine plural, so vues

items.hr: Quelles photos as-tu prises ?
items.en: Which photos did you take?
items.note: the question word carries the object before the verb

items.hr: Il nous a appelés hier soir.
items.en: He called us last night.
items.note: nous is the direct object here, masculine plural, so appelés

activities.title: Accorder ou pas ?
activities.intro: Practise deciding whether the participle agrees and which ending to write.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct participle: 'Les lettres que j'ai ___ sont déjà parties.'
questions.explanation: 'que' stands for 'les lettres', a feminine plural object placed before the verb, so the participle takes -es.

questions.prompt: In which sentence does the participle NOT agree?
options: Les valises que j'ai prises sont lourdes.
questions.explanation: Here the direct object comes after the verb, which is the normal order, so the participle stays unchanged.

questions.prompt: Complete with the participle of voir: 'Ma sœur ? Je l'ai ___ hier après-midi.'
questions.explanation: The pronoun l' stands for 'ma sœur', a feminine singular object placed before the verb, so an -e is added.

questions.prompt: Complete with the participle of envoyer: 'Voici les documents que vous m'avez ___ lundi.'
questions.explanation: 'que' replaces 'les documents', masculine plural and placed before the verb, so the participle ends in -s.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I read them this morning.' (them = the letters)
questions.explanation: The pronoun goes before the auxiliary, and because it is a feminine plural direct object the participle becomes lues.

activities.title: Tu les as vues ?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are checking what has been done before a trip, and every answer uses a pronoun with an agreeing participle.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as préparé les valises ?
lines.en: Have you packed the suitcases?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, je les ai préparées hier soir.
lines.en: Yes, I packed them last night.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et les billets, tu les as imprimés ?
lines.en: And the tickets, did you print them?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je les ai imprimés ce matin, ils sont sur la table.
lines.en: I printed them this morning, they are on the table.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: La réservation que tu as faite, c'est pour quelle heure ?
lines.en: The booking you made, what time is it for?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je l'ai faite pour dix heures. Tu as prévenu tes parents ?
lines.en: I made it for ten. Have you told your parents?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Oui, je les ai appelés dimanche.
lines.en: Yes, I called them on Sunday.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Parfait. Les clés ? Je les ai laissées chez la voisine.
lines.en: Perfect. The keys? I left them at the neighbour's.

days.title: Les pronoms possessifs : le mien, le tien, le sien
days.objective: Replace a possessive adjective and its noun with a possessive pronoun to avoid repetition.
days.paretoFocus: Comparing what is yours with what is someone else's is a daily need, and the pronoun keeps your sentences short and natural.
drills: Compare five of your possessions with a friend's, using a pronoun in the second half.
Say the four forms of one pronoun aloud and match each to a noun you know.
Answer 'C'est ton sac ?' five times about different objects.

items: Which word does the pronoun agree with: the owner or the thing owned?
Give the masculine, feminine, and both plural forms of the first person pronoun.
Say two sentences with the plural owner forms and their circumflex accents.

activities.title: Le mien, le tien, le sien
activities.intro: A possessive pronoun replaces a possessive adjective plus its noun, so instead of 'mon sac' you say 'le mien'. It always carries an article and agrees with the thing owned, not with the owner. That is why 'le sien' can mean his or hers depending on context.

items.hr: C'est mon sac ? Non, c'est le mien.
items.en: Is that my bag? No, this one is mine.
items.note: le mien, la mienne, les miens, les miennes

items.hr: Ta voiture est rouge, la mienne est grise.
items.en: Your car is red, mine is grey.
items.note: the pronoun follows the gender of voiture

items.hr: Mes parents habitent ici, et les tiens ?
items.en: My parents live here, and yours?
items.note: les tiens for a masculine plural

items.hr: Il a oublié son manteau, prends le sien.
items.en: He forgot his coat, take his.
items.note: le sien agrees with manteau, not with the owner

items.hr: Notre appartement est petit, le vôtre est grand.
items.en: Our flat is small, yours is big.
items.note: le nôtre and le vôtre take a circumflex accent

activities.title: À qui c'est ?
activities.intro: Practise picking the pronoun that matches the gender and number of the object owned.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct pronoun for 'mine': 'Ta chambre est propre, ___ est en désordre.'
questions.explanation: The object owned is 'chambre', a feminine singular noun, so the pronoun takes the feminine singular form.

questions.prompt: Which pronoun means 'theirs' when only one object is owned?
questions.explanation: Several owners but one single object gives the singular form with the article le or la.

questions.prompt: Complete with a pronoun meaning 'yours' in the informal singular: 'Mes clés sont là, mais où sont ___ ?'
questions.explanation: The object owned is 'les clés', feminine plural, so the pronoun agrees in the feminine plural.

questions.prompt: Complete with a pronoun meaning 'his': 'Ce n'est pas mon parapluie, c'est ___.'
questions.explanation: Parapluie is masculine singular, so the pronoun stays masculine singular even though the owner is a man.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'This suitcase is not mine.'
questions.explanation: The pronoun agrees with 'valise', a feminine singular noun, and keeps its article inside the negation.

activities.title: Ce n'est pas le mien
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Bags and coats got mixed up after a party and you sort out who owns what.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ce manteau noir, c'est le tien ?
lines.en: This black coat, is it yours?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, le mien est plus court et il est gris.
lines.en: No, mine is shorter and it is grey.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et ces gants ? Ils sont à Julie ?
lines.en: And these gloves? Are they Julie's?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, ce sont les siens, elle les cherche depuis une heure.
lines.en: Yes, they are hers, she has been looking for them for an hour.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Il reste deux parapluies. Le nôtre est lequel ?
lines.en: There are two umbrellas left. Which one is ours?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Le nôtre est bleu. L'autre, c'est celui des voisins, donc c'est le leur.
lines.en: Ours is blue. The other one belongs to the neighbours, so it is theirs.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et cette écharpe rouge ?
lines.en: And this red scarf?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ce n'est pas la mienne. Demande à Marc, je crois que c'est la sienne.
lines.en: It is not mine. Ask Marc, I think it is his.

days.title: L'impératif avec les pronoms : donne-le-moi, ne me le dis pas
days.objective: Give and refuse instructions with object pronouns in the right position and order.
days.paretoFocus: Short commands with pronouns are how people actually ask for things at home, at work and in shops, and the word order flips between yes and no.
drills: Ask for five objects on a table using an affirmative command with two pronouns.
Turn each of those commands into the negative and notice the change of order.
Give three instructions to a group using the vous form.

items: In an affirmative command, which pronoun comes first: the direct or the indirect one?
What happens to 'me' and 'te' at the end of an affirmative command?
Say one command twice, affirmative then negative, and compare the order.

activities.title: Donne-le-moi !
activities.intro: In an affirmative command, the pronouns come after the verb, joined by hyphens, with the direct object first and the indirect one second. The forms 'me' and 'te' become 'moi' and 'toi' at the end of the group. In the negative, everything goes back to the normal position before the verb and to the normal order.

items.hr: Donne-le-moi, s'il te plaît.
items.en: Give it to me, please.
items.note: direct object le before indirect moi, both after the verb

items.hr: Ne me le dis pas maintenant.
items.en: Do not tell me now.
items.note: in the negative the pronouns return before the verb

items.hr: Passe-moi le sel.
items.en: Pass me the salt.
items.note: me becomes moi when it ends the group

items.hr: Envoyez-nous les documents avant vendredi.
items.en: Send us the documents before Friday.
items.note: nous keeps the same form in both positions

items.hr: Ne les invite pas ce soir.
items.en: Do not invite them tonight.
items.note: negative command, pronoun before the verb

activities.title: Ordres et refus
activities.intro: Practise the position and the order of pronouns in positive and negative commands.

questions.prompt: Which command correctly says 'Give them to us'?
questions.explanation: In an affirmative command the direct object pronoun comes first and the indirect one second, both attached with hyphens.

questions.prompt: Which negative command is correct?
questions.explanation: In the negative the pronouns go back before the verb in their usual order, indirect before direct, and 'moi' returns to 'me'.

questions.prompt: Complete the affirmative command with the tu form of lire: 'Ce livre est passionnant, ___-le ce week-end !'
questions.explanation: The tu form of the imperative of lire is 'lis', and the pronoun follows it with a hyphen.

questions.prompt: Complete with the form used at the end of a positive command to mean 'to me': 'Téléphone-___ ce soir.'
questions.explanation: When it closes an affirmative command, the weak pronoun me takes its stressed form.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Do not send it to him.' (it = the letter)
questions.explanation: A negative command puts both pronouns before the verb, direct object first when the indirect one is lui, and the negation wraps the whole group.

activities.title: À table
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are at a family dinner asking for things and reacting with commands that contain pronouns.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu veux le pain ?
lines.en: Do you want the bread?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, passe-le-moi, s'il te plaît.
lines.en: Yes, pass it to me, please.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et le sel ? Je le mets où ?
lines.en: And the salt? Where shall I put it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Donne-le à ton frère, il en a besoin.
lines.en: Give it to your brother, he needs it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: J'ai une nouvelle pour toi, mais c'est un secret.
lines.en: I have some news for you, but it is a secret.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Alors ne me le dis pas devant tout le monde.
lines.en: Then do not tell me in front of everybody.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: D'accord. Je t'envoie un message après le dessert ?
lines.en: All right. Shall I send you a message after dessert?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, envoie-le-moi ce soir, mais ne l'écris pas au groupe.
lines.en: Yes, send it to me tonight, but do not write it to the group.

days.title: Les quantités et le partitif : du, de la, un peu de, assez de, trop de
days.objective: Talk about undefined quantities of food and matter, and measure them with expressions of quantity.
days.paretoFocus: Every meal, recipe and shopping conversation needs the partitive, and the switch to 'de' after quantities and negations is a rule you use dozens of times a day.
drills: List what you ate today using the partitive with every item.
Turn five of those sentences into negatives and watch the article change.
Describe a recipe using 'un peu de', 'assez de' and 'trop de' at least once each.

items: Say the four partitive forms and one noun for each.
What replaces the partitive after 'beaucoup' and after 'pas'?
Correct aloud: 'Je ne mange pas du fromage.'

activities.title: Du, de la, un peu de
activities.intro: The partitive article expresses an undefined amount of something you cannot count: du, de la, de l' and des. After any expression of quantity such as un peu, beaucoup, assez or trop, the article disappears and only 'de' remains. The same happens after a negation.

items.hr: Je voudrais du pain et de la confiture.
items.en: I would like some bread and some jam.
items.note: du before a masculine noun, de la before a feminine one

items.hr: Il ne boit pas de lait le matin.
items.en: He does not drink milk in the morning.
items.note: after a negation, du becomes de

items.hr: Ajoute un peu de sel dans la sauce.
items.en: Add a little salt to the sauce.
items.note: quantity expressions are always followed by de alone

items.hr: Il y a assez de place pour six personnes.
items.en: There is enough room for six people.
items.note: assez de, never assez du

items.hr: Tu as mis trop de sucre dans le gâteau.
items.en: You put too much sugar in the cake.
items.note: trop de works the same way

activities.title: Combien exactement ?
activities.intro: Practise choosing between a partitive article and the reduced form after quantities and negations.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct article: 'Elle mange ___ fromage tous les jours.'
questions.explanation: Fromage is a masculine noun beginning with a consonant, so the partitive form is du.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is correct after a negation?
options: Je ne mange pas du poisson.
Je ne mange pas de poisson.
Je ne mange pas le poisson jamais.
Je ne mange pas des poisson.
questions.answer: Je ne mange pas de poisson.
questions.explanation: In a negative sentence the partitive article is reduced to 'de' in front of the noun.

questions.prompt: Complete after the quantity word: 'Il reste beaucoup ___ travail avant vendredi.'
questions.explanation: Expressions of quantity such as beaucoup are followed by the bare preposition, with no article after it.

questions.prompt: Complete with a partitive article before a vowel: 'Au petit déjeuner, je bois ___ eau et un café.'
questions.explanation: Eau is feminine but starts with a vowel, so the partitive elides to de l'.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'There is too much salt in the soup.'
questions.explanation: The quantity expression 'trop' is followed by 'de' with no article, while the definite article stays in 'la soupe'.

activities.title: On prépare le dîner
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are cooking with a friend and every line talks about quantities of ingredients.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Qu'est-ce qu'il faut acheter pour ce soir ?
lines.en: What do we need to buy for tonight?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il faut du riz, de la crème et un peu de fromage.
lines.en: We need rice, cream and a little cheese.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: On a encore des œufs dans le frigo ?
lines.en: Do we still have eggs in the fridge?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, il n'y a plus d'œufs, mais il reste assez de beurre.
lines.en: No, there are no eggs left, but there is enough butter.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et du vin ? J'en prends une bouteille ?
lines.en: And wine? Shall I get a bottle?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Prends du vin blanc, mais pas trop de sucré, s'il te plaît.
lines.en: Get white wine, but nothing too sweet, please.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: J'ai mis beaucoup de sel dans la soupe, je crois.
lines.en: I think I put a lot of salt in the soup.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Alors ajoute un peu d'eau, il y a trop de sel maintenant.
lines.en: Then add a little water, there is too much salt now.

days.title: Au restaurant : commander, réagir et commenter un repas
days.objective: Order a full meal, ask the server questions and comment on the food politely.
days.paretoFocus: A restaurant meal follows a fixed script, so a dozen memorised phrases let you handle the whole visit without hesitating.
drills: Say the whole script aloud, from asking for a table to asking for the bill.
Prepare three questions for a server about a dish you do not recognise.
Give three polite comments about a meal, one positive, one neutral, one critical.

items: How do you ask for a recommendation without saying you do not understand the menu?
Give two ways to order a drink politely.
Say one polite complaint about a dish that is cold.

activities.title: Commander un repas
activities.intro: A French meal out follows a predictable order: you ask for a table, look at the menu, order a starter, a main and a drink, then ask for the bill. The polite conditional 'je voudrais' or 'je vais prendre' is what people really use to order. Comments on the food usually pair an adjective with a small qualification.

items.hr: Une table pour deux, s'il vous plaît.
items.en: A table for two, please.
items.note: the standard opening on arrival

items.hr: Qu'est-ce que vous me conseillez ?
items.en: What do you recommend?
items.note: conseiller is the usual verb for a recommendation

items.hr: Je vais prendre le plat du jour.
items.en: I will have the dish of the day.
items.note: aller + infinitive is very common when ordering

items.hr: C'était délicieux, mais un peu trop salé.
items.en: It was delicious, but a bit too salty.
items.note: un peu trop softens a criticism

items.hr: L'addition, s'il vous plaît.
items.en: The bill, please.
items.note: l'addition, not la note, in a restaurant

activities.title: Au restaurant
activities.intro: Practise the phrases you need at each stage of a meal out.

questions.prompt: You have finished eating and want to pay. What do you say?
questions.explanation: 'l'addition' is the bill in a restaurant, while 'la carte' is the menu you read at the start.

questions.prompt: The server asks 'Et comme boisson ?'. Which reply fits?
options: Une carafe d'eau, s'il vous plaît.
La table est réservée à mon nom.
questions.answer: Une carafe d'eau, s'il vous plaît.
questions.explanation: The question is about drinks, and a jug of tap water is one of the most common answers in France.

questions.prompt: Complete the polite order with the conditional of vouloir: 'Je ___ prendre le poisson, s'il vous plaît.'
questions.explanation: The conditional of vouloir is the standard polite form for ordering, softer than 'je veux'.

questions.prompt: Complete the compliment with an adjective meaning very good: 'Le dessert était vraiment ___.'
questions.explanation: Both adjectives are masculine singular to agree with 'le dessert' and are the usual way to praise a dish.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I would like a table near the window.'
questions.explanation: The polite conditional opens the request, and 'près de' introduces the location you want.

activities.title: Une table pour deux
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are eating out and you order, ask a question and comment on the meal.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bonsoir, vous avez réservé ?
lines.en: Good evening, do you have a reservation?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, désolé. Une table pour deux, c'est possible ?
lines.en: No, sorry. Is a table for two possible?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bien sûr, installez-vous. Voici la carte.
lines.en: Of course, please sit down. Here is the menu.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Merci. Qu'est-ce que vous me conseillez ce soir ?
lines.en: Thank you. What do you recommend this evening?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Le poisson est très frais, et le plat du jour est un risotto.
lines.en: The fish is very fresh, and the dish of the day is a risotto.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Alors je vais prendre le poisson, avec une carafe d'eau.
lines.en: Then I will have the fish, with a jug of water.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tout s'est bien passé ? Le plat vous a plu ?
lines.en: Was everything all right? Did you like the dish?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'était excellent, un peu trop salé, mais très bon. L'addition, s'il vous plaît.
lines.en: It was excellent, a bit too salty, but very good. The bill, please.

days.title: À la banque et à la poste : les démarches courantes
days.objective: Carry out basic banking and postal transactions and understand the questions you are asked.
days.paretoFocus: Opening an account, withdrawing money and sending a parcel are the first administrative tasks anyone faces abroad, and each one has a fixed vocabulary.
drills: Say aloud what you would need to open an account, in three sentences.
Ask three questions about the price and the speed of sending a parcel.
Explain a problem with a card in two sentences, calmly and politely.

items: Name five items of banking vocabulary and use each in a sentence.
How do you ask how long a parcel will take to arrive?
Say two sentences you would hear from a clerk at a counter.

activities.title: Démarches à la banque et à la poste
activities.intro: Counter transactions in French use a small, very stable set of words: un compte, une carte, un virement, un distributeur, un timbre, un colis, un guichet. Requests are usually made with 'je voudrais' plus an infinitive. The clerk will normally ask for identification and for the exact service you want.

items.hr: Je voudrais ouvrir un compte.
items.en: I would like to open an account.
items.note: un compte courant is a current account

items.hr: Où est le distributeur le plus proche ?
items.en: Where is the nearest cash machine?
items.note: un distributeur automatique de billets

items.hr: Je dois retirer de l'argent avant midi.
items.en: I need to withdraw some money before noon.
items.note: retirer for withdrawing, déposer for depositing

items.hr: Combien coûte un timbre pour l'Espagne ?
items.en: How much is a stamp for Spain?
items.note: un timbre for a stamp, une enveloppe for an envelope

items.hr: Je voudrais envoyer ce colis en recommandé.
items.en: I would like to send this parcel by registered post.
items.note: en recommandé gives proof of delivery

activities.title: Au guichet
activities.intro: Practise the vocabulary and the standard requests used at a bank or post office counter.

questions.prompt: You want to send a parcel to another country. Where do you go?
questions.explanation: Parcels and stamps are handled at the post office, while accounts and cards are handled at the bank.

questions.prompt: Which word means a cash machine in the street?
questions.explanation: 'un distributeur' dispenses banknotes, whereas 'un guichet' is a staffed counter and 'un virement' is a bank transfer.

questions.prompt: Complete with the verb meaning to open: 'Je voudrais ___ un compte dans votre banque.'
questions.explanation: After 'je voudrais' the second verb stays in the infinitive, and the fixed collocation for an account is this verb.

questions.prompt: Complete with the small item you stick on a letter: 'Pour envoyer cette lettre, il me faut un ___.'
questions.explanation: A stamp is 'un timbre', a masculine noun, which matches the article 'un' in the sentence.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I would like to withdraw two hundred euros.'
questions.explanation: The polite conditional is followed by the infinitive, and 'cent' takes an -s in 'deux cents' when no other number follows.

activities.title: Au guichet de la poste
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You need to send a parcel and buy stamps, and you ask about prices and delivery times.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bonjour, qu'est-ce que je peux faire pour vous ?
lines.en: Hello, what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bonjour, je voudrais envoyer ce colis en Italie.
lines.en: Hello, I would like to send this parcel to Italy.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Très bien. En tarif normal ou en recommandé ?
lines.en: Very good. Standard rate or registered?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: En recommandé. Il arrive en combien de jours ?
lines.en: Registered. How many days will it take to arrive?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Comptez trois ou quatre jours. Ça fait quatorze euros.
lines.en: Count on three or four days. That comes to fourteen euros.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: D'accord. Je voudrais aussi cinq timbres pour la France.
lines.en: All right. I would also like five stamps for France.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Voilà. Vous payez en carte ou en espèces ?
lines.en: Here you are. Are you paying by card or in cash?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: En carte. Est-ce qu'il y a un distributeur près d'ici ?
lines.en: By card. Is there a cash machine near here?

days.title: Louer un logement : visiter et poser les bonnes questions
days.objective: Visit a flat, understand a rental advertisement and ask the questions that matter before signing.
days.paretoFocus: Renting is one of the highest stakes conversations you will have in French, and a handful of precise terms protects you from expensive misunderstandings.
drills: Describe your ideal flat in five sentences with size, floor and price.
Prepare five questions to ask during a visit, about charges, heating and the deposit.
Read a short advert aloud and explain in English what each abbreviation means to you.

items: What is the difference between le loyer and la caution?
Ask two questions about what is included in the monthly price.
Say when you would be available to move in, using a date.

activities.title: Visiter un appartement
activities.intro: French rental vocabulary is precise: 'le loyer' is the rent, 'les charges' are the shared building costs, and 'la caution' is the deposit you get back at the end. Flats are described by their number of rooms, so a 'deux-pièces' has a living room and one bedroom. Asking what is included avoids the most common surprise on the first bill.

items.hr: Je cherche un deux-pièces meublé.
items.en: I am looking for a furnished one bedroom flat.
items.note: un studio has no separate bedroom

items.hr: Le loyer est de six cents euros, charges comprises.
items.en: The rent is six hundred euros, bills included.
items.note: charges comprises is often written CC in adverts

items.hr: Est-ce que le chauffage est inclus ?
items.en: Is the heating included?
items.note: a key question before signing

items.hr: Il faut verser une caution d'un mois de loyer.
items.en: You have to pay a deposit of one month's rent.
items.note: la caution is refunded if there is no damage

items.hr: L'appartement est libre à partir du premier septembre.
items.en: The flat is available from the first of September.
items.note: libre here means available, not free of charge

activities.title: Trouver un logement
activities.intro: Practise the key rental terms and the questions to ask at a viewing.

questions.prompt: What does 'charges comprises' tell you about the price?
options: The deposit has already been paid.
The shared building costs are included in the rent.
questions.answer: The shared building costs are included in the rent.
questions.explanation: 'les charges' are the collective costs of the building, and 'comprises' means they are already inside the announced monthly figure.

questions.prompt: Which question asks about the deposit?
options: L'appartement fait combien de mètres carrés ?
Le quartier est calme le soir ?
questions.explanation: 'la caution' is the refundable sum paid before moving in, distinct from the monthly rent.

questions.prompt: Complete with the adjective meaning it already contains furniture: 'L'appartement est ___, il y a un lit et une table.'
questions.explanation: The adjective agrees with the masculine singular noun 'appartement' and describes a flat that comes with furniture.

questions.prompt: Complete with the word for the monthly sum you pay the owner: 'Le ___ est de sept cents euros par mois.'
questions.explanation: 'le loyer' is the recurring rent, unlike the one off deposit paid at the start.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Is the flat available in September?'
questions.explanation: 'Est-ce que' turns the statement into a question without changing the word order, and 'libre' means available here.

activities.title: La visite de l'appartement
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are visiting a flat and asking the owner the questions that decide whether you take it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Voilà le salon, et la chambre est juste à côté.
lines.en: This is the living room, and the bedroom is right next door.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est clair, j'aime bien. Le loyer est de combien ?
lines.en: It is bright, I like it. How much is the rent?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Six cent cinquante euros, charges comprises.
lines.en: Six hundred and fifty euros, bills included.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Est-ce que le chauffage et l'eau sont inclus aussi ?
lines.en: Are the heating and the water included as well?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: L'eau oui, l'électricité non, c'est à votre charge.
lines.en: Water yes, electricity no, that is for you to pay.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: D'accord. Et la caution, c'est un mois ou deux ?
lines.en: All right. And the deposit, is it one month or two?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Un mois de loyer. L'appartement est libre le premier octobre.
lines.en: One month's rent. The flat is available on the first of October.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Parfait, ça me convient. Qu'est-ce qu'il faut comme documents ?
lines.en: Perfect, that suits me. What documents do you need?

days.title: Prendre et déplacer un rendez-vous par téléphone
days.objective: Make, move and cancel an appointment on the phone and handle the standard questions you are asked.
days.paretoFocus: Phone calls remove all visual clues, so having the exact appointment phrases ready is what makes the difference between managing and panicking.
drills: Rehearse the opening of a call: greet, say why you are calling, give your name.
Practise three ways of proposing a time and three ways of refusing one.
Say aloud a full call where you move an existing appointment to another day.

items: Give two polite openings for a phone call to a professional office.
How do you ask for a different date without giving a reason?
Say the phrase you use to confirm and repeat a date and time.

activities.title: Un rendez-vous au téléphone
activities.intro: On the phone you announce your purpose immediately, then negotiate a slot. The core verbs are 'prendre' for making an appointment, 'déplacer' for moving it and 'annuler' for cancelling it. The person on the other end will ask for your name and often for your availability, so prepare those two answers first.

items.hr: Bonjour, je voudrais prendre rendez-vous.
items.en: Hello, I would like to make an appointment.
items.note: prendre rendez-vous, with no article

items.hr: Vous êtes disponible quel jour ?
items.en: Which day are you available?
items.note: disponible is the standard word for free

items.hr: Est-ce que jeudi matin, c'est possible ?
items.en: Is Thursday morning possible?
items.note: a simple way to propose a slot

items.hr: Je dois déplacer mon rendez-vous de mardi.
items.en: I need to move my Tuesday appointment.
items.note: déplacer for changing the date

items.hr: Je suis désolé, je dois annuler.
items.en: I am sorry, I have to cancel.
items.note: annuler cancels without a new date

activities.title: Allô, c'est pour un rendez-vous
activities.intro: Practise the phrases used to make, change and confirm an appointment by phone.

questions.prompt: You cannot come on Tuesday and you want another date. What do you say?
questions.explanation: Moving an existing appointment to another slot is expressed with the verb déplacer.

questions.prompt: The receptionist asks 'C'est à quel nom ?'. What is she asking for?
questions.explanation: The expression 'à quel nom' asks under which name the appointment or booking should be registered.

questions.prompt: Complete with the verb used in the fixed expression for making an appointment: 'Je voudrais ___ rendez-vous avec le docteur Martin.'
questions.explanation: French says 'prendre rendez-vous' with no article between the verb and the noun.

questions.prompt: Complete with the verb meaning to call something off: 'Malheureusement, je dois ___ mon rendez-vous de demain.'
questions.explanation: Annuler removes the appointment altogether, unlike déplacer which moves it to another time.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Are you available on Friday afternoon?'
questions.explanation: A rising intonation question keeps the statement order, and days of the week take no preposition.

activities.title: Je dois déplacer mon rendez-vous
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You call a medical practice to move an appointment you cannot keep.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Cabinet médical, bonjour.
lines.en: Medical practice, good morning.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bonjour, je dois déplacer mon rendez-vous de mardi matin.
lines.en: Hello, I need to move my Tuesday morning appointment.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bien sûr. C'est à quel nom ?
lines.en: Of course. What name is it under?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Au nom de Dubois. J'ai un rendez-vous à neuf heures.
lines.en: Under the name Dubois. I have an appointment at nine.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Je vois. Vous êtes disponible quel jour cette semaine ?
lines.en: I see. Which day are you available this week?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Est-ce que jeudi après-midi, c'est possible ?
lines.en: Is Thursday afternoon possible?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: J'ai une place jeudi à quinze heures trente.
lines.en: I have a slot on Thursday at half past three.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Parfait, je note : jeudi à quinze heures trente. Merci beaucoup.
lines.en: Perfect, I am writing it down: Thursday at half past three. Thank you very much.

days.title: Internet et le téléphone au quotidien
days.objective: You can describe everyday phone and internet actions and explain simply what is not working.
days.paretoFocus: Connection problems and quick phone messages come up almost daily, and the same handful of verbs covers nearly all of them.
drills: Write three sentences about what you did on your phone today, using a different verb each time.
Describe out loud two things that are not working on your phone right now.
Say five short messages you could send a friend to change a meeting time.

items: How do you say you cannot manage to do something, using a verb plus a preposition?
Give three French verbs for handling a phone or an app.
How do you say the signal is bad and you will call back?

activities.title: Se connecter et envoyer
activities.intro: French uses a small set of fixed verbs for phones and the internet, and most of them keep the same preposition every time. Notice that 'arriver à' plus an infinitive is the everyday way to say you cannot manage something, and that messages travel 'par' a channel. Learn these as whole chunks rather than word by word.

items.hr: Je n'arrive pas à me connecter au wifi.
items.en: I cannot manage to connect to the wifi.
items.note: arriver à + infinitif = to manage to do something

items.hr: Mon téléphone n'a plus de batterie, je dois le recharger.
items.en: My phone is out of battery, I have to charge it.
items.note: ne plus de + noun = no more of something

items.hr: Tu peux m'envoyer le mot de passe par message ?
items.en: Can you send me the password by text?
items.note: envoyer quelque chose par message / par mél

items.hr: L'application met du temps à se télécharger.
items.en: The app is taking a while to download.
items.note: mettre du temps à + infinitif = to take a while to

items.hr: Je te rappelle dans cinq minutes, le réseau passe mal.
items.en: I will call you back in five minutes, the signal is bad.
items.note: le réseau passe mal is the normal way to say the signal is weak

activities.title: Problèmes de connexion
activities.intro: Practise the fixed prepositions and the everyday phone vocabulary.

questions.prompt: Je n'arrive pas ___ me connecter au wifi.
questions.explanation: The verb arriver always takes 'à' before an infinitive when it means to manage to do something.

questions.prompt: Which sentence says the phone has run out of battery?
options: Mon téléphone n'a plus de batterie.
questions.answer: Mon téléphone n'a plus de batterie.
questions.explanation: 'Ne... plus de' means there is none left, so the battery is dead. The others talk about silent mode, a new phone and ringing.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Tu peux m'envoyer le code ___ message ?' (using the channel word for 'by')
questions.explanation: The channel of a message is introduced by 'par': par message, par mél, par téléphone.

questions.prompt: Give the French infinitive meaning 'to charge up a phone again'.
questions.explanation: The prefix 're' adds the idea of filling it up again, so charger becomes recharger.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'The app is taking a while to download.'
questions.explanation: Mettre du temps à is a fixed expression, and the download verb stays reflexive in the infinitive.

activities.title: Le wifi ne marche pas
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend calls while you are struggling with your connection, so use the phone and internet verbs from this lesson.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Allô ? Je t'entends très mal.
lines.en: Hello? I can hardly hear you.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Attends, le réseau passe mal ici. Je te rappelle dans deux minutes.
lines.en: Wait, the signal is bad here. I will call you back in two minutes.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: D'accord. Tu as réussi à télécharger l'application ?
lines.en: All right. Did you manage to download the app?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, je n'arrive pas à me connecter au wifi de l'hôtel.
lines.en: No, I cannot manage to connect to the hotel wifi.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as bien le mot de passe ?
lines.en: Do you actually have the password?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pas encore. Tu peux me l'envoyer par message ?
lines.en: Not yet. Can you send it to me by text?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Je te l'envoie tout de suite.
lines.en: I am sending it to you right away.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Merci, mais fais vite: mon téléphone n'a presque plus de batterie.
lines.en: Thanks, but be quick: my phone is almost out of battery.

days.title: Le sport et la forme
days.objective: You can say which sports you do, how often, and how your body feels afterwards.
days.paretoFocus: French splits sports between 'faire de' and 'jouer à', and choosing wrongly is one of the most audible learner mistakes.
drills: List five sports and say for each whether it takes faire de or jouer à.
Write three sentences saying how often you exercise each week.
Describe two parts of your body that hurt after exercise.

items: Which sports take jouer à, and why?
How do you say you feel much better?
How do you say your legs hurt?

activities.title: Faire du sport et se sentir bien
activities.intro: Individual activities use 'faire de' plus the article, which contracts to du, de la or de l'. Games and team sports with an opponent use 'jouer à', which contracts to au or aux. Body parts that hurt follow 'avoir mal à' with the same contractions.

items.hr: Je fais du vélo trois fois par semaine.
items.en: I cycle three times a week.
items.note: faire de + le = du

items.hr: Ma sœur fait de la natation le samedi matin.
items.en: My sister swims on Saturday mornings.
items.note: feminine activity keeps de la

items.hr: Nous jouons au foot avec des collègues.
items.en: We play football with colleagues.
items.note: jouer à + le = au, used for games

items.hr: Après le sport, je me sens beaucoup mieux.
items.en: After exercise, I feel much better.
items.note: se sentir + adverb, not adjective

items.hr: J'ai mal aux jambes parce que j'ai couru hier.
items.en: My legs hurt because I ran yesterday.
items.note: avoir mal à + les = aux

activities.title: Faire ou jouer
activities.intro: Practise choosing between the two sport constructions and describing how you feel.

questions.prompt: Ma sœur fait ___ natation le samedi.
questions.explanation: Natation is feminine and takes faire de, so de plus la stays 'de la' with no contraction.

questions.prompt: Nous jouons ___ foot le dimanche.
questions.explanation: Team games take jouer à, and à plus le contracts to au.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct present form of 'sentir': 'Après le sport, je me ___ mieux.'
questions.explanation: Sentir follows the partir pattern: je sens, tu sens, il sent.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'J'ai mal ___ jambes après ma course.'
questions.explanation: Jambes is plural, and à plus les contracts to aux.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I cycle three times a week.'
questions.explanation: Frequency is expressed with a number plus fois plus par plus the time unit, with no article after par.

activities.title: Au club de sport
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You meet a neighbour at the gym, so use faire de, jouer à and expressions about how your body feels.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tiens, tu viens souvent ici ?
lines.en: Oh, do you come here often?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Trois fois par semaine. Je fais du vélo et un peu de musculation.
lines.en: Three times a week. I cycle and do a bit of weight training.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Moi, je fais surtout de la natation. Et tu joues au tennis aussi ?
lines.en: I mostly swim. And do you play tennis too?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je joue au tennis le week-end, mais je suis très mauvais.
lines.en: I play tennis at the weekend, but I am very bad.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as l'air fatigué aujourd'hui.
lines.en: You look tired today.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: J'ai mal aux jambes, j'ai couru dix kilomètres hier.
lines.en: My legs hurt, I ran ten kilometres yesterday.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Repose-toi un peu alors.
lines.en: Rest a bit then.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, mais après le sport je me sens toujours beaucoup mieux.
lines.en: Yes, but after exercise I always feel much better.

days.title: La musique, le cinéma et les sorties
days.objective: You can suggest an outing, accept or refuse it, and say what kind of films and music you like.
days.paretoFocus: Two ready-made openers, 'ça te dit de' and 'on pourrait', let you propose anything to anyone without building a complicated sentence.
drills: Write three different invitations for tonight using a different opener each time.
Say out loud two polite refusals with a reason.
Describe the last film you watched in three sentences.

items: How do you ask a friend if they feel like doing something?
Which opener uses a verb form meaning 'we could'?
How do you say a film starts at half past eight in the evening?

activities.title: Proposer une sortie
activities.intro: 'Ça te dit de' plus an infinitive is the standard informal way to ask if someone feels like doing something, and 'de' becomes 'd'' before a vowel. 'On pourrait' plus an infinitive softens a suggestion. 'Avoir envie de' plus an infinitive says what you feel like yourself.

items.hr: Ça te dit d'aller au cinéma ce soir ?
items.en: Do you feel like going to the cinema tonight?
items.note: ça te dit de + infinitif, elided to d' before a vowel

items.hr: On pourrait écouter le nouvel album ensemble.
items.en: We could listen to the new album together.
items.note: nouvel is used before a masculine vowel sound

items.hr: J'ai envie de sortir, je reste toujours à la maison.
items.en: I feel like going out, I always stay at home.
items.note: avoir envie de + infinitif

items.hr: Le film passe à vingt heures trente, on se retrouve devant ?
items.en: The film is on at half past eight, shall we meet outside?
items.note: passer is used for films and shows being on

items.hr: Je préfère les concerts en plein air, l'ambiance est meilleure.
items.en: I prefer open air concerts, the atmosphere is better.
items.note: en plein air = outdoors

activities.title: Une invitation pour ce soir
activities.intro: Practise the invitation openers and the vocabulary of films and concerts.

questions.prompt: Ça te dit ___ aller au concert samedi ?
questions.explanation: The preposition de loses its vowel before another vowel, so it becomes d' in front of aller.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is a soft suggestion rather than a statement?
options: On pourrait dîner en ville avant le film.
Nous dînons en ville tous les jeudis.
J'ai dîné en ville hier soir.
Le restaurant est fermé le lundi.
questions.answer: On pourrait dîner en ville avant le film.
questions.explanation: Pourrait is a conditional form and turns the sentence into a suggestion, while the others simply report facts.

questions.prompt: Complete the fixed phrase for an outdoor concert: 'un concert en plein ___'.
questions.explanation: En plein air is a fixed phrase meaning outdoors, and it never takes an article.

questions.prompt: Complete with the right verb: 'Le film ___ à vingt heures trente.' (is showing)
questions.explanation: For films and programmes, French uses passer, not a verb of starting.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I feel like going out tonight.'
questions.explanation: Avoir envie de is followed by an infinitive, and the time phrase closes the sentence.

activities.title: On sort ce soir ?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague suggests an evening out, so use the invitation openers and say what you prefer.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ça te dit d'aller au cinéma ce soir ?
lines.en: Do you feel like going to the cinema tonight?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bonne idée, j'ai envie de sortir. Il passe quoi ?
lines.en: Good idea, I feel like going out. What is on?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Une comédie française. Le film passe à vingt heures trente.
lines.en: A French comedy. The film is on at half past eight.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Parfait. On pourrait manger un morceau avant, non ?
lines.en: Perfect. We could grab a bite before, right?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: D'accord. Et samedi, il y a un concert dans le parc.
lines.en: All right. And on Saturday there is a concert in the park.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: J'adore ça, je préfère les concerts en plein air.
lines.en: I love that, I prefer open air concerts.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Alors on se retrouve devant le cinéma à vingt heures ?
lines.en: So we meet outside the cinema at eight?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ça marche, à ce soir !
lines.en: Works for me, see you tonight!

days.title: Les fêtes et les traditions
days.objective: You can describe what people generally do on holidays and celebrations using the impersonal 'on'.
days.paretoFocus: 'On' plus a third person singular verb is how French describes customs, and it replaces the heavy English 'people usually' in one short word.
drills: Write three sentences about a holiday in your country, all starting with On.
Say two things people do not do on a public holiday.
List four celebration words with their dates.

items: Which verb ending goes with on?
How do you say people give lily of the valley on the first of May?
What is the French word for a public holiday?

activities.title: Ce qu'on fait les jours de fête
activities.intro: To describe a custom, French uses 'on' with the same verb form as il or elle, even though the meaning is 'people in general'. Reflexive verbs keep 'se' with on, as in 'on se retrouve'. Dates of celebrations use 'le' plus the number, with no preposition.

items.hr: En France, on offre du muguet le premier mai.
items.en: In France, people give lily of the valley on the first of May.
items.note: on takes the same form as il: on offre

items.hr: Le soir du réveillon, on se retrouve en famille.
items.en: On New Year's Eve, people get together with family.
items.note: reflexive with on: on se retrouve

items.hr: Pour un anniversaire, on apporte souvent un petit cadeau.
items.en: For a birthday, people often bring a small present.
items.note: apporter is used for objects you carry

items.hr: Le quatorze juillet, il y a un feu d'artifice dans presque chaque ville.
items.en: On the fourteenth of July, there are fireworks in almost every town.
items.note: dates take le, and only the first of the month uses premier

items.hr: Chez nous, on ne travaille pas les jours fériés.
items.en: Where I live, people do not work on public holidays.
items.note: un jour férié = a public holiday

activities.title: Fêtes et coutumes
activities.intro: Practise talking about customs with on and using celebration vocabulary.

questions.prompt: ___ offre du muguet le premier mai.
questions.explanation: On is the standard subject for a general custom, and it takes the il form of the verb.

questions.prompt: Le soir du réveillon, on ___ en famille.
questions.explanation: Even though on means several people, the verb stays singular and keeps the reflexive pronoun se.

questions.prompt: Write the number 14 in French letters, as it appears in the date of the national holiday.
questions.explanation: Dates use plain cardinal numbers in French, so the fourteenth is simply quatorze.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Les magasins sont fermés les jours ___.' (public holidays)
questions.explanation: Un jour férié is a day when work stops by law, and the adjective agrees in the plural.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'For a birthday, people often bring a small present.'
questions.explanation: Souvent goes straight after the conjugated verb, and petit comes before the noun.

activities.title: Les traditions de chez nous
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A French friend asks about celebrations where you live, so answer with on plus the present tense.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Chez vous, qu'est-ce qu'on fait pour le nouvel an ?
lines.en: Where you live, what do people do for New Year?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: On se retrouve en famille et on mange très tard.
lines.en: People get together with family and eat very late.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et on offre des cadeaux à ce moment-là ?
lines.en: And do people give presents at that time?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, on apporte plutôt un dessert ou une bouteille.
lines.en: No, people rather bring a dessert or a bottle.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ici, le quatorze juillet, il y a un feu d'artifice partout.
lines.en: Here, on the fourteenth of July, there are fireworks everywhere.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est un jour férié ? On ne travaille pas ?
lines.en: Is it a public holiday? People do not work?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Exactement, tout est fermé.
lines.en: Exactly, everything is closed.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Chez nous aussi, on ne travaille pas les jours de fête.
lines.en: Where I live too, people do not work on holidays.

days.title: Chez le coiffeur et les services du quotidien
days.objective: You can say that you have something done for you, at the hairdresser or any other everyday service.
days.paretoFocus: 'Faire' plus an infinitive is the only way French expresses 'have something done', and it unlocks hairdressers, repairs, cleaning and keys in one pattern.
drills: Write three sentences with faire plus an infinitive about services you use.
Say two things you had done for you last month.
Practise asking for a short haircut politely, in two different ways.

items: What form of the second verb follows faire in this pattern?
How do you say you get your hair cut every two months?
How do you say you want your watch repaired?

activities.title: Se faire couper les cheveux
activities.intro: When someone else does the action for you, French uses 'faire' plus an infinitive, and the second verb never changes. If the action affects your own body, add the reflexive pronoun: se faire couper, se faire teindre. Body parts in this pattern take the plain article, not a possessive.

items.hr: Je me fais couper les cheveux tous les deux mois.
items.en: I get my hair cut every two months.
items.note: se faire + infinitif, and les cheveux not mes cheveux

items.hr: Je voudrais faire réparer ma montre.
items.en: I would like to have my watch repaired.
items.note: faire + infinitif for an object

items.hr: Elle s'est fait teindre les cheveux en roux.
items.en: She had her hair dyed red.
items.note: fait stays invariable in this construction

items.hr: Je dois faire nettoyer ce manteau avant l'hiver.
items.en: I have to get this coat cleaned before winter.
items.note: the object comes after the infinitive

items.hr: Vous pouvez me faire une coupe courte, s'il vous plaît ?
items.en: Could you give me a short cut, please?
items.note: une coupe = a haircut

activities.title: Services et rendez-vous
activities.intro: Practise the have-it-done pattern and the vocabulary of everyday services.

questions.prompt: Je me fais ___ les cheveux chaque printemps.
questions.explanation: After faire, the second verb is always an infinitive, never a participle.

questions.prompt: Which sentence means 'I have to have my watch repaired'?
options: Je dois faire réparer ma montre.
questions.answer: Je dois faire réparer ma montre.
questions.explanation: Without faire, the sentence says you do the repair yourself. Faire plus the infinitive puts the work on someone else.

questions.prompt: Complete so that a professional does the work: 'Je dois ___ nettoyer ce manteau.'
questions.explanation: The causative verb is faire, and it sits between the modal verb and the infinitive.

questions.prompt: Give the French word for the person who cuts hair, masculine singular.
questions.explanation: Un coiffeur cuts hair; the feminine form is une coiffeuse.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I get my hair cut every two months.'
questions.explanation: The reflexive pronoun comes before faire, and frequency uses tous les plus a number and a time unit.

activities.title: Un rendez-vous chez le coiffeur
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are at the hairdresser and also need other things done, so use faire plus an infinitive.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bonjour, qu'est-ce qu'on vous fait aujourd'hui ?
lines.en: Hello, what are we doing for you today?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bonjour, je voudrais me faire couper les cheveux, assez court.
lines.en: Hello, I would like to get my hair cut, quite short.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Très bien. On garde la même couleur ?
lines.en: Very good. Are we keeping the same colour?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, je ne veux pas me faire teindre les cheveux.
lines.en: Yes, I do not want to have my hair dyed.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Parfait. Vous revenez dans combien de temps d'habitude ?
lines.en: Perfect. How often do you usually come back?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je me fais couper les cheveux tous les deux mois.
lines.en: I get my hair cut every two months.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Je vous note un rendez-vous en septembre alors.
lines.en: I will book you an appointment in September then.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Merci. Vous savez où je peux faire réparer une montre, par hasard ?
lines.en: Thank you. Do you know where I can get a watch repaired, by any chance?

days.title: Les objets perdus et les petits problèmes
days.objective: You can report a lost or broken item and say what is missing, where and when.
days.paretoFocus: 'Il me manque' is the French way to say something is missing from you, and it works nothing like the English word order, so it needs its own lesson.
drills: Write three sentences about things you have lost or forgotten this year.
Say what is missing from your bag right now, using the missing pattern.
Describe two things that are broken at home and how they broke.

items: What is the subject of the verb in 'il me manque un gant'?
How do you say you left something on the train?
What is the French name for a lost property office?

activities.title: J'ai perdu quelque chose
activities.intro: In French, the thing that is missing is not the subject: you say 'il me manque' plus the object, literally 'it is missing to me'. For losing things, distinguish perdre, oublier and laisser, which are not interchangeable. Broken items use casser, often with the participle as an adjective.

items.hr: J'ai perdu mes clés quelque part dans le bus.
items.en: I lost my keys somewhere on the bus.
items.note: quelque part = somewhere

items.hr: Il me manque un gant, je ne sais pas où il est.
items.en: I am missing one glove, I do not know where it is.
items.note: il me manque + the missing thing

items.hr: J'ai oublié mon parapluie dans le train.
items.en: I forgot my umbrella on the train.
items.note: oublier = to forget, laisser = to leave behind on purpose

items.hr: Le bureau des objets trouvés est ouvert jusqu'à dix-huit heures.
items.en: The lost property office is open until six in the evening.
items.note: French says objets trouvés, not objets perdus

items.hr: Mon sac ne ferme plus, la fermeture est cassée.
items.en: My bag does not close any more, the zip is broken.
items.note: la fermeture = the zip or fastening

activities.title: Au bureau des objets trouvés
activities.intro: Practise reporting losses and using the missing construction correctly.

questions.prompt: ___ manque un gant depuis ce matin.
questions.explanation: This verb needs the impersonal subject il, with the person as an indirect pronoun: il me manque, il te manque.

questions.prompt: J'ai ___ mon parapluie dans le train hier.
questions.explanation: After avoir you need the past participle, and the infinitive cannot follow the auxiliary.

questions.prompt: Complete the name of the office: 'le bureau des objets ___'.
questions.explanation: French names the office after the items that have been found, so the participle of trouver is used in the plural.

questions.prompt: Complete with the feminine form: 'La fermeture de mon sac est ___.' (broken)
questions.explanation: The participle acts as an adjective here and agrees with the feminine noun fermeture.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I lost my keys somewhere on the bus.'
questions.explanation: Quelque part is a fixed two word phrase, and the place follows it with dans.

activities.title: J'ai oublié mon sac
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You report a lost bag at a counter, so use perdre, oublier and the missing construction.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bonjour, je peux vous aider ?
lines.en: Hello, can I help you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bonjour, j'ai oublié mon sac dans le train de dix heures.
lines.en: Hello, I forgot my bag on the ten o'clock train.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Il est comment, votre sac ?
lines.en: What does your bag look like?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il est noir, assez grand, et la fermeture est cassée.
lines.en: It is black, quite big, and the zip is broken.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Il y avait quoi dedans ?
lines.en: What was inside?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Un parapluie et un gant. Il me manque l'autre gant depuis hier.
lines.en: An umbrella and a glove. I have been missing the other glove since yesterday.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Attendez, je regarde dans les objets trouvés.
lines.en: Wait, I will look in the lost property.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Merci beaucoup, j'ai déjà perdu mes clés le mois dernier.
lines.en: Thank you very much, I already lost my keys last month.

days.title: Acheter en ligne : commander, livrer, échanger
days.objective: You can follow an online order and use passive sentences to say what has been sent, delivered or refunded.
days.paretoFocus: Order tracking messages are written almost entirely in the passive, so recognising être plus a participle turns confusing notices into plain information.
drills: Rewrite three active sentences about an order into the passive.
Write two sentences saying when your parcel will be delivered.
Say what you would ask for if a product arrived damaged.

items: Which auxiliary builds the passive, and what agrees with the subject?
How do you say the parcel will be delivered tomorrow?
What are the French words for delivery, exchange and refund?

activities.title: Ma commande est arrivée
activities.intro: The passive is built with the verb être plus a past participle, and the participle agrees with the subject. The tense is shown by être itself: est livré now, sera livré later, a été livré in the past. This is the normal register of shop notices and tracking messages.

items.hr: Ma commande a été envoyée hier soir.
items.en: My order was sent yesterday evening.
items.note: a été + participe passé = past passive

items.hr: Le colis sera livré entre midi et quatorze heures.
items.en: The parcel will be delivered between noon and two.
items.note: sera + participe = future passive

items.hr: Le produit n'est pas conforme, je voudrais l'échanger.
items.en: The product is not as described, I would like to exchange it.
items.note: conforme = matching the description

items.hr: Si l'article ne vous plaît pas, vous êtes remboursé sous quinze jours.
items.en: If you do not like the item, you are refunded within fifteen days.
items.note: sous + duration = within that time

items.hr: La livraison est gratuite à partir de cinquante euros.
items.en: Delivery is free from fifty euros upwards.
items.note: à partir de = starting from

activities.title: Suivre une commande
activities.intro: Practise passive sentences and the vocabulary of online shopping.

questions.prompt: Ma commande a ___ envoyée hier.
questions.explanation: The past passive needs avoir plus the participle of être, so it is a été plus the main participle.

questions.prompt: Which sentence says the parcel will be delivered tomorrow?
options: Le colis a été perdu hier.
questions.explanation: Sera is the future of être, so sera livré is a future passive; the others are past, active or static.

questions.prompt: Complete with the noun: 'La ___ est gratuite à partir de cinquante euros.' (bringing the parcel to your door)
questions.explanation: The noun formed from livrer is la livraison, a feminine noun.

questions.prompt: Complete with an infinitive: 'Je voudrais ___ ce pull contre une autre taille.' (swap it)
questions.explanation: Échanger quelque chose contre quelque chose is the fixed pattern for swapping an item.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'The parcel will be delivered between noon and two.'
questions.explanation: The future of être comes before the participle, and time ranges use entre plus et.

activities.title: Un problème avec ma commande
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You call a shop about an online order, so use passive forms and shopping vocabulary.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Service client, bonjour. C'est à quel sujet ?
lines.en: Customer service, hello. What is it about?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bonjour, ma commande a été envoyée la semaine dernière et je n'ai rien reçu.
lines.en: Hello, my order was sent last week and I have received nothing.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Je vérifie. Le colis sera livré demain entre midi et quatorze heures.
lines.en: I am checking. The parcel will be delivered tomorrow between noon and two.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Très bien. Et si le produit n'est pas conforme ?
lines.en: Very good. And what if the product is not as described?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vous pouvez l'échanger ou vous êtes remboursé sous quinze jours.
lines.en: You can exchange it or you are refunded within fifteen days.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: La livraison du retour est gratuite aussi ?
lines.en: Is the return delivery free as well?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Oui, à partir de cinquante euros d'achat.
lines.en: Yes, from fifty euros of purchase upwards.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Parfait, merci pour ces informations.
lines.en: Perfect, thank you for that information.

days.title: Écrire un mél informel : invitation, remerciement, excuse
days.objective: You can write a short informal message that invites, thanks or apologises, with the right opening and closing.
days.paretoFocus: Informal French messages follow a fixed skeleton, so learning the four standard lines lets you write any short message in a minute.
drills: Write a four line invitation to a friend for a weekend meal.
Write a two line thank you message for a favour.
Write a short apology for missing an event, with a reason.

items: Which opening word suits a friend, and which one suits a stranger?
How do you say you are writing in order to invite someone?
Give two informal ways to close a message.

activities.title: Un petit mél à un ami
activities.intro: An informal French message opens with Salut or Coucou plus the first name, never with a formal address. The purpose is announced with 'Je t'écris pour' plus an infinitive. Thanks take 'merci pour' plus a noun or 'merci de' plus an infinitive, and the message closes with a short friendly formula.

items.hr: Salut Léa, j'espère que tu vas bien.
items.en: Hi Lea, I hope you are well.
items.note: informal opening plus a quick friendly line

items.hr: Je t'écris pour t'inviter à mon anniversaire samedi.
items.en: I am writing to invite you to my birthday on Saturday.
items.note: je t'écris pour + infinitif

items.hr: Merci beaucoup pour ton aide, c'était vraiment gentil.
items.en: Thank you very much for your help, it was really kind.
items.note: merci pour + nom

items.hr: Je suis désolé, je ne pourrai pas venir dimanche.
items.en: I am sorry, I will not be able to come on Sunday.
items.note: désolé agrees: désolée if you are female

items.hr: J'attends ta réponse. À bientôt et bises, Marc.
items.en: I am waiting for your reply. See you soon and love, Marc.
items.note: bises is a common informal sign off between friends

activities.title: Le squelette du message
activities.intro: Practise the standard lines of a short informal message.

questions.prompt: Which opening suits a message to a close friend?
questions.explanation: Salut plus a first name is informal; the other three belong to formal or commercial letters.

questions.prompt: Je t'écris ___ t'inviter à mon anniversaire.
questions.explanation: Purpose before an infinitive is always expressed with pour.

questions.prompt: Complete the informal closing: 'À ___ et bises !' (see you soon)
questions.explanation: À bientôt is the standard friendly closing, with the circumflex on the o.

questions.prompt: Complete with the masculine form: 'Je suis ___, je ne pourrai pas venir.' (sorry)
questions.explanation: Désolé is an adjective and takes an extra e when the writer is female.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I am writing to invite you to my birthday.'
questions.explanation: Inviter takes à before the event, and both pronouns elide before the vowel.

activities.title: On se répond par mél
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend reacts to your written message, so use the invitation, thanks and apology formulas out loud.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: J'ai reçu ton message, mais je n'ai pas tout compris.
lines.en: I got your message, but I did not understand everything.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je t'écris pour t'inviter à mon anniversaire samedi soir.
lines.en: I am writing to invite you to my birthday on Saturday evening.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ah super ! Et il faut apporter quelque chose ?
lines.en: Oh great! And should I bring something?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Rien du tout. Merci beaucoup pour ton aide la semaine dernière, d'ailleurs.
lines.en: Nothing at all. Thank you very much for your help last week, by the way.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: De rien. Tu invites aussi Marc ?
lines.en: You are welcome. Are you inviting Marc too?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, mais il m'a écrit qu'il est désolé, il ne pourra pas venir.
lines.en: Yes, but he wrote to me that he is sorry, he will not be able to come.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dommage. Je te confirme demain, alors.
lines.en: Too bad. I will confirm to you tomorrow, then.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Parfait, j'attends ta réponse. À bientôt !
lines.en: Perfect, I am waiting for your reply. See you soon!

days.title: Raconter son week-end : enchaîner les événements
days.objective: You can tell what you did over a weekend and place each event before or after another one.
days.paretoFocus: 'Avant de' takes an infinitive but 'après' takes a noun, and getting this pair right makes any story sound organised instead of listed.
drills: Write three sentences about yesterday using avant de plus an infinitive.
Write three sentences about yesterday using après plus a noun.
Tell your last weekend out loud in six sentences in order.

items: What follows avant de, and what follows après?
Where does the reflexive pronoun go after avant de?
Give three words that order events in a story.

activities.title: Avant, après, et le reste
activities.intro: 'Avant de' is followed by an infinitive and never by a conjugated verb. 'Après' in everyday speech is followed by a noun, so you say après le déjeuner rather than a verb phrase. With reflexive verbs, the pronoun matches the subject and stays in front of the infinitive.

items.hr: Samedi matin, avant de partir, j'ai pris un café tranquillement.
items.en: On Saturday morning, before leaving, I had a coffee calmly.
items.note: avant de + infinitif

items.hr: Après le déjeuner, nous sommes allés au marché.
items.en: After lunch, we went to the market.
items.note: après + nom, with the article

items.hr: J'ai d'abord fait le ménage, puis j'ai retrouvé des amis.
items.en: I first did the housework, then I met up with friends.
items.note: d'abord and puis order the events

items.hr: Le soir, avant de me coucher, j'ai regardé un film.
items.en: In the evening, before going to bed, I watched a film.
items.note: reflexive pronoun agrees with the subject: avant de me coucher

items.hr: Dimanche, on a fini la journée par une longue promenade.
items.en: On Sunday, we ended the day with a long walk.
items.note: finir par + nom = to end with

activities.title: Mettre le week-end en ordre
activities.intro: Practise placing events in order with the right structure after each time word.

questions.prompt: Avant ___ partir, j'ai pris un café.
questions.explanation: Avant needs de before an infinitive; avant alone is only used with a noun.

questions.prompt: Which sequence after 'après' is correct in everyday French?
questions.explanation: Après is followed directly by a noun with its article, with no linking preposition.

questions.prompt: Complete the reflexive infinitive: 'Avant de me ___, j'ai regardé un film.' (go to bed)
questions.explanation: The verb stays in the infinitive after avant de, and only the pronoun changes with the subject.

questions.prompt: Complete with the ordering word meaning 'first of all': 'J'ai ___ fait le ménage, puis j'ai vu des amis.'
questions.explanation: D'abord opens a sequence and is usually paired later with puis or ensuite.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'After lunch, we went to the market.'
questions.explanation: Aller takes être in the past, and the participle agrees with the plural subject.

activities.title: Alors, ce week-end ?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague asks about your weekend, so order your story with avant de, après and sequence words.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Alors, tu as fait quoi ce week-end ?
lines.en: So, what did you do this weekend?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Samedi, j'ai d'abord fait le ménage, puis j'ai retrouvé des amis.
lines.en: On Saturday, I first did the housework, then I met up with friends.

lines.speaker: Partner
