lines.hr: Vous êtes sortis en ville ?
lines.en: Did you go out into town?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, après le déjeuner, nous sommes allés au marché.
lines.en: Yes, after lunch, we went to the market.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et le soir ?
lines.en: And in the evening?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Avant de me coucher, j'ai regardé un vieux film.
lines.en: Before going to bed, I watched an old film.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et dimanche, tu es resté à la maison ?
lines.en: And on Sunday, did you stay at home?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, on a fini la journée par une longue promenade.
lines.en: No, we ended the day with a long walk.

days.title: Décrire un lieu : la ville, le quartier, la campagne
days.objective: You can describe a neighbourhood or a village and locate places in relation to each other.
days.paretoFocus: The relative word 'où' links a place to what happens there, which lets you replace two short sentences with one natural description.
drills: Write three sentences about your area, each containing où.
Locate four places near you using compass points or position phrases.
Describe the countryside and a city centre in three contrasting sentences.

items: Which relative word introduces a place, and how is it different from que?
Which verb means 'to be located'?
How do you say a village is surrounded by fields?

activities.title: Le quartier où j'habite
activities.intro: The relative word 'où' links a noun of place to a whole clause, and unlike qui or que it never changes form. 'Se trouver' is the standard verb for saying where something is located. Position is given with compass points or with fixed phrases like en bas de and au bout de.

items.hr: J'habite dans un quartier où il y a beaucoup de petits commerces.
items.en: I live in a neighbourhood where there are a lot of small shops.
items.note: où refers to a place, never to a person

items.hr: La gare se trouve au nord de la ville.
items.en: The station is located in the north of the town.
items.note: se trouver = to be located

items.hr: C'est un village calme, entouré de champs et de forêts.
items.en: It is a quiet village, surrounded by fields and forests.
items.note: entouré de + nom, no article after de here

items.hr: En bas de la rue, il y a une place avec une fontaine.
items.en: At the bottom of the street, there is a square with a fountain.
items.note: en bas de, au bout de, en face de

items.hr: À la campagne, tout est plus loin, mais l'air est meilleur.
items.en: In the countryside, everything is further away, but the air is better.
items.note: à la campagne, en ville, en banlieue

activities.title: Situer et décrire
activities.intro: Practise the place relative and the vocabulary of describing an area.

questions.prompt: C'est un quartier ___ il y a beaucoup de commerces.
questions.explanation: The clause says what happens in that place, so the place relative où is needed, not an object or subject relative.

questions.prompt: La gare se ___ au nord de la ville.
questions.explanation: The reflexive verb is conjugated in the present with the singular subject la gare.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Le village est ___ de champs.' (has fields all around it)
questions.explanation: Entouré de plus a plain noun says what surrounds the place, and it agrees with the masculine village.

questions.prompt: Complete the position phrase: 'En ___ de la rue, il y a une place.' (at the lower end)
questions.explanation: En bas de is the fixed opposite of en haut de and always keeps de before the noun.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'In the countryside, everything is further away.'
questions.explanation: À la campagne is fixed, and plus loin is the comparative of the adverb loin.

activities.title: Tu habites où exactement ?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A new colleague asks about your area, so describe it using où, se trouver and position phrases.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu habites dans quel quartier ?
lines.en: Which neighbourhood do you live in?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dans un quartier tranquille où il y a beaucoup de petits commerces.
lines.en: In a quiet neighbourhood where there are a lot of small shops.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: C'est loin de la gare ?
lines.en: Is it far from the station?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, la gare se trouve au nord, à dix minutes à pied.
lines.en: No, the station is located to the north, ten minutes on foot.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Il y a des endroits agréables pour boire un café ?
lines.en: Are there nice places to have a coffee?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, en bas de ma rue, il y a une place avec une fontaine.
lines.en: Yes, at the bottom of my street, there is a square with a fountain.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Moi, je viens d'un petit village.
lines.en: I come from a small village.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: À la campagne, tout est plus loin, mais l'air est meilleur.
lines.en: In the countryside, everything is further away, but the air is better.

days.title: Exprimer ses goûts et ses préférences
days.objective: You can say what appeals to you and highlight what you like using ce qui and ce que.
days.paretoFocus: 'Plaire' reverses the English word order and 'ce qui / ce que' is the standard way to emphasise a taste, so together they lift your speech above aimer alone.
drills: Write three sentences with plaire, changing the person each time.
Write two highlighting sentences that start with ce qui or ce que.
Say three things you prefer, comparing them to something else.

items: In a plaire sentence, who is the subject: the person or the thing?
When do you use ce qui rather than ce que?
Which preposition follows préférer when you compare two things?

activities.title: Ce qui me plaît
activities.intro: With plaire, the thing you like is the subject and the person is an indirect pronoun, so 'ce film me plaît' literally means the film is pleasing to me. To highlight a taste, start with ce qui when the following verb needs a subject, and ce que when it needs an object, then continue with c'est. Préférer compares two things with à.

items.hr: Ce film m'a beaucoup plu.
items.en: I liked that film a lot.
items.note: the participle of plaire is plu and stays invariable

items.hr: Ce que j'aime, c'est marcher le matin.
items.en: What I like is walking in the morning.
items.note: ce que + subject + verb, then c'est

items.hr: Ce qui me plaît ici, c'est le calme.
items.en: What I like here is the quiet.
items.note: ce qui is followed directly by the verb

items.hr: La musique classique ne me plaît pas du tout.
items.en: I do not like classical music at all.
items.note: the negation wraps around plaît, with the pronoun inside

items.hr: Je préfère les séries aux films, elles durent plus longtemps.
items.en: I prefer series to films, they last longer.
items.note: préférer A à B, with à plus les giving aux

activities.title: Goûts et préférences
activities.intro: Practise plaire and the two highlighting words.

questions.prompt: ___ me plaît ici, c'est le calme.
questions.explanation: The verb plaît has no other subject, so the subject form ce qui is required.

questions.prompt: Ce film m'a beaucoup ___.
questions.explanation: After the auxiliary you need the past participle of plaire, which is plu.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Je préfère les séries ___ films.' (to the)
questions.explanation: Préférer compares with à, and à plus les contracts to aux.

questions.prompt: Complete with the present tense: 'La musique classique ne me ___ pas du tout.' (appeal to me)
questions.explanation: The subject is la musique classique, so the verb is third person singular with a circumflex on the i.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'What I like is walking in the morning.'
questions.explanation: Ce que introduces the object clause, and the second half always starts with c'est.

activities.title: Chacun ses goûts
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend compares tastes with you, so reach for plaire and the two highlighting structures you just learned.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as vu le film hier soir ? Il t'a plu ?
lines.en: Did you see the film last night? Did you like it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, ce film m'a beaucoup plu, surtout la fin.
lines.en: Yes, I liked that film a lot, especially the ending.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Moi, je préfère les séries.
lines.en: I prefer series.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je préfère les séries aux films aussi, elles durent plus longtemps.
lines.en: I prefer series to films too, they last longer.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et la musique classique, ça te dit quelque chose ?
lines.en: And classical music, does that appeal to you at all?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Franchement, la musique classique ne me plaît pas du tout.
lines.en: Honestly, I do not like classical music at all.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Qu'est-ce que tu aimes, alors ?
lines.en: What do you like, then?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ce que j'aime, c'est écouter un concert en marchant le matin.
lines.en: What I like is listening to a concert while walking in the morning.

days.title: Comparer deux villes, deux produits, deux habitudes
days.objective: You can compare quantities and say that two things are the same, different or similar.
days.paretoFocus: Comparing amounts needs 'autant de' and 'moins de' with de before the noun, a detail that learners drop constantly and that changes the meaning.
drills: Write three sentences comparing quantities of noise, traffic or work in two places.
Write two sentences with le même que and two with différent de.
Compare two products you own in four sentences.

items: Which small word must come between autant and the noun?
Which preposition follows différent?
How does même agree, and what follows it in a comparison?

activities.title: Le même ou pas le même
activities.intro: When you compare amounts of a noun, French inserts de: autant de bruit, moins de circulation, plus de travail. Sameness uses le même, la même or les mêmes plus que, while difference uses différent de. Se ressembler says two things look or feel alike without naming a criterion.

items.hr: Ce téléphone a les mêmes fonctions que l'autre, mais il coûte moins cher.
items.en: This phone has the same functions as the other one, but it costs less.
items.note: le même / la même / les mêmes + que

items.hr: Il y a autant de bruit ici qu'au centre-ville.
items.en: There is as much noise here as in the town centre.
items.note: autant de + nom + que, and que elides to qu'

items.hr: Ma vie à la campagne est très différente de ma vie en ville.
items.en: My life in the countryside is very different from my life in town.
items.note: différent de, agreeing with the subject

items.hr: Dans ce quartier, il y a moins de circulation que dans le mien.
items.en: In this neighbourhood there is less traffic than in mine.
items.note: moins de + nom, never moins que before a noun

items.hr: Les deux villes se ressemblent beaucoup.
items.en: The two towns are very similar.
items.note: se ressembler is reflexive and plural here

activities.title: Deux choses, une comparaison
activities.intro: Practise comparing quantities and expressing sameness or difference.

questions.prompt: Ce téléphone a les ___ fonctions que l'autre.
questions.explanation: Même is an adjective here and agrees with the plural noun fonctions.

questions.prompt: Il y a autant ___ bruit ici qu'au centre-ville.
questions.explanation: Quantity words are followed by plain de before the noun, with no article.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Ma vie ici est très différente ___ ma vie d'avant.' (from)
questions.explanation: Différent is always followed by de, not by que.

questions.prompt: Complete with the present tense: 'Les deux villes se ___ beaucoup.' (look alike)
questions.explanation: The subject is plural, so the reflexive verb takes the ent ending.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'There is less traffic in this neighbourhood.'
questions.explanation: Moins is followed by de plus the bare noun, and the place phrase comes at the end.

activities.title: Deux villes, deux vies
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend who moved compares her old and new life, so answer with quantity comparisons and sameness words.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Alors, ta nouvelle ville, c'est comment ?
lines.en: So, what is your new town like?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ma vie ici est très différente de ma vie d'avant.
lines.en: My life here is very different from my life before.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: C'est plus calme ?
lines.en: Is it quieter?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pas vraiment, il y a autant de bruit qu'au centre-ville.
lines.en: Not really, there is as much noise as in the town centre.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et la circulation ?
lines.en: And the traffic?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Là, il y a moins de circulation que dans mon ancien quartier.
lines.en: There, there is less traffic than in my old neighbourhood.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Au fond, les deux villes se ressemblent un peu, non ?
lines.en: Deep down, the two towns are a bit similar, right?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, elles ont les mêmes problèmes, mais ici tout coûte moins cher.
lines.en: Yes, they have the same problems, but here everything costs less.

days.title: Le recyclage et les gestes écologiques simples
days.objective: You can talk about sorting waste and describe habits that are increasing or decreasing.
days.paretoFocus: 'De plus en plus' and 'de moins en moins' express any trend in three words, and they work with nouns, verbs and adjectives alike.
drills: Write two sentences with de plus en plus and two with de moins en moins.
List four things you sort or recycle at home.
Say three small habits you could change this month.

items: What extra word do the trend phrases need before a noun?
Which preposition follows essayer before an infinitive?
How do you say you sort your waste every day?

activities.title: Trier et gaspiller moins
activities.intro: 'De plus en plus' and 'de moins en moins' describe a growing or shrinking trend, and they add de before a noun. 'Essayer de' and 'éviter de' both take an infinitive, while éviter takes a plain noun when there is no verb. 'Penser à' plus an infinitive means to remember to do something.

items.hr: Je trie mes déchets: le verre, le papier et le plastique.
items.en: I sort my waste: glass, paper and plastic.
items.note: trier = to sort, les déchets = waste

items.hr: J'essaie d'éviter les sacs en plastique.
items.en: I try to avoid plastic bags.
items.note: essayer de + infinitif, éviter + nom

items.hr: On gaspille de moins en moins d'eau à la maison.
items.en: We waste less and less water at home.
items.note: de moins en moins de + nom

items.hr: De plus en plus de gens vont au travail à vélo.
items.en: More and more people go to work by bike.
items.note: de plus en plus de + nom, at the start of the sentence

items.hr: Pense à éteindre la lumière en sortant.
items.en: Remember to turn off the light on your way out.
items.note: penser à + infinitif = remember to

activities.title: Les petits gestes
activities.intro: Practise trend phrases and the everyday vocabulary of recycling.

questions.prompt: On gaspille ___ d'eau qu'avant.
questions.explanation: The decreasing trend is a fixed three word phrase, and it keeps de before the noun.

questions.prompt: J'essaie ___ éviter les sacs en plastique.
questions.explanation: Essayer takes de before an infinitive, and de elides before the vowel of éviter.

questions.prompt: Complete with the present tense: 'Je ___ mes déchets tous les jours.' (separate them into bins)
questions.explanation: Trier is a regular er verb, so the je form ends in e.

questions.prompt: Complete with an infinitive: 'Pense à ___ la lumière.' (switch it off)
questions.explanation: Éteindre is the verb for switching off a light, and it stays in the infinitive after penser à.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'More and more people go to work by bike.'
questions.explanation: The trend phrase needs de before gens, and transport uses à vélo with no article.

activities.title: On trie tout ça ?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A flatmate asks about recycling at home, so use trend phrases and the verbs of this lesson.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Je mets le carton dans quelle poubelle ?
lines.en: Which bin do I put the cardboard in?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dans la jaune. Je trie mes déchets: le verre, le papier et le plastique.
lines.en: In the yellow one. I sort my waste: glass, paper and plastic.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu prends encore des sacs au supermarché ?
lines.en: Do you still take bags at the supermarket?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, j'essaie d'éviter les sacs en plastique.
lines.en: No, I try to avoid plastic bags.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et l'eau ? On en utilise beaucoup ici.
lines.en: And water? We use a lot of it here.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: On gaspille de moins en moins d'eau depuis l'année dernière.
lines.en: We have been wasting less and less water since last year.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Je devrais aussi venir au bureau autrement.
lines.en: I should also come to the office differently.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: De plus en plus de gens vont au travail à vélo, essaie une semaine.
lines.en: More and more people go to work by bike, try it for a week.

days.title: Donner un conseil et réagir au problème de quelqu'un
days.objective: You can give advice with conditional forms and react kindly when someone tells you about a problem.
days.paretoFocus: 'Tu devrais' and 'à ta place, je' are the two advice formulas French speakers actually use, and they soften what would otherwise sound like an order.
drills: Write three pieces of advice with tu devrais for three different problems.
Write two sentences starting with à ta place.
Say three short reassuring reactions to a friend's bad news.

items: What are the conditional forms of devoir and pouvoir for tu?
Which verb form follows 'à ta place'?
Give two ways to reassure someone in one short sentence.

activities.title: Tu devrais te reposer
activities.intro: Advice uses the conditional, built on the future stem plus the imperfect endings, so devoir gives tu devrais and pouvoir gives tu pourrais. 'À ta place' sets up a hypothetical and is followed by a conditional verb. 'Ce serait mieux de' plus an infinitive suggests a better option without naming anyone.

items.hr: Tu devrais te reposer un peu.
items.en: You should rest a bit.
items.note: devoir in the conditional: je devrais, tu devrais

items.hr: À ta place, je prendrais rendez-vous tout de suite.
items.en: If I were you, I would make an appointment right away.
items.note: à ta place + conditionnel

items.hr: Tu pourrais lui envoyer un message pour expliquer.
items.en: You could send him a message to explain.
items.note: pouvoir in the conditional: tu pourrais

items.hr: Ce serait mieux d'en parler calmement.
items.en: It would be better to talk about it calmly.
items.note: être in the conditional: ce serait

items.hr: Ne t'inquiète pas, ça va s'arranger.
items.en: Do not worry, it will work out.
items.note: s'arranger = to sort itself out

activities.title: Un bon conseil
activities.intro: Practise conditional advice forms and reassuring reactions.

questions.prompt: Tu ___ te reposer un peu, tu as l'air fatigué.
questions.explanation: Advice needs the conditional, formed on the future stem devr with the imperfect ending ais.

questions.prompt: À ta place, je ___ rendez-vous tout de suite.
questions.explanation: After à ta place the situation is imagined, so the verb goes into the conditional.

questions.prompt: Complete with the conditional of être: 'Ce ___ mieux d'en parler calmement.'
questions.explanation: Être has the irregular stem ser, giving ce serait in the conditional.

questions.prompt: Complete with the conditional of pouvoir, tu form: 'Tu ___ lui envoyer un message.'
questions.explanation: Pouvoir has the stem pourr, and the tu ending in the conditional is ais.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Do not worry, it will work out.'
questions.explanation: In a negative command the pronoun stays before the verb, inside ne and pas.

activities.title: Qu'est-ce que je fais ?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend describes a problem at work, so react and advise with conditional forms.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Je dors mal depuis deux semaines, je suis épuisée.
lines.en: I have been sleeping badly for two weeks, I am exhausted.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tu devrais te reposer un peu, tu travailles trop.
lines.en: You should rest a bit, you work too much.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Je sais, mais j'ai un projet à finir.
lines.en: I know, but I have a project to finish.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: À ta place, je prendrais rendez-vous chez le médecin tout de suite.
lines.en: If I were you, I would make a doctor's appointment right away.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et pour mon chef ? Il ne comprendra pas.
lines.en: And what about my boss? He will not understand.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tu pourrais lui envoyer un message pour expliquer la situation.
lines.en: You could send him a message to explain the situation.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu crois vraiment ?
lines.en: Do you really think so?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, ce serait mieux d'en parler calmement. Ne t'inquiète pas, ça va s'arranger.
lines.en: Yes, it would be better to talk about it calmly. Do not worry, it will work out.

days.title: Réviser l'essentiel : les pièges les plus fréquents
days.objective: You can avoid the five confusions that most often break otherwise correct French sentences.
days.paretoFocus: These pairs appear in almost every conversation, so fixing them once removes a large share of the mistakes a listener actually notices.
drills: Write two sentences contrasting the two verbs meaning 'to know'.
Write three sentences, one with each of the three duration words.
Write two sentences that show the difference between carrying an object and accompanying a person.

items: Which verb of knowing goes with a person or a place?
Which duration word is used for a period that is finished?
Which word is an adjective and which is an adverb in the good and well pair?

activities.title: Les pièges les plus fréquents
activities.intro: Connaître goes with people, places and things you are familiar with, while savoir goes with facts and skills. Depuis covers a period still going on, pendant a finished period, and il y a a point in the past. Bon is an adjective, bien is an adverb, and apporter is for objects while amener is for people.

items.hr: Je connais ce quartier, mais je ne sais pas où est la poste.
items.en: I know this neighbourhood, but I do not know where the post office is.
items.note: connaître + nom, savoir + information

items.hr: J'habite ici depuis deux ans, avant j'étais à Lille.
items.en: I have lived here for two years, before that I was in Lille.
items.note: depuis = the situation is still true

items.hr: J'ai travaillé pendant trois heures, puis j'ai arrêté.
items.en: I worked for three hours, then I stopped.
items.note: pendant = a finished period

items.hr: Ce restaurant est bon et on y mange bien.
items.en: This restaurant is good and you eat well there.
items.note: bon describes a noun, bien describes a verb

items.hr: J'apporte le dessert et j'amène mon frère.
items.en: I am bringing the dessert and bringing my brother along.
items.note: apporter for objects, amener for people

activities.title: Ne plus se tromper
activities.intro: Practise choosing correctly between the pairs that cause the most mistakes.

questions.prompt: Je ___ ce quartier depuis longtemps.
questions.explanation: Familiarity with a place takes connaître; savoir would need a fact or a clause after it.

questions.prompt: J'ai travaillé ___ trois heures hier, puis j'ai arrêté.
questions.explanation: The work is over, so the finished period word is needed; depuis would mean it still continues.

questions.prompt: Complete with the adjective: 'Ce gâteau est très ___.' (tasty)
questions.explanation: The word describes the noun gâteau, so the adjective form is required, not the adverb.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'J'___ mon frère à la fête ce soir.' (bring a person with you, present tense)
questions.explanation: People are accompanied with amener, and the je form has a grave accent on the first e of the ending.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I have lived here for two years.'
questions.explanation: French keeps the present tense with depuis when the situation still continues today.

activities.title: Tu es sûr de ton français ?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend corrects your French while you plan an evening, so use each tricky pair correctly.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu connais un bon restaurant dans le coin ?
lines.en: Do you know a good restaurant around here?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je connais ce quartier, mais je ne sais pas si le nouveau est ouvert.
lines.en: I know this neighbourhood, but I do not know if the new one is open.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu habites ici depuis longtemps, non ?
lines.en: You have lived here a long time, right?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: J'habite ici depuis deux ans, avant j'étais à Lille.
lines.en: I have lived here for two years, before that I was in Lille.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as l'air fatigué ce soir.
lines.en: You look tired tonight.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: J'ai travaillé pendant trois heures après le bureau.
lines.en: I worked for three hours after the office.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Alors on se retrouve à vingt heures. Tu viens seul ?
lines.en: So we meet at eight. Are you coming alone?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, j'apporte le dessert et j'amène mon frère, il mange bien partout.
lines.en: No, I am bringing the dessert and bringing my brother, he eats well anywhere.

days.title: L'exclamation : Quel ! Que ! Comme !
days.objective: You can react with genuine feeling using quel plus a noun, and que or comme plus a full clause.
days.paretoFocus: Reacting is half of conversation. Without an exclamation pattern every reaction flattens into c'est bien, and quel is the single most used opener for surprise, praise and complaint alike.
drills: Look around the room and produce four quel exclamations, one for each of the four forms.
Take three ordinary statements and turn each into a que exclamation.
Say the same reaction twice, once with que and once with comme, and notice they mean the same.

items: What are the four forms of quel and what decides which one you use?
What kind of word follows que and comme in an exclamation?
How would you praise a meal, a view and a piece of news, each with a different pattern?

activities.title: Réagir avec force
activities.intro: Quel goes directly in front of a noun and agrees with it: quel, quelle, quels, quelles. Que and comme go in front of a complete clause with a subject and a verb, and they mean the same thing. No article ever appears after quel in an exclamation.

items.hr: Quel beau jardin !
items.en: What a beautiful garden!
items.note: quel plus masculine singular noun, no article

items.hr: Quelle bonne idée !
items.en: What a good idea!
items.note: idée is feminine, so quelle

items.hr: Quelles belles photos !
items.en: What beautiful photos!
items.note: feminine plural: quelles, and the adjective agrees too

items.hr: Que c'est joli !
items.en: How pretty that is!
items.note: que plus subject plus verb, never a bare noun

items.hr: Comme tu chantes bien !
items.en: How well you sing!
items.note: comme works exactly like que and sounds a little warmer

activities.title: S'étonner et admirer
activities.intro: Practise choosing between the noun pattern and the clause pattern.

questions.prompt: ____ belle voiture !
questions.explanation: voiture is feminine singular, so quel takes the form quelle.

questions.prompt: Which exclamation is built on a full clause rather than a noun?
questions.explanation: comme is followed by a subject and a verb; the other three put quel straight in front of a noun.

questions.prompt: Fill the gap with a single word that is not comme: ____ ce film est long !
questions.explanation: que introduces a clause and is interchangeable with comme in exclamations.

questions.prompt: Adjective agreement from your earlier work finishes this off: Quelles ____ vacances ! (beau)
questions.explanation: beau has the irregular feminine belle, and vacances is always plural, giving belles.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'What a good idea!'
questions.explanation: No article stands between quelle and the noun, and the adjective keeps its usual place before idée.

activities.title: Devant les photos de vacances
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend shows you holiday photos and you react to each one with a different exclamation pattern.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Regarde, voilà la maison où nous étions.
lines.en: Look, this is the house where we were staying.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Quelle jolie maison !
lines.en: What a pretty house!

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et là, c'est la vue depuis la terrasse.
lines.en: And there, that is the view from the terrace.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Comme c'est beau ! Vous avez eu de la chance.
lines.en: How beautiful that is! You were lucky.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Il a fait trente-cinq degrés toute la semaine.
lines.en: It was thirty-five degrees all week.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Que c'est chaud ! Quelles belles vacances quand même.
lines.en: How hot that is! What a lovely holiday all the same.

days.title: Si + présent : les hypothèses certaines
days.objective: You can state a real condition and its consequence with si plus the present, followed by a present, a future or an imperative.
days.paretoFocus: This is the condition people actually use all day, for offers, plans and instructions, and it is the base on which every later hypothesis is built.
drills: Make three offers to a friend, each beginning with si tu veux.
Give three pieces of advice built on si plus present plus an imperative.
Turn three of your weekend plans into si plus present plus a simple future.

items: Which three verb forms can follow the si clause?
Which tense is never allowed directly after si in this pattern?
When does si lose its i, and before which words?

activities.title: Si c'est vrai, alors...
activities.intro: For a condition you consider real or likely, si is followed by the present tense. The result clause is a present, a simple future or an imperative, depending on whether you are describing, promising or instructing. Si never takes a future or a conditional straight after it.

items.hr: Si tu veux, on y va.
items.en: If you want, we can go there.
items.note: present plus present: a real and immediate condition

items.hr: Si tu as le temps, appelle-moi.
items.en: If you have time, call me.
items.note: present plus imperative: an instruction

items.hr: S'il fait beau demain, nous irons à la plage.
items.en: If the weather is nice tomorrow, we will go to the beach.
items.note: si becomes s' only before il and ils

items.hr: Si je finis tôt, je passe te voir.
items.en: If I finish early, I will come by and see you.
items.note: spoken French often uses a present for the near future result

items.hr: Si vous ne comprenez pas, posez une question.
items.en: If you do not understand, ask a question.
items.note: the negative sits inside the si clause as usual

activities.title: Conditions réelles
activities.intro: Practise building real conditions and their three possible results.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence about rain.
options: Si il pleut, je reste ici.
Si il pleuvra, je reste ici.
questions.explanation: si contracts to s' before il, and the verb after si stays in the present.

questions.prompt: Si tu as faim, ____ un sandwich.
questions.explanation: An instruction in the result clause takes the imperative: prends.

questions.prompt: Use the present tense: Si vous ____ partir maintenant, c'est possible. (vouloir)
questions.explanation: vouloir gives vous voulez, and the si clause always stays in the present here.

questions.prompt: This one leans on the simple future you built earlier: Si j'ai le temps, je ____ demain. (venir)
questions.explanation: venir has the irregular future stem viendr-, so je viendrai, and the future belongs in the result clause only.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'If you want, we are going there.'
questions.explanation: The condition comes first, and y stands in for the place before the verb.

activities.title: Des projets pour samedi
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Planning Saturday with a friend, you make each plan hinge on a real condition.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu fais quoi samedi ?
lines.en: What are you doing on Saturday?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: S'il fait beau, je vais à la campagne.
lines.en: If the weather is nice, I am going to the countryside.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et s'il pleut ?
lines.en: And if it rains?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Si tu veux, on reste en ville et on va au musée.
lines.en: If you want, we will stay in town and go to the museum.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: D'accord. Je dois travailler le matin.
lines.en: All right. I have to work in the morning.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Si tu finis tôt, envoie-moi un message.
lines.en: If you finish early, send me a message.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et si je finis tard ?
lines.en: And if I finish late?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Si tu finis tard, nous irons dimanche.
lines.en: If you finish late, we will go on Sunday.

days.title: On : les trois valeurs
days.objective: You can recognise and use the three meanings of on: we, people in general, and an unidentified someone.
days.paretoFocus: On is the most frequent subject pronoun in spoken French, and mistaking which of its three meanings is intended is a constant source of misunderstanding.
drills: Write one sentence with on for each of its three meanings and say which is which.
Take three sentences with nous and say them again with on.
Rephrase three sentences where you do not know who did something, using on.

items: Which verb person always follows on, whatever it means?
How does context tell you whether on means we or people in general?
How would you say that your bike was stolen without naming a thief?

activities.title: Un pronom, trois sens
activities.intro: On always takes the third person singular verb, but it carries three different meanings. It replaces nous in everyday speech, it states what people generally do, and it stands in for an agent you cannot or will not name. Only the surrounding sentence tells you which one is meant.

items.hr: On part demain matin.
items.en: We are leaving tomorrow morning.
items.note: value one: on = nous, the everyday spoken form

items.hr: En France, on dîne vers vingt heures.
items.en: In France, people have dinner around eight in the evening.
items.note: value two: people in general, a custom

items.hr: On a sonné à la porte.
items.en: Somebody rang the doorbell.
items.note: value three: an unidentified person

items.hr: On m'a dit que le magasin est fermé.
items.en: I was told that the shop is closed.
items.note: value three again, where English often prefers a passive

items.hr: Quand on est fatigué, on dort mal.
items.en: When you are tired, you sleep badly.
items.note: generic value; the adjective stays singular after on

activities.title: Qui est on ?
activities.intro: Practise reading the meaning of on from its context and using it correctly.

questions.prompt: In 'On a volé mon vélo', on means...
questions.explanation: The speaker does not know the thief, so on stands in for an unidentified agent.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses on to describe what people generally do?
options: On sort ce soir, tu viens ?
Au Japon, on enlève ses chaussures à l'entrée.
questions.answer: Au Japon, on enlève ses chaussures à l'entrée.
questions.explanation: A custom attached to a place is the general value; the others mean we or someone unknown.

questions.prompt: Give the right verb form: On ____ au marché le samedi. (aller)
questions.explanation: on takes the same form as il and elle whatever it means, so aller gives va.

questions.prompt: An object pronoun you already handle slots in before the verb: On ____ a téléphoné hier soir. (to me)
questions.explanation: The indirect object me becomes m' before a vowel and stands in front of the auxiliary.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Somebody rang the doorbell.'
questions.explanation: The unidentified agent becomes on, and the past participle follows the auxiliary a.

activities.title: Au bureau, le matin
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Two colleagues open the office and on appears with all three of its meanings.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: On commence la réunion à neuf heures ?
lines.en: Are we starting the meeting at nine?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, on est tous là sauf Julie.
lines.en: Yes, we are all here except Julie.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: On m'a dit qu'elle arrive plus tard.
lines.en: I was told she is arriving later.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ici, on prend toujours un café avant.
lines.en: Here, people always have a coffee first.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Attends, on frappe à la porte.
lines.en: Wait, someone is knocking at the door.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est elle. Bon, on y va !
lines.en: It is her. Right, let us go!

days.title: La nominalisation : la visite, la réponse, le départ
days.objective: You can turn a common verb into the noun that goes with it and use that noun in signs, notices and short announcements.
days.paretoFocus: Timetables, signs and official notices almost never use verbs, so a learner who only knows partir cannot read le départ, which is the word actually printed.
drills: Write the noun that matches partir, arriver, ouvrir, fermer and répondre.
Turn three sentences with a verb into a noun phrase with de.
Find three notices or labels around you and say them again as a full sentence with a verb.

items: Which noun comes from arriver and which article does it take?
What happens to de before le and before les?
How would you say that the shop opens at nine, using a noun rather than a verb?

activities.title: Du verbe au nom
activities.intro: Many everyday nouns are built straight from a verb, and this is how notices are written. Some drop the ending, like partir giving le départ, and some add one, like arriver giving l'arrivée. The person or thing involved then follows with de.

items.hr: La visite du musée commence à dix heures.
items.en: The tour of the museum starts at ten.
items.note: visiter gives la visite

items.hr: J'attends ta réponse.
items.en: I am waiting for your answer.
items.note: répondre gives la réponse

items.hr: Le départ est à sept heures précises.
items.en: Departure is at seven o'clock sharp.
items.note: partir gives le départ, a masculine noun

items.hr: L'arrivée du train est prévue à midi.
items.en: The train's arrival is scheduled for noon.
items.note: arriver gives l'arrivée, feminine despite the elision

items.hr: L'ouverture du magasin est à neuf heures.
items.en: The shop opens at nine.
items.note: ouvrir gives l'ouverture, and fermer gives la fermeture

activities.title: Lire les panneaux
activities.intro: Practise turning familiar verbs into the nouns that appear on notices.

questions.prompt: Which noun is built from partir?
questions.explanation: partir gives le départ; la partie and le parti are unrelated words.

questions.prompt: Rewrite 'Le train arrive à huit heures' as a noun phrase.
options: L'arrivée du train est à huit heures.
L'arrive du train est à huit heures.
Le arrivage du train est à huit heures.
L'arrivant du train est à huit heures.
questions.answer: L'arrivée du train est à huit heures.
questions.explanation: arriver gives l'arrivée, and the subject of the verb becomes du train after the noun.

questions.prompt: Complete with the noun built from répondre: Je vous remercie de votre ____.
questions.explanation: répondre gives la réponse, and the accent sits on the first e.

questions.prompt: A contraction from your early article work is needed: La visite ____ château dure une heure. (de plus le)
questions.explanation: de plus le always contracts to du before a masculine noun.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'The opening of the shop is at nine.'
questions.explanation: The noun comes first and what it belongs to follows with du.

activities.title: À l'office de tourisme
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You ask about times at a tourist office, where everything is announced with nouns.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bonjour, je peux vous renseigner ?
lines.en: Hello, can I help you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, à quelle heure est la visite du château ?
lines.en: Yes, what time is the tour of the castle?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Le départ est à quatorze heures devant la porte.
lines.en: Departure is at two in the afternoon in front of the gate.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Et l'ouverture du musée, c'est à quelle heure ?
lines.en: And what time does the museum open?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: À dix heures, avec une fermeture à dix-huit heures.
lines.en: At ten, with closing at six in the evening.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Merci, j'attends encore la réponse de mon amie.
lines.en: Thank you, I am still waiting for my friend's answer.

days.title: Féliciter, souhaiter, remercier
days.objective: You can congratulate someone, send good wishes for a birthday or a celebration, and thank someone properly in speech and in writing.
days.paretoFocus: Social life runs on these three moves, and each has a short fixed formula that a learner either knows or visibly lacks at the exact moment it is expected.
drills: Write a one-line congratulation for someone who has passed an exam.
Write three birthday or celebration wishes without repeating a formula.
Thank someone for a present, for an invitation and for help, using a different structure each time.

items: Which preposition follows remercier before a noun?
What do you say to someone celebrating a birthday, and what do you say for a name day?
Which single word congratulates someone on a success?

activities.title: Les formules qui font plaisir
activities.intro: Félicitations congratulates a success, bon anniversaire and bonne fête mark a celebration, and merci thanks. Souhaiter takes a person then the thing wished: je te souhaite une bonne journée. Remercier is followed by de or pour before the reason.

items.hr: Félicitations ! Tu as réussi ton examen.
items.en: Congratulations! You passed your exam.
items.note: féliciter quelqu'un pour or de quelque chose

items.hr: Bon anniversaire ! Je te souhaite une belle journée.
items.en: Happy birthday! I wish you a lovely day.
items.note: souhaiter plus indirect object plus the thing wished

items.hr: Bonne fête ! On pense à toi aujourd'hui.
items.en: Happy name day! We are thinking of you today.
items.note: la fête is the saint's day, not the birthday

items.hr: Merci beaucoup pour ton cadeau, c'est très gentil.
items.en: Thank you very much for your present, that is very kind.
items.note: merci pour plus a concrete thing

items.hr: Je vous remercie de votre invitation.
items.en: Thank you for your invitation.
items.note: remercier is the more formal verb, with de or pour

activities.title: Les bons mots au bon moment
activities.intro: Practise picking the formula that fits the occasion.

questions.prompt: Your friend has just passed her driving test. What do you say?
questions.explanation: Félicitations marks an achievement; bon courage is for an effort still ahead.

questions.prompt: Which is the natural way to thank someone for a present?
questions.explanation: merci is followed by pour or de before the thing, never by a bare noun.

questions.prompt: Complete the birthday wish: Bon ____ ! Tu as vingt-cinq ans aujourd'hui.
questions.explanation: Bon anniversaire is the fixed birthday formula; bonne fête belongs to a name day.

questions.prompt: The compound past comes back for the auxiliary: Il ____ réussi son examen.
questions.explanation: réussir forms its compound past with avoir, so il a réussi.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I wish you a very good day.'
questions.explanation: The person wished comes as a pronoun before the verb, and what is wished follows.

activities.title: Une carte d'anniversaire
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are writing a card with a colleague and you use the congratulation and thanking formulas.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: C'est l'anniversaire de Claire demain.
lines.en: It is Claire's birthday tomorrow.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: On écrit : Bon anniversaire, Claire !
lines.en: We write: Happy birthday, Claire!

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Elle a aussi réussi son concours cette semaine.
lines.en: She also passed her competitive exam this week.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Alors j'ajoute : Félicitations pour ta réussite !
lines.en: Then I will add: Congratulations on your success!

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Parfait. Et pour finir ?
lines.en: Perfect. And to finish?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je te souhaite une belle année et merci pour ton aide.
lines.en: I wish you a wonderful year and thank you for your help.

days.title: Avertir et mettre en garde
days.objective: You can warn someone about a danger and tell them what not to do, using attention à, il ne faut pas and sinon.
days.paretoFocus: Warnings are the sentences you may need fastest and cannot afford to build slowly, and the same three structures cover a wet floor, a hot pan and a missed train.
drills: Warn someone about three physical dangers in your home using attention à.
State three rules of a place you know with il ne faut pas.
Give three pieces of advice where the second half starts with sinon.

items: What kind of word follows attention à, and what follows il ne faut pas?
What does sinon add to a piece of advice?
How do you tell a person to be careful of their bag rather than of a place?

activities.title: Prévenir du danger
activities.intro: Attention à is followed by a noun and points at a danger. Il ne faut pas is followed by an infinitive and states a prohibition that applies to everyone. Sinon introduces the bad outcome you avoid by following the advice.

items.hr: Attention à la marche !
items.en: Mind the step!
items.note: attention à plus a noun

items.hr: Attention, le sol est mouillé.
items.en: Careful, the floor is wet.
items.note: attention alone, then a full clause explaining why

items.hr: Il ne faut pas toucher, c'est très chaud.
items.en: You must not touch it, it is very hot.
items.note: il ne faut pas plus infinitive, impersonal and general

items.hr: Pars maintenant, sinon tu vas rater le train.
items.en: Leave now, otherwise you are going to miss the train.
items.note: sinon = otherwise, introducing the consequence

items.hr: Fais attention à ton sac dans le métro.
items.en: Watch your bag on the underground.
items.note: faire attention à is the personal version of the warning

activities.title: Attention !
activities.intro: Practise warning someone and stating what is not allowed.

questions.prompt: Which sentence tells someone not to do something?
options: Il ne faut pas ouvrir cette porte.
Il ne faut pas de porte.
questions.answer: Il ne faut pas ouvrir cette porte.
questions.explanation: il ne faut pas plus an infinitive is the standard general prohibition.

questions.prompt: Couvre-toi bien, ____ tu vas avoir froid.
questions.explanation: sinon introduces what will happen if the advice is ignored.

questions.prompt: Complete the warning: Fais ____ à la voiture !
questions.explanation: faire attention à is the fixed expression for warning a specific person.

questions.prompt: The command form you drilled earlier is what is missing: ____ doucement ! (faire, tu)
questions.explanation: The tu imperative of faire is fais, with no subject pronoun.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'You must not run here.'
questions.explanation: ne and pas wrap faut, and the second verb stays in the infinitive.

activities.title: Dans la cuisine
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are cooking with someone who is not being careful, and you warn them.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Je prends la casserole, elle est où ?
lines.en: I will take the pan, where is it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Attention à la poignée, elle est brûlante !
lines.en: Careful with the handle, it is boiling hot!

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: D'accord. Et le couteau, je le laisse là ?
lines.en: All right. And the knife, shall I leave it there?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, il ne faut pas le laisser au bord de la table.
lines.en: No, you must not leave it on the edge of the table.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bon, je nettoie le sol après.
lines.en: Fine, I will clean the floor afterwards.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Fais-le tout de suite, sinon quelqu'un va tomber.
lines.en: Do it right away, otherwise someone is going to fall.

days.title: Raconter la vie de quelqu'un
days.objective: You can tell the story of a person's life with naître, mourir, se marier and the vocabulary of la jeunesse, choosing between the compound past and the imperfect.
days.paretoFocus: Introducing a family member, a public figure or yourself in a few past sentences is the most common piece of extended speech at this level, and it needs exactly these verbs.
drills: Say where and when three people in your family were born.
Tell the life of someone you admire in four past sentences.
Describe what someone's youth was like using the imperfect twice.

items: Which auxiliary do naître, mourir and se marier take, and what does that mean for agreement?
Which tense describes what life was generally like, and which one lists the events?
What are the past participles of naître and mourir?

activities.title: Une vie en quelques phrases
activities.intro: Naître, mourir and all reflexive verbs form their compound past with être, so the participle agrees with the subject. Use the compound past for the events of a life and the imperfect for the background: what someone did habitually or what things were like.

items.hr: Elle est née à Lyon en mille neuf cent quatre-vingt-cinq.
items.en: She was born in Lyon in nineteen eighty-five.
items.note: naître gives né, and with être the participle agrees: née

items.hr: Il est mort à l'âge de quatre-vingt-dix ans.
items.en: He died at the age of ninety.
items.note: mourir gives mort, also with être

items.hr: Ils se sont mariés il y a dix ans.
items.en: They got married ten years ago.
items.note: reflexive verbs always take être

items.hr: Pendant sa jeunesse, il travaillait dans une ferme.
items.en: During his youth, he worked on a farm.
items.note: imperfect for the habitual background

items.hr: Elle a déménagé à Paris, puis elle est devenue professeure.
items.en: She moved to Paris, then she became a teacher.
items.note: déménager takes avoir, devenir takes être

activities.title: Naître, vivre, se marier
activities.intro: Practise the life verbs and the choice of past tense.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence about a woman born in 1990.
questions.explanation: naître takes être, and the participle agrees with the feminine subject: née.

questions.prompt: Which verb tells you that two people got married?
questions.explanation: se marier means to get married; the others mean to get up, to remember and to be mistaken.

questions.prompt: Complete with the participle of mourir: Mon grand-père est ____ en 2003.
questions.explanation: mourir has the irregular participle mort, which agrees with the subject after être.

questions.prompt: Here the imperfect from your earlier work is what the sentence needs: Quand il était jeune, il ____ au football. (jouer)
questions.explanation: A repeated activity in the past takes the imperfect: il jouait.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'They got married two years later.'
questions.explanation: The reflexive pronoun sits before the auxiliary être, and the participle agrees in the plural.

activities.title: Un portrait de famille
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Someone asks about a photograph of your grandmother and you tell her life story.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: C'est qui, sur cette photo ?
lines.en: Who is that in this photo?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est ma grand-mère. Elle est née en Bretagne.
lines.en: That is my grandmother. She was born in Brittany.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Elle a vécu là-bas toute sa vie ?
lines.en: Did she live there all her life?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, pendant sa jeunesse elle habitait à la campagne, puis elle est partie en ville.
lines.en: No, during her youth she lived in the countryside, then she left for the city.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et elle s'est mariée jeune ?
lines.en: And did she marry young?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, ils se sont mariés à vingt ans. Mon grand-père est mort en 2010.
lines.en: Yes, they got married at twenty. My grandfather died in 2010.

days.title: Compréhension de l'oral : annonces et messages
days.objective: You can handle the listening task shape used at A2: catch the key facts in a short public announcement or a phone message on a single hearing.
days.paretoFocus: Announcements are dense but predictable, so knowing which three or four pieces of information to hunt for turns a wall of speech into a short list.
drills: Listen to any short French announcement and write down only the numbers you hear.
Say a station announcement out loud, then repeat it changing the time and the platform.
Leave yourself a thirty second voice message in French and write down its three key facts.

items: Which three details do you note first in a transport announcement?
Which two verb tenses dominate public announcements?
What does reporté à tell you about an appointment?

activities.title: Écouter pour l'essentiel
activities.intro: Short announcements always follow the same shape: who is being addressed, what is happening, and the numbers that matter. Read the questions before listening, then hunt only for those facts. Phone messages add one more element: what the caller wants you to do.

items.hr: Mesdames et messieurs, le train de quinze heures dix pour Bordeaux partira voie sept.
items.en: Ladies and gentlemen, the fifteen ten train to Bordeaux will leave from platform seven.
items.note: note the time, the destination and the platform, in that order

items.hr: En raison de travaux, la piscine sera fermée du lundi trois au vendredi sept.
items.en: Because of building work, the swimming pool will be closed from Monday the third to Friday the seventh.
items.note: en raison de introduces the reason; the dates are the answer

items.hr: Bonjour, c'est Camille. Je ne peux pas venir jeudi, on se voit vendredi ?
items.en: Hello, it is Camille. I cannot come on Thursday, shall we meet on Friday?
items.note: a message has three parts: who, the problem, the new proposal

items.hr: Chers clients, le magasin fermera dans dix minutes.
items.en: Dear customers, the shop will close in ten minutes.
items.note: chers clients signals a shop announcement, so expect a time

items.hr: Votre rendez-vous chez le dentiste est reporté à mardi quatorze heures.
items.en: Your dental appointment has been moved to Tuesday at two in the afternoon.
items.note: reporté à means moved to a later time, not cancelled

activities.title: Repérer l'information
activities.intro: Practise the listening strategy on the announcements above.

questions.prompt: In the station announcement, which three details must you catch first?
options: the weather, the time, the price
the time, the destination, the platform
the driver, the platform, the price
the ticket type, the season, the date
questions.answer: the time, the destination, the platform
questions.explanation: Transport announcements are built around those three facts, and questions almost always target them.

questions.prompt: Camille's message asks you to...
questions.explanation: She states the problem, then proposes a new day, which is the part the question will test.

questions.prompt: Complete the announcement opening: En ____ de travaux, la rue est fermée.
questions.explanation: en raison de is the standard formula announcing the cause of a disruption.

questions.prompt: The simple future is everywhere in announcements, so bring it back here: Le magasin ____ à vingt heures. (fermer)
questions.explanation: Regular -er verbs build the future on the infinitive: fermer plus a gives fermera.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'The shop will close in ten minutes.'
questions.explanation: dans plus a length of time means after that amount of time has passed.

activities.title: Dans le hall de la gare
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A traveller did not catch the announcement and you report the key facts back.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Excusez-moi, vous avez entendu l'annonce ?
lines.en: Excuse me, did you hear the announcement?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, le train pour Bordeaux part voie sept.
lines.en: Yes, the train to Bordeaux leaves from platform seven.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: À quelle heure exactement ?
lines.en: At exactly what time?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: À quinze heures dix, dans vingt minutes.
lines.en: At three ten, in twenty minutes.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et pourquoi il y a du retard aujourd'hui ?
lines.en: And why are there delays today?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: En raison de travaux. Attention, ils ont changé la voie.
lines.en: Because of building work. Careful, they have changed the platform.

days.title: Compréhension des écrits : documents du quotidien
days.objective: You can handle the reading task shape used at A2: pull the required facts out of a small ad, a note, a short email, a sign or a set of instructions.
days.paretoFocus: Everyday documents are read by scanning, not by translating, and knowing where the answer lives in each document type saves the time the task does not give you.
drills: Scan any short advert and write down every number and date in it before reading a single verb.
Read a set of instructions and list the actions in order, one verb each.
Write a five line note to a housemate and then underline the one fact they must not miss.

items: What do you look at first in a small ad, and why?
Which words on a sign tell you something is forbidden?
Where does an email put the deadline you are being asked about?

activities.title: Lire vite et juste
activities.intro: Each everyday document hides its answer in a predictable place. Small ads put it in the numbers, notes put it in the location, emails put it in the deadline, signs put it in the verb, and instructions put it in the order of the steps.

items.hr: Appartement deux pièces, quarante-cinq mètres carrés, six cent vingt euros par mois, libre le premier septembre.
items.en: Two room flat, forty-five square metres, six hundred and twenty euros a month, available from the first of September.
items.note: in an advert the rent and the date carry the question

items.hr: Je suis chez le voisin, la clé est sous le pot de fleurs.
items.en: I am at the neighbour's, the key is under the flowerpot.
items.note: a note answers where, so read the prepositions

items.hr: Votre commande est prête. Vous pouvez la retirer en magasin jusqu'au douze mai.
items.en: Your order is ready. You can collect it in store until the twelfth of May.
items.note: jusqu'au marks the deadline, which is what gets tested

items.hr: Interdit de stationner devant l'entrée.
items.en: No parking in front of the entrance.
items.note: interdit de plus infinitive is the sign formula for a prohibition

items.hr: Mélangez la farine et les œufs, puis ajoutez le lait.
items.en: Mix the flour and the eggs, then add the milk.
items.note: instructions use the vous imperative and connectors of order

activities.title: Chercher la réponse
activities.intro: Practise scanning the five document types above for the fact that is asked.

questions.prompt: According to the flat advert, the rent is...
options: six hundred and twenty euros a month
questions.answer: six hundred and twenty euros a month
questions.explanation: Forty-five is the surface area, so the price is the figure followed by par mois.

questions.prompt: When you scan a small ad, what should you read first?
questions.explanation: Adverts are lists of figures, and the questions are built on the figures rather than the wording.

questions.prompt: Complete the sign: Interdit de ____ devant l'entrée. (to park)
questions.explanation: stationner is the word used on signs; garer belongs to spoken French.

questions.prompt: A direct object pronoun you already handle avoids repeating the noun: Votre commande est prête, vous pouvez ____ retirer demain.
questions.explanation: commande is feminine singular, so it is replaced by la before the infinitive.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'The key is under the flowerpot.'
questions.explanation: sous places the object underneath, and pot de fleurs is a fixed compound.

activities.title: Devant le tableau d'affichage
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A neighbour cannot read the notices in the hall and you scan them for the facts.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Il y a une annonce pour un appartement ?
lines.en: Is there an advert for a flat?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, deux pièces, six cent vingt euros par mois.
lines.en: Yes, two rooms, six hundred and twenty euros a month.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Il est libre quand ?
lines.en: When is it available?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Le premier septembre, d'après l'annonce.
lines.en: The first of September, according to the advert.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et l'autre papier, là, à côté ?
lines.en: And the other notice, there, next to it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est un panneau : interdit de stationner devant l'entrée.
lines.en: It is a sign: no parking in front of the entrance.

days.title: Production écrite : un message de soixante mots
days.objective: You can write a short personal message of about sixty words that invites, apologises or recounts, with a proper opening and closing.
days.paretoFocus: A sixty word message has room for four moves and no more, so knowing the four moves in advance is worth more than knowing extra vocabulary.
drills: Write an invitation of about sixty words and count the words afterwards.
Write a refusal that apologises and gives one reason, in three sentences.
Recount one thing you did last weekend in two sentences and add one sentence saying how it felt.

items: What are the four moves of a short personal message?
Which opening and which closing suit a friend rather than an office?
Which tense carries the events of a short account, and which one carries the feeling?

activities.title: Écrire court et complet
activities.intro: A short personal message has four moves: greet, say why you are writing, give the content in two or three sentences, and close. Keep the register informal with a friend. Sixty words means roughly five short sentences, so plan them before writing.

items.hr: Coucou Marc, j'espère que tout va bien pour toi.
items.en: Hi Marc, I hope all is well with you.
items.note: move one: informal greeting plus one friendly line

items.hr: Je t'écris pour t'inviter à mon anniversaire samedi soir.
items.en: I am writing to invite you to my birthday on Saturday evening.
items.note: move two: je t'écris pour plus infinitive states the purpose

items.hr: Je suis vraiment désolé, je ne peux pas venir : je travaille ce jour-là.
items.en: I am really sorry, I cannot come: I am working that day.
items.note: an apology always needs a reason right behind it

items.hr: Ce week-end, je suis allé à la mer avec des amis. C'était super.
items.en: This weekend I went to the seaside with friends. It was great.
items.note: compound past for the event, imperfect for the impression

items.hr: Réponds-moi vite. Bises, Marc.
items.en: Write back soon. Love, Marc.
items.note: move four: a short request plus an informal sign off

activities.title: Construire le message
activities.intro: Practise the building blocks of the short written message.

questions.prompt: A sixty word message to a close friend should open with...
questions.explanation: The register is informal, so a first name greeting fits and the formal formulas do not.

questions.prompt: Which sentence apologises and gives a reason?
options: Désolé, je ne peux pas venir parce que je travaille.
questions.answer: Désolé, je ne peux pas venir parce que je travaille.
questions.explanation: A refusal is only complete when the apology is followed by a justification.

questions.prompt: Complete the purpose sentence: Je t'écris ____ t'inviter à la fête.
questions.explanation: pour plus infinitive states why you are writing.

questions.prompt: Your compound past with être resurfaces here: Samedi, je ____ allée chez ma tante.
questions.explanation: aller takes être, and the extra e on allée shows the writer is female.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Write back quickly, see you Saturday.'
questions.explanation: The imperative keeps its pronoun attached with a hyphen, and à plus a day means see you then.

activities.title: Préparer un petit mot
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend helps you draft a short invitation and you say each move out loud.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu commences comment, ton message ?
lines.en: How are you starting your message?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Salut Léa, j'espère que tu vas bien.
lines.en: Hi Léa, I hope you are well.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bien. Et pourquoi tu écris ?
lines.en: Good. And why are you writing?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je t'écris pour t'inviter à la fête de samedi.
lines.en: I am writing to invite you to Saturday's party.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Il faut donner l'heure et le lieu aussi.
lines.en: You need to give the time and the place too.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: On se retrouve chez moi à vingt heures.
lines.en: We are meeting at my place at eight.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et tu finis comment ?
lines.en: And how are you finishing?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Réponds-moi vite. Bises !
lines.en: Write back soon. Love!

days.title: Production orale : entretien, monologue et jeu de rôle
days.objective: You can handle the three spoken parts used at A2: introduce yourself, speak alone for a minute on a familiar topic, and carry out a simple transaction with a partner.
days.paretoFocus: Each of the three parts rewards a different habit, and the biggest gain is knowing which habit belongs to which part before you open your mouth.
drills: Introduce yourself out loud in four sentences without pausing.
Speak for one minute about a free time activity, giving two reasons.
Act out returning an item in a shop, starting politely and ending with a question about payment.

items: What is the difference between a one word answer and a good answer in the guided interview?
Which connector turns a bare statement into a real monologue sentence?
Which polite verb form opens a request in a shop?

activities.title: Parler en trois temps
activities.intro: The first part is a guided interview about you, where every answer should add one extra detail. The second is a short solo turn where each statement needs a reason. The third is a role play, where politeness and one clear question do most of the work.

items.hr: Bonjour, je m'appelle Sofia, j'ai vingt-huit ans et je suis infirmière.
items.en: Hello, my name is Sofia, I am twenty-eight and I am a nurse.
items.note: part one: three facts in one breath, never a single word

items.hr: J'habite à Nantes depuis trois ans avec mon frère.
items.en: I have been living in Nantes for three years with my brother.
items.note: depuis plus a length of time for something still true

items.hr: Le sport est important pour moi parce que ça m'aide à me détendre.
items.en: Sport matters to me because it helps me relax.
items.note: part two: statement plus parce que plus reason

items.hr: Excusez-moi, je voudrais échanger ce pull, il est trop petit.
items.en: Excuse me, I would like to exchange this jumper, it is too small.
items.note: part three: apologise for interrupting, then state the request

items.hr: C'est combien ? Est-ce que je peux payer par carte ?
items.en: How much is it? Can I pay by card?
items.note: asking a question keeps the role play alive

activities.title: Se présenter et négocier
activities.intro: Practise the habits that each spoken part rewards.

questions.prompt: In the guided interview, which answer to 'Vous vous appelez comment ?' is strongest?
options: Je m'appelle Sofia et je suis infirmière à Nantes.
questions.answer: Je m'appelle Sofia et je suis infirmière à Nantes.
questions.explanation: The interview rewards adding one relevant detail beyond the bare answer.

questions.prompt: A short solo turn is stronger when you...
options: give a reason with parce que
questions.answer: give a reason with parce que
questions.explanation: Justifying an opinion is exactly the descriptor being assessed at this level.

questions.prompt: Make the request polite: Je ____ échanger ce pull. (vouloir)
questions.explanation: The conditional voudrais softens a request; je veux sounds blunt in a shop.

questions.prompt: The question opener from your earlier lessons is missing: ____ vous acceptez la carte ?
questions.explanation: Est-ce que turns a statement into a question without changing the word order.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I have been living in Nantes for three years.'
questions.explanation: French keeps the present tense with depuis where English uses a perfect.

activities.title: Au magasin : échanger un pull
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. This is the role play part: you exchange an item, stay polite and ask your own questions.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bonjour, je peux vous aider ?
lines.en: Hello, can I help you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bonjour, je voudrais échanger ce pull, il est trop petit.
lines.en: Hello, I would like to exchange this jumper, it is too small.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vous avez le ticket de caisse ?
lines.en: Do you have the receipt?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, le voilà. Vous avez la taille au-dessus ?
lines.en: Yes, here it is. Do you have the next size up?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: En bleu seulement, et il coûte cinq euros de plus.
lines.en: In blue only, and it costs five euros more.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: D'accord. Est-ce que je peux payer par carte ?
lines.en: All right. Can I pay by card?

days.title: Examen blanc : niveau A2
days.objective: You can run through all four skills at A2 in one sitting, budgeting your time and applying the strategy each part needs.
days.paretoFocus: The single biggest score loss at this level is not weak French but bad time management, and one full rehearsal fixes it better than any amount of extra grammar.
drills: Do one full run of the four parts in order, with a timer and no pauses.
Note the one part where you ran out of time and repeat only that part tomorrow.
Reread your two written texts and count how many mistakes you can find without help.

items: How many minutes do you give to each of the four parts?
What do you do in the last five minutes of the written part?
Which structures from the last few lessons did you actually use today?

activities.title: Le déroulé de l'épreuve
activities.intro: The four parts always come in the same order and each has its own clock. Read the questions before every listening, scan before you read closely, plan before you write, and keep the last five minutes for rereading. Treat the whole thing as one continuous session.

items.hr: Écoute : deux annonces et un message, environ vingt-cinq minutes.
items.en: Listening: two announcements and one message, about twenty-five minutes.
items.note: read the questions during the pause before each recording

items.hr: Lecture : quatre documents courts, environ trente minutes.
items.en: Reading: four short documents, about thirty minutes.
items.note: scan for numbers and dates before reading the sentences

items.hr: Écriture : deux textes de soixante mots, environ quarante-cinq minutes.
items.en: Writing: two texts of sixty words, about forty-five minutes.
items.note: plan four moves per text, then write

items.hr: Oral : entretien, monologue et jeu de rôle, six à huit minutes.
items.en: Speaking: interview, solo turn and role play, six to eight minutes.
items.note: add one detail in the interview, one reason in the monologue

items.hr: Conseil : garde cinq minutes à la fin pour te relire.
items.en: Tip: keep five minutes at the end to reread your work.
items.note: check agreements, accents and auxiliaries first

activities.title: Tout ce qui vient d'être vu
activities.intro: Run through the structures of the last few lessons in one mixed set.

questions.prompt: ____ tu as le temps, passe à la maison.
questions.explanation: A real condition followed by an imperative takes si plus the present.

questions.prompt: ____ belle surprise !
questions.explanation: The exclamation runs straight into a feminine noun, so it takes quelle rather than que or comme.

questions.prompt: State the prohibition: Il ne ____ pas fumer ici.
questions.explanation: The impersonal falloir gives il faut, wrapped by ne and pas for a prohibition.

questions.prompt: Give the participle of naître: Elle est ____ en 1992.
questions.explanation: naître takes être, so the participle agrees with the feminine subject.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'In France, people have dinner around eight in the evening.'
questions.explanation: The general value of on renders English people or you, and the verb stays singular.

activities.title: Après l'examen blanc
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You debrief the practice session with a classmate and say what you will change.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Alors, comment ça s'est passé ?
lines.en: So, how did it go?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: L'écoute, ça allait, mais j'ai manqué de temps à l'écrit.
lines.en: The listening was fine, but I ran out of time on the writing.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as fait un plan avant d'écrire ?
lines.en: Did you make a plan before writing?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, et c'est là que j'ai perdu dix minutes.
lines.en: No, and that is where I lost ten minutes.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et l'oral ? Le jeu de rôle est court.
lines.en: And the speaking? The role play is short.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Quelle bonne surprise ! J'ai posé deux questions moi-même.
lines.en: What a nice surprise! I asked two questions myself.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu recommences quand ?
lines.en: When are you doing it again?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Si j'ai le temps dimanche, je referai tout.
lines.en: If I have time on Sunday, I will do the whole thing again.

days.title: Présenter un pays et une ville
days.objective: Introduce a country or a town: where it lies, how big it is, and what makes the place worth visiting.
days.paretoFocus: Saying where a place is and what it is like is the first thing anyone asks you abroad, and it recycles the whole geography vocabulary in one shot.
drills: Write four sentences about your own town: location, size, one nice spot, one thing people do there.
Pick a country you like and say in French where it is, using au bord de, à l'ouest de or au milieu de.
Describe a place you know using both un endroit and un lieu, and notice the difference in tone.

items: How do you say that a town lies on the edge of a river?
Which verb do you use to give a population figure?
Name three ways to situate a place: middle, edge, and a compass direction.

activities.title: Où se trouve cette ville ?
activities.intro: To present a place you need three moves: situate it, size it, and describe it. Se trouver is the everyday verb for 'to be located'. Compter plus a number is how French gives a population.

items.hr: Le Portugal se trouve au bord de l'océan Atlantique, à l'ouest de l'Espagne.
items.en: Portugal lies on the edge of the Atlantic Ocean, west of Spain.
items.note: au bord de = on the edge of, by

items.hr: Lyon compte environ cinq cent mille habitants, c'est la troisième ville du pays.
items.en: Lyon has about five hundred thousand inhabitants, it is the third city of the country.
items.note: compter + number = to have a population of

items.hr: Au milieu de la ville, il y a un endroit très calme que j'aime beaucoup.
items.en: In the middle of the town there is a very quiet spot that I like a lot.
items.note: un endroit is the everyday word for a spot

items.hr: C'est un lieu historique : la vieille ville existe depuis plus de mille ans.
items.en: It is a historic place: the old town has existed for more than a thousand years.
items.note: un lieu is slightly more formal than un endroit

items.hr: En Suisse, on parle français, allemand et italien, et beaucoup d'étrangers viennent y travailler.
items.en: In Switzerland people speak French, German and Italian, and many foreigners come to work there.
items.note: y replaces the place already mentioned

activities.title: Situer et décrire
activities.intro: Practise locating a place, giving its size and describing what is there.

questions.prompt: Le village ___ au bord d'une rivière.
questions.explanation: Se trouver is a reflexive verb: the subject il/elle takes se plus the present form trouve.

questions.prompt: Earlier material: Ma cousine habite ___ Portugal et travaille ___ Lisbonne.
questions.explanation: Masculine countries take au, and towns take à.

questions.prompt: Cette ville ___ deux cent mille habitants.
questions.explanation: Compter plus a figure is the standard way to state a population.

questions.prompt: Je connais un petit ___ très tranquille près de la mer.
questions.explanation: Un endroit is the ordinary spoken word for a spot or place.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'The museum is in the middle of the old town.'
questions.explanation: Au milieu de takes de plus the article, and vieille comes before the noun.

activities.title: Tu viens d'où ?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You present your home town to someone who has never been there.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu viens d'où exactement ?
lines.en: Where exactly are you from?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je viens d'une petite ville qui se trouve au bord d'un lac, à l'est du pays.
lines.en: I come from a small town that lies by a lake, in the east of the country.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Elle est grande ?
lines.en: Is it big?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, elle compte trente mille habitants, mais il y a un endroit magnifique au milieu de la vieille ville.
lines.en: No, it has thirty thousand inhabitants, but there is a magnificent spot in the middle of the old town.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et les gens y parlent quelle langue ?
lines.en: And what language do people speak there?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: On parle deux langues, et beaucoup d'étrangers viennent y travailler l'été.
lines.en: People speak two languages, and a lot of foreigners come to work there in the summer.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ça existe depuis longtemps, cette ville ?
lines.en: Has that town existed for a long time?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, c'est un lieu très ancien : il y avait déjà un village ici il y a mille ans.
lines.en: Yes, it is a very old place: there was already a village here a thousand years ago.

days.title: Un fait divers simple
