days.objective: Tell a short news story about something that happened to someone: a theft, a fall, a missed train.
days.paretoFocus: Small everyday incidents are the most common story anyone tells, and they force the passé composé with both auxiliaries into real use.
drills: Retell in three sentences something that went wrong for you this month.
Write two sentences with quelqu'un as the subject and two with une dame.
Say out loud, three times, what you do when you miss a train.

items: Which auxiliary does tomber take in the passé composé?
How do you say 'it is the third time this has happened'?
Give the noun for the person who steals, and the verb.

activities.title: Ce qui est arrivé hier
activities.intro: A short news item answers who, what, where and when, in the passé composé. Voler means both to steal and to fly, and le voleur is the thief. Rater is the everyday verb for missing a train or failing something.

items.hr: Hier matin, quelqu'un a volé le sac d'une dame devant la gare.
items.en: Yesterday morning someone stole a lady's bag in front of the station.
items.note: voler quelque chose à quelqu'un

items.hr: Le voleur est parti très vite, mais un passant a crié et il est tombé.
items.en: The thief left very fast, but a passer-by shouted and he fell.
items.note: partir and tomber take être

items.hr: La dame a raté son train, alors elle a demandé de l'aide à un employé.
items.en: The lady missed her train, so she asked an employee for help.
items.note: demander quelque chose à quelqu'un

items.hr: Ce n'est pas la première fois que cela arrive dans ce quartier.
items.en: It is not the first time that this has happened in this neighbourhood.
items.note: la fois = the occasion, une fois, deux fois

items.hr: La police est arrivée dix minutes plus tard et tout le monde allait bien.
items.en: The police arrived ten minutes later and everyone was fine.
items.note: la police is singular in French

activities.title: Raconter l'incident
activities.intro: Practise reporting a small incident in the past.

questions.prompt: Le voleur ___ tombé devant la boulangerie.
questions.explanation: Tomber is a movement verb, so its passé composé uses être.

questions.prompt: Which sentence correctly says 'She did not miss her train'?
options: Elle n'a pas raté son train.
Elle a ne pas raté son train.
Elle n'a raté pas son train.
Elle ne rate pas son train hier.
questions.answer: Elle n'a pas raté son train.
questions.explanation: In the passé composé the ne and the pas wrap the auxiliary, not the participle.

questions.prompt: Quelqu'un ___ mon vélo devant l'immeuble la semaine dernière.
questions.explanation: Voler is a regular -er verb with avoir: a plus volé.

questions.prompt: C'est la troisième ___ que je perds mes clés ce mois-ci.
questions.explanation: La fois counts occasions; une heure or un temps would not work here.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'A passer-by asked for help.'
questions.explanation: Demander de l'aide keeps the partitive de l' before aide.

activities.title: Devant la gare
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A neighbour asks you about the incident you saw this morning.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Il paraît qu'il y a eu un problème devant la gare ce matin.
lines.en: Apparently there was a problem in front of the station this morning.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, quelqu'un a volé le sac d'une dame vers huit heures.
lines.en: Yes, someone stole a lady's bag at around eight o'clock.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et le voleur, il est parti ?
lines.en: And the thief, did he get away?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il est parti très vite, mais il est tombé au coin de la rue.
lines.en: He left very fast, but he fell at the corner of the street.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: La pauvre dame, elle allait bien ?
lines.en: The poor lady, was she all right?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Elle allait bien, mais elle a raté son train et elle a demandé de l'aide à un employé.
lines.en: She was fine, but she missed her train and asked an employee for help.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ce n'est pas la première fois ici.
lines.en: It is not the first time here.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, c'est la deuxième fois ce mois-ci, et maintenant tout le monde fait attention.
lines.en: No, it is the second time this month, and now everyone is careful.

days.title: La famille élargie et les étapes de la vie
days.objective: Talk about your wider family and the stages of a life: being born, growing up, marrying, dying.
days.paretoFocus: Every conversation about family runs on naître, se marier and mourir, and these three verbs are also the backbone of any simple biography.
drills: Draw your family tree and label eight people in French.
Write three sentences about a grandparent using naître, la jeunesse and l'âge.
Say aloud who in your family you resemble, and why.

items: Give the passé composé of naître and of mourir.
How do you say 'at the age of ninety'?
What is the difference between un an and une année in these sentences?

activities.title: Naître, grandir, se marier
activities.intro: Naître and mourir both take être in the passé composé, and so does the reflexive se marier. Use à l'âge de plus a number of ans to say how old someone was.

items.hr: Ma grand-mère est née en Bretagne et elle a passé toute sa jeunesse à la campagne.
items.en: My grandmother was born in Brittany and spent her whole youth in the countryside.
items.note: naître -> il est né, elle est née

items.hr: Mon grand-père est mort il y a trois ans, à l'âge de quatre-vingt-douze ans.
items.en: My grandfather died three years ago, at the age of ninety-two.
items.note: mourir -> il est mort

items.hr: Mes parents se sont mariés très jeunes : maman avait vingt ans et papa vingt-deux.
items.en: My parents got married very young: mum was twenty and dad twenty-two.
items.note: maman and papa are used without an article

items.hr: Ma tante a deux enfants, donc j'ai un neveu et une nièce.
items.en: My aunt has two children, so I have a nephew and a niece.

items.hr: Mon petit frère ressemble beaucoup à papa quand il rit.
items.en: My little brother looks a lot like dad when he laughs.
items.note: ressembler à quelqu'un

activities.title: Les étapes d'une vie
activities.intro: Practise the life-stage verbs and the family words.

questions.prompt: Ma grand-mère ___ en 1940, juste avant la guerre.
questions.explanation: Naître takes être, and the participle agrees with the feminine subject: née.

questions.prompt: Revisiting possessives: ___ tante et ___ oncle habitent à Nantes.
questions.explanation: The possessive agrees with the noun, not with the owner: tante is feminine, oncle masculine.

questions.prompt: Mon arrière-grand-père est ___ pendant la guerre, très loin de sa famille.
questions.explanation: The participle of mourir is mort, and it agrees with the masculine subject.

questions.prompt: Elle ___ beaucoup à sa mère : elles ont la même voix.
questions.explanation: Ressembler always keeps the preposition à before the person.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'My parents got married in the spring.'
questions.explanation: Reflexive verbs take être, and the participle agrees with the plural subject.

activities.title: Les photos de famille
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend is looking through your family photos and asking who everyone is.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: C'est qui, cette dame sur la photo ?
lines.en: Who is this lady in the photo?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est ma grand-mère. Elle est née dans un petit village en 1938.
lines.en: That is my grandmother. She was born in a small village in 1938.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Elle est toujours vivante ?
lines.en: Is she still alive?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, mais mon grand-père est mort il y a trois ans, à l'âge de quatre-vingt-dix ans.
lines.en: Yes, but my grandfather died three years ago, at the age of ninety.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et à côté, ce sont tes parents ?
lines.en: And next to her, are those your parents?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, ils se sont mariés très jeunes, maman avait à peine vingt ans.
lines.en: Yes, they got married very young, mum was barely twenty.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Le petit garçon ressemble beaucoup à ton père.
lines.en: The little boy looks a lot like your father.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est mon neveu, et tout le monde dit qu'il a la voix de papa.
lines.en: That is my nephew, and everyone says he has dad's voice.

days.title: Les mesures et les quantités
days.objective: Give measurements and fractions: metres, kilometres, kilos, halves, quarters and doubles.
days.paretoFocus: Shopping, cooking, travelling and renting all run on measures, and half, quarter and double are among the most reused words in daily French.
drills: Measure three objects at home and write their size in French.
Write a shopping list of five items with a quantity for each.
Say out loud how far you live from the town centre, in kilometres.

items: How do you say 'a quarter of an hour' and 'half of the class'?
Which verb do you use for 'I am two euros short'?
What is the French for 'twice as much'?

activities.title: Combien ça fait ?
activities.intro: Faire plus a number gives dimensions. La moitié, le quart and le double all take de before the next noun. Manquer works backwards from English: il me manque means I am short of.

items.hr: La piscine fait vingt-cinq mètres de long et deux mètres de profondeur.
items.en: The pool is twenty-five metres long and two metres deep.
items.note: faire + measurement + de long

items.hr: Il y a environ quinze kilomètres entre le village et la ville la plus proche.
items.en: There are about fifteen kilometres between the village and the nearest town.

items.hr: Je voudrais un kilo de tomates et la moitié d'un melon, s'il vous plaît.
items.en: I would like a kilo of tomatoes and half a melon, please.
items.note: la moitié de = half of

items.hr: Un quart d'heure, ça suffit pour préparer ce plat.
items.en: A quarter of an hour is enough to prepare this dish.
items.note: suffire = to be enough

items.hr: Il me manque deux euros : ce livre coûte le double de l'autre.
items.en: I am two euros short: this book costs twice as much as the other one.
items.note: il me manque = I am short of

activities.title: Mesurer et compter
activities.intro: Practise sizes, fractions and the verbs of quantity.

questions.prompt: Un ___ d'heure, c'est quinze minutes.
questions.explanation: Le quart is a fourth, so un quart d'heure is fifteen minutes.

questions.prompt: A look back at partitives: Je voudrais ___ sucre et ___ pommes.
questions.explanation: Sucre is masculine singular so it takes du, and pommes is plural so it takes des.

questions.prompt: Il me ___ dix euros pour acheter ce sac.
questions.explanation: With manquer the missing thing is the subject, so the verb stays singular here.

questions.prompt: Ce sac pèse trois ___ , c'est trop lourd pour moi.
questions.explanation: Kilo takes a plural s after a number greater than one.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Half of the class lives in the village.'
questions.explanation: La moitié de takes a singular verb in everyday French.

activities.title: Au marché
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are buying food and need exact quantities.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bonjour, qu'est-ce qu'il vous faut aujourd'hui ?
lines.en: Hello, what do you need today?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Un kilo de tomates et la moitié d'un melon, s'il vous plaît.
lines.en: A kilo of tomatoes and half a melon, please.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Voilà. Le melon fait presque deux kilos, c'est beaucoup pour une personne.
lines.en: Here you are. The melon is almost two kilos, that is a lot for one person.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Alors la moitié suffit, je suis seul cette semaine.
lines.en: Then half is enough, I am on my own this week.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ça vous fait onze euros.
lines.en: That comes to eleven euros.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ah, il me manque deux euros, je reviens dans un quart d'heure.
lines.en: Ah, I am two euros short, I will come back in a quarter of an hour.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pas de problème, je garde vos courses ici.
lines.en: No problem, I will keep your shopping here.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Merci, la banque est à un kilomètre, j'y vais vite.
lines.en: Thank you, the bank is a kilometre away, I will go there quickly.

days.title: Chez le voisin : la vie de quartier
days.objective: Talk about your neighbours and your local life, and arrange to meet a friend nearby.
days.paretoFocus: Neighbourhood talk is the most frequent small talk there is, and it carries copain, voisin, sortir and d'accord, all in the top of the spoken frequency list.
drills: Describe three of your neighbours in French, one sentence each.
Write a short message inviting a friend for a coffee this afternoon.
Practise answering an invitation with d'accord and one condition.

items: What is the difference in tone between un ami and un copain?
How do you accept a suggestion in one word?
Which verb means to go out, and what is its passé composé?

activities.title: Les gens du quartier
activities.intro: Un copain is a mate, warmer and more casual than un ami. Retrouver means to meet up with people you already know, unlike rencontrer. D'accord is the standard one-word yes to a suggestion.

items.hr: Mon voisin du dessus est très gentil : il garde mes plantes quand je pars.
items.en: My upstairs neighbour is very kind: he looks after my plants when I go away.

items.hr: La dame du rez-de-chaussée connaît tout le monde dans l'immeuble.
items.en: The lady on the ground floor knows everyone in the building.
items.note: une dame is polite for a woman you do not know well

items.hr: Je retrouve mes copains au café du coin le samedi après-midi.
items.en: I meet up with my mates at the corner cafe on Saturday afternoons.
items.note: retrouver = to meet people you already know

items.hr: Ici, on se dit bonjour dans la rue comme dans un village.
items.en: Here people say hello in the street as in a village.

items.hr: Tu veux sortir prendre un café ? D'accord, mais pas avant midi.
items.en: Do you want to go out for a coffee? All right, but not before noon.
items.note: sortir takes être in the passé composé

activities.title: Voisins et copains
activities.intro: Practise talking about the people around you and answering invitations.

questions.prompt: Je ___ mes copains devant le cinéma à huit heures.
questions.explanation: With je the -er verb ends in -e: je retrouve.

questions.prompt: Pronoun check from earlier: Tu connais la dame du troisième ? Oui, je ___ connais bien.
questions.explanation: Connaître takes a direct object, and la dame is feminine, so the pronoun is la.

questions.prompt: Ce soir, je ne veux pas ___ : je suis trop fatigué.
questions.explanation: After vouloir the second verb stays in the infinitive.

questions.prompt: On se voit à midi ? ___ , à tout à l'heure !
questions.explanation: D'accord is the standard one-word agreement to a plan.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'My neighbour helps us every week.'
questions.explanation: The object pronoun nous goes before the conjugated verb.

activities.title: Dans l'escalier
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You meet a neighbour on the stairs and end up making a plan.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bonjour ! Vous êtes le nouveau voisin du quatrième, non ?
lines.en: Hello! You are the new neighbour on the fourth floor, aren't you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, je suis arrivé le mois dernier. Tout le monde est très gentil ici.
lines.en: Yes, I moved in last month. Everyone is very kind here.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: C'est un quartier tranquille, on se dit bonjour comme dans un village.
lines.en: It is a quiet neighbourhood, people say hello as in a village.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: J'ai remarqué. La dame du rez-de-chaussée m'a déjà tout expliqué.
lines.en: I noticed. The lady on the ground floor has already explained everything to me.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Elle connaît tout le monde. Vous voulez sortir prendre un café avec nous samedi ?
lines.en: She knows everyone. Do you want to go out for a coffee with us on Saturday?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: D'accord, avec plaisir, mais pas avant midi, je travaille le matin.
lines.en: All right, with pleasure, but not before noon, I work in the morning.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Parfait, on retrouve deux copains au café du coin vers deux heures.
lines.en: Perfect, we are meeting two mates at the corner cafe around two.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Très bien, je vous retrouve là-bas samedi après-midi.
lines.en: Very good, I will meet you there on Saturday afternoon.

days.title: L'école d'hier et d'aujourd'hui
days.objective: Describe how school used to be and contrast it with how it is now, using the imparfait against the present.
days.paretoFocus: The imparfait is the tense of how things used to be, and comparing past habits with today is the single most common use of it in real speech.
drills: Write five sentences beginning with Quand j'étais petit about your own school.
Take three of those sentences and rewrite them in the present, starting with Maintenant.
Say aloud two things that did not exist in classrooms when you were a child.

items: Give the imparfait of avoir, être and faire for nous.
Which tense do you use for a repeated habit in the past?
How do you introduce the contrast with today in one word?

activities.title: Quand j'étais à l'école
activities.intro: The imparfait describes past habits, states and settings, with no idea of a finished event. Its endings are -ais, -ais, -ait, -ions, -iez, -aient on the nous stem of the present. Maintenant plus the present makes the contrast with today.

items.hr: Quand j'étais petit, l'école commençait à huit heures et finissait à cinq heures.
items.en: When I was little, school started at eight and finished at five.
items.note: commencer -> nous commençons -> il commençait

items.hr: Au début de l'année, la maîtresse écrivait tout au tableau et nous écrivions dans un cahier.
items.en: At the beginning of the year, the teacher wrote everything on the board and we wrote in an exercise book.
items.note: le début = the beginning

items.hr: Il n'y avait pas d'écrans : on regardait des images dans un grand livre.
items.en: There were no screens: we looked at pictures in a big book.
items.note: une image = a picture

items.hr: Les élèves ne prenaient pas la parole sans lever la main.
items.en: Pupils did not speak up without raising their hand.
items.note: prendre la parole = to take the floor

items.hr: Maintenant, les enfants travaillent souvent à deux et posent beaucoup de questions.
items.en: Nowadays children often work in pairs and ask a lot of questions.
items.note: maintenant introduces the contrast with today

activities.title: Avant, c'était différent
activities.intro: Practise the imparfait for past habits and its contrast with the present.

questions.prompt: Quand j'étais enfant, nous ___ à pied à l'école tous les matins.
questions.explanation: Tous les matins marks a repeated past habit, which takes the imparfait.

questions.prompt: Tense contrast already met: Je lisais tranquillement quand le téléphone ___ .
questions.explanation: The background is in the imparfait and the single interrupting event is in the passé composé.

questions.prompt: Avant, il n'y ___ pas d'ordinateurs dans les salles de classe.
questions.explanation: Il y a becomes il y avait in the imparfait.

questions.prompt: Mon frère ___ toujours ses devoirs le dimanche soir, jamais avant.
questions.explanation: Faire builds its imparfait on the nous stem: nous faisons gives il faisait.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'The teacher spoke slowly and we listened.'
questions.explanation: Both verbs describe the ongoing scene, so both are in the imparfait.

activities.title: Grand-mère raconte l'école
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your grandmother describes her school days and you compare them with today.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: De mon temps, l'école commençait à huit heures, même en hiver.
lines.en: In my day, school started at eight, even in winter.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Et vous écriviez sur quoi, au début ?
lines.en: And what did you write on, at the beginning?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sur des cahiers. La maîtresse écrivait tout au tableau et nous recopiions en silence.
lines.en: On exercise books. The teacher wrote everything on the board and we copied in silence.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Maintenant les enfants travaillent à deux et parlent beaucoup en classe.
lines.en: Nowadays children work in pairs and talk a lot in class.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Chez nous, personne ne prenait la parole sans lever la main.
lines.en: With us, nobody spoke up without raising a hand.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il n'y avait pas d'écrans non plus, j'imagine.
lines.en: There were no screens either, I imagine.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Non, on regardait des images dans un grand livre, et ça nous suffisait.
lines.en: No, we looked at pictures in a big book, and that was enough for us.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Franchement, ça avait aussi du bon, cette école-là.
lines.en: Honestly, that school had its good sides too.

days.title: Le corps en mouvement
days.objective: Give and follow instructions about moving the body: get up, sit down, hold, move, throw.
days.paretoFocus: These five verbs cover a doctor's appointment, a sports class and a crowded train, and three of them are reflexive, which is where learners lose points.
drills: Give five instructions aloud to an imaginary class, using the vous imperative.
Rewrite the same five instructions for a child, using the tu form.
Describe where you hurt after a long walk, in two sentences.

items: Where does the pronoun go in a positive command, and in a negative one?
Give the je form of s'asseoir.
How do you say 'my back hurts' and 'my knees hurt'?

activities.title: Bougez, asseyez-vous, tenez
activities.intro: In a positive command the reflexive pronoun follows the verb and is joined with a hyphen, and te becomes toi. In a negative command the pronoun goes back before the verb. Avoir mal takes à plus the article: au dos, aux genoux.

items.hr: Levez-vous doucement et asseyez-vous sur la chaise.
items.en: Stand up slowly and sit down on the chair.
items.note: se lever -> levez-vous ; s'asseoir -> asseyez-vous

items.hr: Ne bougez pas la tête, tournez seulement les épaules.
items.en: Do not move your head, turn only your shoulders.
items.note: bouger can be used with or without an object

items.hr: Tiens ce sac avec les deux mains, il est lourd.
items.en: Hold this bag with both hands, it is heavy.
items.note: tenir -> je tiens, tu tiens, nous tenons

items.hr: Il a jeté la balle trop loin et elle est tombée dans l'eau.
items.en: He threw the ball too far and it fell into the water.
items.note: jeter -> je jette, nous jetons

items.hr: Après une heure de marche, j'avais mal au dos et aux genoux.
items.en: After an hour of walking, my back and my knees hurt.

activities.title: Donner des consignes
activities.intro: Practise commands with reflexive verbs and the vocabulary of the body.

questions.prompt: ___ , s'il vous plaît, le cours commence dans deux minutes.
questions.explanation: In a positive command the pronoun follows the verb and is joined by a hyphen.

questions.prompt: From an earlier lesson on pain: J'ai mal ___ genou depuis hier soir.
questions.explanation: À plus le contracts to au before a masculine noun.

questions.prompt: Ne ___ pas ce papier, je vais le garder.
questions.explanation: The tu command of jeter doubles the t and drops the final s: jette.

questions.prompt: Pendant le cours, je ___ toujours au fond de la salle.
questions.explanation: S'asseoir in the first person is je m'assieds, with the reflexive pronoun before the verb.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Hold my bag, please.'
questions.explanation: The tu command of tenir drops the final s of tu tiens.

activities.title: Au cours de gymnastique
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are in a gentle exercise class and the instructor gives you directions.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bonjour à tous. Levez-vous doucement, sans forcer.
lines.en: Hello everyone. Stand up slowly, without forcing.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je peux me lever lentement ? J'ai mal au dos depuis hier.
lines.en: Can I get up slowly? My back has been hurting since yesterday.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bien sûr. Ne bougez pas la tête, tournez seulement les épaules.
lines.en: Of course. Do not move your head, turn only your shoulders.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Comme ça ? Je sens quelque chose dans le genou droit.
lines.en: Like this? I feel something in my right knee.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Alors asseyez-vous une minute et tenez le dos droit.
lines.en: Then sit down for a minute and hold your back straight.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: D'accord, je m'assieds au fond de la salle.
lines.en: All right, I will sit at the back of the room.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Prenez cette petite balle et jetez-la doucement à votre voisin.
lines.en: Take this small ball and throw it gently to your neighbour.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je la tiens à deux mains, sinon elle tombe toujours.
lines.en: I am holding it with both hands, otherwise it always falls.

days.title: Parler de son état
days.objective: Say how you feel, what you need, and react to someone who looks unwell.
days.paretoFocus: Avoir besoin de and se sentir are the two everyday ways to talk about your state, and they open every conversation with a doctor, a colleague or a friend.
drills: Write four sentences with avoir besoin de: two with a noun, two with a verb.
Describe how you felt yesterday evening and how you feel now.
Practise three kind replies to someone who says they are exhausted.

items: What follows avoir besoin, and does it change with the article?
How do you say 'I feel better today'?
Give two ways to say something is possible but not certain.

activities.title: Comment tu te sens ?
activities.intro: Avoir besoin is always followed by de, whether a noun or an infinitive comes next. Se sentir describes your inner state, while avoir l'air describes how you look to others. Dans ce cas introduces a consequence of what was just said.

items.hr: Je ne me sens pas très bien aujourd'hui, j'ai besoin de dormir.
items.en: I do not feel very well today, I need to sleep.
items.note: avoir besoin de + infinitive

items.hr: Tu as l'air fatigué ; tu es en forme pour le match de demain ?
items.en: You look tired; are you in shape for tomorrow's match?
items.note: être en forme = to feel good, to be fit

items.hr: Peut-être que c'est le froid : dans ce cas, reste à la maison.
items.en: Maybe it is the cold: in that case, stay at home.
items.note: peut-être que + normal word order

items.hr: Elle a besoin d'aide pour porter ses courses jusqu'au troisième étage.
items.en: She needs help to carry her shopping up to the third floor.
items.note: de plus a vowel becomes d'

items.hr: Après une bonne nuit, je me sens beaucoup mieux.
items.en: After a good night, I feel much better.
items.note: mieux is the adverb, meilleur the adjective

activities.title: État, besoin, forme
activities.intro: Practise saying how you feel and what you need.

questions.prompt: J'ai besoin ___ repos avant le voyage.
questions.explanation: Avoir besoin is fixed with de, whatever follows it.

questions.prompt: Comparison recall: Aujourd'hui je vais ___ qu'hier, la fièvre est partie.
questions.explanation: Aller takes an adverb, and the comparative adverb of bien is mieux, not meilleur.

questions.prompt: Je ne me ___ pas bien depuis deux jours.
questions.explanation: Se sentir follows the pattern of partir: je me sens, tu te sens, il se sent.

questions.prompt: Tu es malade ? Dans ce ___ , appelle le médecin ce matin.
questions.explanation: Dans ce cas is the set phrase for drawing a consequence.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I need to rest a little.'
questions.explanation: After besoin de the verb stays in the infinitive and keeps its reflexive pronoun.

activities.title: Tu as l'air fatigué
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague notices you look unwell and you explain your state.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as l'air fatigué ce matin, tout va bien ?
lines.en: You look tired this morning, is everything all right?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je ne me sens pas très bien, j'ai mal dormi cette nuit.
lines.en: I do not feel very well, I slept badly last night.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Peut-être que c'est le froid, il fait moins cinq dehors.
lines.en: Maybe it is the cold, it is minus five outside.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Peut-être, mais surtout j'ai besoin de deux jours de repos.
lines.en: Maybe, but above all I need two days of rest.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dans ce cas, rentre chez toi, je m'occupe de la réunion.
lines.en: In that case, go home, I will handle the meeting.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tu es sûr ? J'ai besoin d'aide pour finir le dossier, c'est vrai.
lines.en: Are you sure? I do need help to finish the file, that is true.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bien sûr. Repose-toi, tu seras en forme demain.
lines.en: Of course. Rest, you will be in shape tomorrow.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Merci, après une bonne nuit je me sentirai sûrement mieux.
lines.en: Thank you, after a good night I will surely feel better.

days.title: Les objets de tous les jours 2
days.objective: Name an object whose word you have forgotten, and explain what it is for.
days.paretoFocus: No learner knows every object name, so truc, machin and servir à are the survival tools that keep a conversation moving instead of stopping it.
drills: Pick five objects in your kitchen and say what each one is used for.
Describe three objects without naming them, so someone has to guess.
Write two sentences with utile and two with servir à.

items: What do you say when the name of a thing escapes you?
Which preposition follows servir when you explain a purpose?
How do you ask what something is called?

activities.title: Ce truc, là
activities.intro: Un truc and un machin are the casual stand-ins for any object whose name you forget; both are very common in speech. Servir à plus an infinitive explains what a thing is for. Une chose is the neutral word and works in any register.

items.hr: Ce truc sur la table, c'est à toi ?
items.en: This thing on the table, is it yours?
items.note: un truc is informal but extremely frequent

items.hr: Comment s'appelle ce machin qu'on met sous la casserole ?
items.en: What is this thingy called that you put under the saucepan?
items.note: un machin is a shade vaguer than un truc

items.hr: Cet appareil sert à ouvrir les bouteilles.
items.en: This device is used to open bottles.
items.note: servir à + infinitive

items.hr: C'est une chose très utile quand on voyage souvent.
items.en: It is a very useful thing when you travel often.

items.hr: Je cherche un objet pour tenir les papiers ensemble, mais j'ai oublié son nom.
items.en: I am looking for an object to hold papers together, but I have forgotten its name.
items.note: le nom = the name

activities.title: À quoi ça sert ?
activities.intro: Practise naming and explaining everyday objects.

questions.prompt: Une pince à linge ___ à tenir le linge sur le fil.
questions.explanation: Servir in the third person singular is sert, and the purpose is introduced by à.

questions.prompt: Relative pronouns again: C'est un objet ___ sert tous les jours.
questions.explanation: The relative pronoun is the subject of sert, so it must be qui.

questions.prompt: Passe-moi ce ___ rouge sur l'étagère, je ne sais plus comment ça s'appelle.
questions.explanation: When the name escapes you, the casual placeholder keeps the sentence going.

questions.prompt: Ce petit sac est très ___ pour ranger les câbles.
questions.explanation: The adjective is invariable in the masculine singular here and follows être.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'This machine is used to make coffee.'
questions.explanation: Cet is used before a masculine noun starting with a vowel, and servir à keeps the infinitive.

activities.title: Dans le magasin
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You want to buy an object but you cannot remember its name.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bonjour, je peux vous aider ?
lines.en: Hello, can I help you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, je cherche un truc pour tenir les papiers ensemble, mais j'ai oublié le nom.
lines.en: Yes, I am looking for a thing to hold papers together, but I have forgotten the name.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Un objet en métal, petit, qu'on presse avec les doigts ?
lines.en: A small metal object that you press with your fingers?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est ça. Et je voudrais aussi le machin qui sert à ouvrir les bouteilles.
lines.en: That's it. And I would also like the thingy that is used to open bottles.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Nous avons les deux, au fond du magasin, deuxième étagère.
lines.en: We have both, at the back of the shop, second shelf.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Parfait. Ce sont des choses très utiles quand on voyage.
lines.en: Perfect. Those are very useful things when you travel.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et cet appareil-là sert à couper le pain, si vous en avez besoin.
lines.en: And that device is used to cut bread, if you need one.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non merci, j'ai déjà le même à la maison.
lines.en: No thank you, I already have the same one at home.

days.title: Avant et après
days.objective: Order a story in time: the day before, the next day, and the chain first, then, finally.
days.paretoFocus: A story that is not ordered is not understood; la veille, le lendemain and the d'abord chain are what turn a list of events into a narrative.
drills: Tell the story of your last trip in five sentences, using d'abord, ensuite and enfin.
Write two sentences with la veille and two with le lendemain.
Take any past event and say what you did the day before and the day after.

items: What is the difference between hier and la veille?
Which three connectors put a story in order?
Which verb means to leave a place or a job for good?

activities.title: La veille, le lendemain
activities.intro: Hier and demain point at today; la veille and le lendemain point at another day already mentioned in the story. D'abord, ensuite and enfin chain the steps of a narrative and are heard constantly in spoken accounts.

items.hr: La veille du départ, j'ai préparé toutes mes affaires.
items.en: The day before departure, I got all my things ready.
items.note: la veille de + noun

items.hr: Le lendemain matin, j'ai quitté la maison très tôt.
items.en: The next morning I left the house very early.
items.note: quitter always takes a direct object

items.hr: D'abord j'ai pris le bus, ensuite le train jusqu'à la frontière.
items.en: First I took the bus, then the train as far as the border.

items.hr: Enfin, je suis arrivé chez mon frère vers midi.
items.en: Finally I arrived at my brother's place around noon.
items.note: midi takes no article

items.hr: Deux jours après, j'ai repris le travail comme si de rien n'était.
items.en: Two days later I went back to work as if nothing had happened.
items.note: reprendre le travail = to go back to work

activities.title: Mettre l'histoire dans l'ordre
activities.intro: Practise ordering events with time markers and connectors.

questions.prompt: Je suis parti lundi ; ___ , mardi, j'ai visité la vieille ville.
questions.explanation: Le lendemain means the day after another day already mentioned, here Monday.

questions.prompt: Time expressions from before: J'ai quitté mon travail ___ trois mois.
questions.explanation: Il y a plus a duration places a finished event in the past.

questions.prompt: ___ du concert, nous avons dormi chez des amis.
questions.explanation: This marks the day before an event already named in the story.

questions.prompt: D'abord on mange, ___ on regarde un film, et enfin on se couche.
questions.explanation: The middle step of a narrative chain takes this connector.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'The next day, I went back to my job.'
questions.explanation: Reprendre forms its participle like prendre: repris.

activities.title: Le récit du week-end
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend asks how your weekend trip went and you tell it in order.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Alors, ce week-end à la mer, raconte !
lines.en: So, that weekend at the seaside, tell me about it!

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: La veille du départ, j'ai préparé mes affaires jusqu'à minuit.
lines.en: The day before leaving, I got my things ready until midnight.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et tu es parti tôt le matin ?
lines.en: And did you leave early in the morning?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, le lendemain j'ai quitté la maison à six heures. D'abord le bus, ensuite le train.
lines.en: Yes, the next day I left the house at six. First the bus, then the train.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ça fait long. Tu es arrivé quand ?
lines.en: That is long. When did you arrive?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Enfin vers midi, et j'ai retrouvé mes copains directement sur la plage.
lines.en: Finally around noon, and I met up with my mates straight on the beach.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et le retour ?
lines.en: And the way back?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Deux jours après, j'ai repris le travail, un peu fatigué mais très content.
lines.en: Two days later I went back to work, a bit tired but very happy.

days.title: Donner son avis simplement
days.objective: Give a simple opinion, agree or disagree, and say something is better or worse.
days.paretoFocus: Avoir raison, avoir tort and être d'accord are the three moves of any ordinary disagreement, and meilleur and pire are the two comparatives learners most often get wrong.
drills: Write four opinions about films or restaurants using je trouve que.
Answer each opinion twice: once agreeing, once disagreeing politely.
Compare two things you know using meilleur and two using pire.

items: Which verb goes with raison and tort, and why is it not être?
What is the comparative adjective of bon, and of mauvais?
How do you disagree with a person without being rude?

activities.title: À mon avis
activities.intro: Avoir raison and avoir tort use avoir, never être. Meilleur is the comparative of the adjective bon, and pire is the comparative of mauvais. Être d'accord takes avec before a person.

items.hr: Je trouve que ce film est meilleur que le premier.
items.en: I think this film is better than the first one.
items.note: meilleur = comparative of bon

items.hr: Tu as raison : le service était vraiment pire qu'avant.
items.en: You are right: the service was really worse than before.
items.note: pire = comparative of mauvais

items.hr: Je ne suis pas d'accord avec toi, mais je comprends ton idée.
items.en: I do not agree with you, but I understand your idea.
items.note: être d'accord avec quelqu'un

items.hr: Elle a tort de partir maintenant, il va pleuvoir dans une heure.
items.en: She is wrong to leave now, it is going to rain within an hour.
items.note: avoir tort de + infinitive

items.hr: À mon avis, c'est la meilleure solution pour tout le monde.
items.en: In my opinion, it is the best solution for everyone.
items.note: la meilleure is the superlative form

activities.title: D'accord ou pas d'accord
activities.intro: Practise expressing agreement, disagreement and comparison.

questions.prompt: Ce restaurant est ___ que l'autre : la cuisine y est excellente.
questions.explanation: Bon has an irregular comparative adjective, meilleur; plus bon does not exist.

questions.prompt: Stress pronouns revisited: Tu es d'accord avec Paul ? Non, je ne suis pas d'accord avec ___ .
questions.explanation: After a preposition French uses a stress pronoun, so il becomes lui.

questions.prompt: Tu ___ raison, j'ai oublié de fermer la porte à clé.
questions.explanation: Raison goes with avoir, so the tu form is as.

questions.prompt: Le deuxième livre est ___ que le premier : je n'ai rien compris.
questions.explanation: The comparative of mauvais in this fixed use is pire.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'In my opinion, she is wrong.'
questions.explanation: Avoir tort keeps avoir, and à mon avis opens the sentence.

activities.title: Après le film
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Leaving the cinema, you and a mate argue over the film.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Franchement, je trouve que ce film est meilleur que le premier.
lines.en: Honestly, I think this film is better than the first one.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je ne suis pas d'accord avec toi, le début est beaucoup trop long.
lines.en: I do not agree with you, the beginning is far too long.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: C'est vrai, la première demi-heure est pire que dans l'autre film.
lines.en: That is true, the first half hour is worse than in the other film.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tu as raison sur ce point, mais la fin sauve tout.
lines.en: You are right on that point, but the ending saves everything.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Donc on est d'accord au moins sur la fin.
lines.en: So we agree at least about the ending.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, et à mon avis c'est la meilleure scène de l'année.
lines.en: Yes, and in my opinion it is the best scene of the year.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Alors j'ai eu tort de me plaindre pendant une heure.
lines.en: Then I was wrong to complain for an hour.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Un peu, mais tout le monde a le droit de changer d'avis.
lines.en: A little, but everyone has the right to change their mind.

days.title: Le monde du travail 2
days.objective: Talk about your workplace: the boss, the team, meetings and hiring.
days.paretoFocus: Le patron, l'équipe and la réunion appear in almost every workplace conversation, and le travail as a noun is one of the commonest words in French.
drills: Describe your workplace or your ideal workplace in four sentences.
Write three sentences with demander: to ask for something, and to ask someone to do something.
Say aloud what your team is working on this month.

items: What is the noun for the person who runs a company, in both genders?
How do you say 'the company is going to hire three people'?
Which preposition follows demander before the person?

activities.title: Au bureau
activities.intro: Le travail is the noun for work, alongside the verb travailler. Demander is followed by à before the person and de before an infinitive. Embaucher is the everyday verb for hiring staff.

items.hr: Notre patron est arrivé à neuf heures pour la réunion de l'équipe.
items.en: Our boss arrived at nine for the team meeting.
items.note: le patron, la patronne

items.hr: L'entreprise va embaucher trois personnes avant l'été.
items.en: The company is going to hire three people before the summer.
items.note: embaucher = to take on staff

items.hr: J'ai demandé une augmentation, mais il faut attendre le mois prochain.
items.en: I asked for a pay rise, but we have to wait until next month.
items.note: demander quelque chose à quelqu'un

items.hr: Je cherche un travail plus près de chez moi, je perds deux heures par jour.
items.en: I am looking for a job closer to home, I lose two hours a day.
items.note: un travail = a job, le travail = work

items.hr: Quelqu'un doit préparer les documents pour la réunion de demain.
items.en: Someone has to prepare the documents for tomorrow's meeting.
items.note: quelqu'un takes a singular verb

activities.title: Équipe, patron, réunion
activities.intro: Practise the vocabulary of the workplace.

questions.prompt: La ___ commence à dix heures dans la grande salle.
questions.explanation: Only a meeting starts at a time in a room; the others are people or an organisation.

questions.prompt: Future forms once more: L'entreprise ___ deux personnes l'année prochaine.
questions.explanation: L'année prochaine points forward, so the futur simple is required.

questions.prompt: Mon ___ m'a demandé de finir ce dossier avant vendredi.
questions.explanation: This is the noun for the person in charge of the company or the shop.

questions.prompt: Toute l'___ travaille sur le même projet depuis janvier.
questions.explanation: The collective noun for a group of colleagues is feminine and takes a singular verb.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I am looking for a job near my home.'
questions.explanation: Chercher takes no preposition in French, and près de is followed by chez moi.

activities.title: Avant la réunion
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague briefs you before the team meeting.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu sais que le patron arrive à neuf heures pour la réunion ?
lines.en: Do you know that the boss is coming at nine for the meeting?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, quelqu'un doit encore préparer les documents.
lines.en: Yes, someone still has to prepare the documents.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Je m'en occupe. L'entreprise va embaucher trois personnes avant l'été.
lines.en: I will take care of it. The company is going to hire three people before the summer.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Enfin ! Toute l'équipe travaille trop depuis janvier.
lines.en: At last! The whole team has been working too much since January.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as demandé ton augmentation au patron ?
lines.en: Did you ask the boss for your pay rise?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je l'ai demandée la semaine dernière, il faut attendre le mois prochain.
lines.en: I asked for it last week, we have to wait until next month.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et sinon, tu cherches toujours un travail plus près de chez toi ?
lines.en: And otherwise, are you still looking for a job closer to home?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Un peu, mais si l'équipe grandit, je reste ici avec plaisir.
lines.en: A little, but if the team grows, I will happily stay here.

days.title: Révision : raconter, décrire, comparer
days.objective: Combine the three core A2 skills in one account: tell what happened, describe the setting, and compare it with something else.
days.paretoFocus: Real speech never uses one tense at a time; this lesson forces the passé composé, the imparfait and the comparative to work together, which is exactly what an examiner listens for.
drills: Tell the story of your last holiday in eight sentences, mixing both past tenses.
Add two comparisons to that story, one with plus and one with meilleur.
Describe one place you have been to twice, and say what changed between the visits.

items: Which tense carries the events and which one carries the background?
How do you say 'twice a year' and 'the day after I arrived'?
Give one comparative of superiority and one of inferiority.

activities.title: Un souvenir de vacances
activities.intro: Events move the story forward in the passé composé, while the imparfait paints the scene around them. Comparatives then let you place that memory against something else.

items.hr: L'été dernier, je suis allé deux fois dans le même endroit au bord de la mer.
items.en: Last summer I went twice to the same spot by the sea.
items.note: deux fois = twice

items.hr: C'était un petit village très calme et les gens se disaient bonjour dans la rue.
items.en: It was a very quiet little village and people said hello to each other in the street.
items.note: imparfait for the setting

items.hr: Le lendemain de mon arrivée, j'ai retrouvé des copains sur la plage.
items.en: The day after I arrived, I met up with some mates on the beach.

items.hr: Cet endroit est plus tranquille que la ville, et la nourriture y est meilleure.
items.en: That place is quieter than the city, and the food there is better.

items.hr: J'ai besoin de vacances comme celles-là au moins une fois par an.
items.en: I need holidays like those at least once a year.
items.note: une fois par an = once a year

activities.title: Tout ensemble
activities.intro: Practise the two past tenses and comparison in the same short account.

questions.prompt: Quand nous sommes arrivés, il ___ et la plage était complètement vide.
questions.explanation: The rain is the background around the arrival, so it takes the imparfait.

questions.prompt: Comparatives one more time: Ce café est ___ cher que l'autre, et pourtant il est meilleur.
questions.explanation: Moins plus the adjective plus que forms a comparative of inferiority.

questions.prompt: Je vais chez mes grands-parents deux ___ par an, en été et à Noël.
questions.explanation: Frequency is counted with this noun: une fois, deux fois par an.

questions.prompt: Nous avons visité un ___ magnifique au bord du lac, loin des touristes.
questions.explanation: This is the everyday noun for a spot or location.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'The next day we went back to the village.'
questions.explanation: Retourner takes être and its participle agrees with the plural subject.

activities.title: Et tes vacances ?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend asks about last summer and you narrate, describe and compare.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu es parti où l'été dernier ?
lines.en: Where did you go last summer?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dans un petit village au bord de la mer, deux fois dans la même année.
lines.en: To a small village by the sea, twice in the same year.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: C'était comment ?
lines.en: What was it like?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'était très calme, les gens se disaient bonjour dans la rue et personne n'était pressé.
lines.en: It was very quiet, people said hello in the street and nobody was in a hurry.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et tu connaissais quelqu'un là-bas ?
lines.en: And did you know anyone there?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Le lendemain de mon arrivée, j'ai retrouvé des copains sur la plage, par hasard.
lines.en: The day after I arrived, I met up with some mates on the beach, by chance.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: C'est mieux que la ville, alors ?
lines.en: So it is better than the city?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Cet endroit est plus tranquille et la nourriture y est meilleure, j'y retourne l'année prochaine.
lines.en: That place is quieter and the food there is better, I am going back next year.
